Ciafardini | birth_date = {{birth date and age|1956|6|11|df=y}} | birth_place = [[Buenos Aires]], Argentina | death_date = | death_place = | party = [[La Libertad Avanza|LLA]] (since 2025) | other_party = [[Justicialist Party|PJ]] (1972–1996)
[[New Leadership]] (1996–1997)
[[:es:Unión por la Libertad|Union for Freedom]] (1997–2018)
[[Republican Proposal|PRO]] (2018–2025) | alma_mater = {{plainlist| *[[University of Palermo (Buenos Aires)|University of Palermo]] ([[Bachelor of Arts|BA]]) *[[Latin American Faculty of Social Sciences|FLACSO]] ([[Master of Arts|MA]]) *[[National University of General San Martín]] ([[Doctor of Philosophy|PhD]]){{cite web |title=Nueva doctora en Ciencia Política de la UNSAM |url=https://noticias.unsam.edu.ar/2013/09/04/nueva-doctora-en-ciencia-politica-de-la-unsam/ |publisher=UNSAM |access-date=26 June 2025 |date=4 September 2013 |trans-title=New PhD in Political Science from UNSAM}} }} | spouse = {{plainlist| * {{Marriage|Marcelo Langieri|1975|1982|end=div.}} * {{marriage|Guillermo Yanco|1997}} }} | children = 1 | signature = Patricia-Bullrich Signature.svg | relations = | relatives = [[Esteban Bullrich]] (second cousin){{cite web | url=https://caras.perfil.com/noticias/celebridades/elecciones2023-vinculo-patricia-bullrich-con-esteban-bullrich.phtml | title=Familia política y aristocrática, el lazo que une a Patricia Bullrich y Esteban Bullrich | date=30 August 2023 }} }} '''Patricia Bullrich''' ({{IPA|es-419|paˈtɾisja ˈβulritʃ|lang|Patricia Bullrich - nombre (ES-ar).ogg}}; born 11 June 1956) is an Argentine politician. She currently serves as a [[Argentine Senate|National Senator]] for the [[Buenos Aires|Autonomous City of Buenos Aires]] since December 2025, representing [[La Libertad Avanza]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.a24.com/politica/patricia-bullrich-gano-caba-y-asume-el-10-diciembre-como-senadora-n1491340|title=Patricia Bullrich ganó en CABA y asume el 10 de diciembre como senadora|website=A24.com|date=26 October 2025 |language=es}} She previously served as the [[Ministry of National Security (Argentina)|Minister of National Security]] from 2023 to 2025 under president [[Javier Milei]] and from 2015 to 2019 under president [[Mauricio Macri]]. She was the chairwoman of the [[Republican Proposal]] until 2025.{{Cite web|url=https://www.cba24n.com.ar/politica/patricia-bullrich-es-formalmente-la-presidenta-del-pro_a5e3bfe3d9fb5571836d61bda|title=Patricia Bullrich es formalmente la presidenta del Pro|website=Cba24n|date=6 February 2020 |language=es}} She was born in Buenos Aires, part of the [[Pueyrredón family]]. Bullrich graduated from the [[University of Palermo (Buenos Aires)|University of Palermo]], and as a young woman she was involved with the [[Peronist Youth]]. She married Marcelo Langieri, secretary of Rodolfo Galimberti; Galimberti was a leader of the [[Montoneros]] guerrilla and her brother-in-law. They went into exile in 1977, during the [[Dirty War]], and she returned in 1982, after the [[Falklands War]]. She was elected deputy for the [[Justicialist Party]] in 1993. She left the party and started her own, but could not secure a re-election. She joined the cabinet of president [[Fernando De la Rúa]] in 2001. Following the election of [[Mauricio Macri]] to the presidency in the [[2015 Argentine general election]], she became the Minister of Security. She placed regulations against roadblocks from [[piquetero]]s, and opposed the secessionist organization [[Resistencia Ancestral Mapuche]]. Leading the "hawk" sector of the [[Juntos por el Cambio]] coalition, she was appointed its president in 2020. She ran for the 2023 Argentine [[2023 Argentine primary elections|primary]] and [[2023 Argentine general election|general]] elections. She won the primaries against [[Horacio Rodríguez Larreta]], but ended third in the general elections behind [[Sergio Massa]] and [[Javier Milei]]. She supported Milei on the ballotage with Massa. After Milei was elected President, he chose Bullrich to serve again as Minister of Security. On November 30, 2025, Bullrich resigned from her post as Minister of National Security after she was sworn in as a Senator in the [[Argentine Senate]].{{Cite web|title=Patricia Bullrich presentó su renuncia como ministra de Seguridad Nacional|url=https://www.filo.news/noticia/2025/11/30/patricia-bullrich-presento-su-renuncia-como-ministra-de-seguridad-nacional|website=Filo News|date=2025-11-30|access-date=2025-12-01|language=es}} ==Early life== Bullrich was born on 11 June 1956 in [[Buenos Aires]], daughter of Alejandro Bullrich, a cardiologist, and Julieta Luro Pueyrredon. She belongs to two wealthy families on each of her parents' sides. On her mother's side, she belongs to the [[Pueyrredón family]], a traditional lineage of Spanish, French, and Irish descent whose members featured prominently in the early years of Argentine Independence (such as [[Juan Martín de Pueyrredón]] and [[Honorio Pueyrredon]]).{{cite news|url=https://www.lanacion.com.ar/cultura/los-pueyrredon-preparan-un-encuentro-de-18000-parientes-nid35260/|work=[[La Nación]]|title=Los Pueyrredon preparan un encuentro de 18.000 parientes|last=Rouillon|first=Jorge|date=2 October 2000|access-date=7 May 2023|language=es}} On her father's side, she descends from [[Adolfo Bullrich]], a businessman and politician of German ancestry, who served as [[List of mayors and chiefs of government of Buenos Aires City|Mayor of Buenos Aires]] from 1898 to 1902.{{cite web |url= https://caras.perfil.com/noticias/celebridades/elecciones2023-vinculo-patricia-bullrich-con-esteban-bullrich.phtml|title= Cuál es el vínculo que une a Patricia Bullrich con Esteban Bullrich|trans-title= Which is the link between Patricia Bullrich and Esteban Bullrich|language= Spanish|first=Oriana|last=Luna|date= 30 August 2023|publisher= Caras|accessdate=24 May 2024}} Although it was a wealthy family, Alejandro and Julieta divorced, and Patricia adjusted to a simpler lifestyle when her mother got a job as publicist. She became politically engaged from an early age, abandoning a potential career in [[field hockey]] to dedicate herself fully to political activism.{{cite news|url=https://www.clarin.com/politica/rugidos-patricia-bullrich_0_rJhnxgAte.html|work=[[Clarín (Argentine newspaper)|Clarín]]|title=Los rugidos de Patricia Bullrich|date=24 July 2003|access-date=7 May 2023|language=es}} She also worked at the ''Cheburger'' fast food joint and became a member of the food workers' union, encouraging coworkers to unionise as well.{{cite book|title=Lazos de familia. El horizonte de la mirada política. Memorias de la acción. Conversaciones con Albino Gómez.|publisher=Asociación Civil Centro de Estudios Sociales Ahora Argentina|isbn=987-20654-0-3|location=Buenos Aires|last=Bullrich|first=Patricia|page=23|language=es}} She is the cousin of the singer-songwriter [[Fabiana Cantilo]], and introduced her to [[Argentine rock]]. In the TV program "''Almorzando con Mirtha Legrand''" Cantilo explained that their mothers were close as siblings, and that Bullrich invited her to a concert of the band [[Pescado Rabioso]], led by [[Luis Alberto Spinetta]]. This was the first concert that Fabiana Cantilo had attended, years before becoming a rock singer herself.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2023/08/04/patricia-bullrcih-vida-familiar-carrera-orix-arg/|title= Así es la vida familiar y carrera de Patricia Bullrich: antepasados, militancia y más|trans-title= This is the family life and career of Patricia Bullrich: ancestors, activism and more|language= Spanish|first=Gilles|last=Salomone|date= 4 August 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=24 May 2024}} Patricia skipped school classes one day to attend the [[music competition]] TV program "Si lo sabe cante", where she sang the song "El extraño de pelo largo" of La Joven Guardia. She was defeated in the competition. ===Peronist Youth years=== Patricia's grandmother, daughter of Honorio Pueyrredon, took her to meet [[Ricardo Balbín]], leader of the [[Radical Civic Union]] (UCR). The meeting had the opposite effect than expected, as there was a huge [[generation gap]] between them. Rather than make her embrace the ideas of the UCR, supportive of [[liberal democracy]], she rejected Balbín and chose [[Peronism]] instead, which proposed [[far-left politics]] at the time. She joined the Peronist Youth (JP), the youth wing of the Peronist movement, aged 17. On 20 June 1973, she joined the procession to [[Ezeiza Airport|Ezeiza]] to bear witness to [[Juan Perón]]'s return to Argentina following his 18-year exile, but left before the [[Ezeiza Massacre]] took place that same day.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2023/12/01/patricia-bullrich-precandidata-juntos-cambio-argentina-orix/|title= ¿Quién es Patricia Bullrich, la elegida por Milei para ser ministra de Seguridad de Argentina?|trans-title= Who is Patricia Bullrich, chosen by Milei to be minister of security?|language= Spanish|first=Betiana|last=Fernández Martino|date= 1 December 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=24 May 2024}} She was also present at the [[Plaza de Mayo]] on the [[International Workers' Day]] of 1974, when Perón, who had once again become president of Argentina, [[Expulsion of Montoneros from Plaza de Mayo|expelled the Montoneros]] and the [[Tendencia Revolucionaria|left-wing youth groups]] from the celebrations. Although both the Peronist unions and guerrillas such as [[Montoneros]] had worked together against the [[Argentine Revolution]] dictatorship, they had begun a dispute over their political clout on the new democratic government; with the aforementioned expulsion Perón openly sided with the unions. Bullrich herself was a member of the Montoneros under the ''[[nom-de-guerre]]'' of "Cali", and active in the ''Columna Norte'' subgroup commanded by [[:es:Rodolfo Galimberti|Rodolfo Galimberti]].{{cite news|url=https://www.perfil.com/noticias/opinion/patricia-la-montonera.phtml|work=[[Perfil]]|title=Patricia, la montonera|last=Duzdevich|first=Aldo A.|date=13 September 2020|access-date=7 May 2023|language=es}} Galimberti was Bullrich's brother-in-law, as he was married to Bullrich's sister, Julieta.{{cite news|url=http://www.clarin.com/diario/2002/02/13/p-02001.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20041122015016/http://www.clarin.com/diario/2002/02/13/p-02001.htm|title=Murió Rodolfo Galimberti, un símbolo de los violentos años 70|last=Amato|first=Alberto|date=13 February 2002|archive-date=22 November 2004|language=es}} Bullrich has denied being a Montoneros member, and maintains that she was just a member of the JP.{{cite news|url=https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/patricia-bullrich-conto-cual-fue-su-rol-en-montoneros-nid2007336/|work=[[La Nación]]|title=Patricia Bullrich contó cuál fue su relación con Montoneros|date=7 April 2017|access-date=7 May 2023|language=es}}{{cite news|url=https://www.infobae.com/politica/2020/10/04/patricia-bullrich-tengo-una-profunda-autocritica-de-lo-que-fue-la-violencia-como-accion-politica/|work=[[Infobae]]|title=Patricia Bullrich hizo autocrítica por su militancia en la JP y afirmó: "Los Montoneros mataron al Padre Mugica"|date=4 October 2020|access-date=7 May 2023|language=es}} Perón died in 1974 and the [[Dirty War]], an armed conflict between the Montoneros and the military, worsened: the guerrillas resumed the tactics used years before to attempt to remove the current authorities from power. In 1975, Bullrich was arrested for spray-painting political messages on the entrance of the [[Faculty of Philosophy and Letters, University of Buenos Aires|University of Buenos Aires Faculty of Philosophy and Letters]], and spent six months in prison. Upon being released, she dropped out of her sociology degree at the University of Buenos Aires and enrolled at the [[Universidad de Belgrano]] to study law. She also rejoined the ''Columna Norte''. By the end of 1975, she started dating Marcelo Langieri, secretary of Galimberti.{{cite web |url= https://noticias.perfil.com/noticias/general/2017-09-18-los-amores-de-patricia-bullrich.phtml|title= Los amores de Patricia Bullrich|trans-title= The loves of Patricia Bullrich|language= Spanish|first=Carlos|last=Claá|date= 18 September 2017|publisher= Noticias|accessdate=5 November 2024}} The [[1976 Argentine coup d'état|1976 coup d'état]] established a military dictatorship, the [[National Reorganization Process]], that continued the armed conflict against the Montoneros. Montoneros organized an attack on 4 September 1976, that intended to kill a manager of the Sudamtex textile firm. On her way to participate, Bullrich felt that she was being followed and fled. The military had abducted one of the masterminds of the plan and learned about the attack, which allowed them to surprise the Montoneros and kill the whole unit. In January 1977, Galimberti and Bullrich attached an [[improvised explosive device]] to the car of Pepe Noguer, mayor of [[San Isidro, Buenos Aires|San Isidro]]. The device exploded, injuring the daughter and the daughter-in-law of the mayor, Ana María Noguer and Hortensia M. de Noguer. Patricia Bullrich went into exile in 1977 with her partner Marcelo Langieri, first settling in Brazil, and later in Mexico, Spain and France.{{cite news|url=https://www.clarin.com/politica/pebeta-apellidos_0_S1pgcuxgAFg.html|work=[[Clarín (Argentine newspaper)|Clarín]]|title=Pebeta de tres apellidos|date=20 August 2000|access-date=7 May 2023|language=es}} She was still a member of Montoneros living abroad, until Galimberti cut ties with the organization in 1979. She returned briefly to Argentina in that year, to give birth to her son Francisco. ==Political career== Bullrich returned to the country after the 1982 [[Falklands War]]. She had conflicts with [[Dante Gullo]] over the reorganization of the JP, thinking that it should have its own political leaders rather than follow the older ones.{{cite web |url= https://www.perfil.com/noticias/elobservador/militancia-juvenil-de-la-coordinadora-a-la-campora-20131013-0096.phtml|title= Militancia juvenil: de la Coordinadora a La Cámpora|trans-title= Youth activism: from La Coordinadora to La Cámpora|language= Spanish|author= |date= 13 October 2013|publisher= Perfil|accessdate=5 November 2024}} Peronism lost the [[1983 Argentine general election]], so she joined the internal faction of [[Antonio Cafiero]] that sought to renew the party. Peronism returned to power in 1989 when [[Carlos Menem]] won the [[1989 Argentine general election]]. She was elected deputy for the [[Justicialist Party]] (PJ) in 1993, alongside [[Erman González]]. She proposed over two hundred bills, including the Art University law, the Cinema law, and the Leasing Contract law. In 1995, she was named the Legislator of the Year.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/patricia-bullrich-ministra-de-seguridad-nid1848784/|title= Patricia Bullrich, ministra de Seguridad|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich, minister of security|language= Spanish|author= |date= 25 November 2015|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=29 May 2024}} Patricia Bullrich left the PJ in 1996 and started [[New Leadership]] alongside [[Gustavo Béliz]]. She had disagreements with him because she thought that he managed the party unilaterally, without making her part of the decisions.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/bullrich-me-dolio-que-beliz-no-me-consultara-nid174075/|title= Bullrich: "Me dolió que Beliz no me consultara"|trans-title= Bullrich: "It hurts that Beliz did not ask me"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 19 November 1996|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=31 May 2024}} She left the party as a result and, after briefly considering joining the [[Front for a Country in Solidarity|Frepaso]], she started another party. Initially named Cambio 97, it was soon renamed "Unión por Todos" (UPT) as the Cambio 97 name had already been registered. The new party did not get enough votes to allow her to be reelected as deputy in the [[1997 Argentine legislative election]]. She then worked for the [[Buenos Aires Province]] on security matters, assisting the municipality of [[Hurlingham Partido|Hurlingham]]. She resigned in 1998, because of conflicts with [[León Arslanián]], minister of justice of the province.{{cite web |url= https://www.clarin.com/politica/bullrich-alianza_0_HJfbURxAYe.html|title= Bullrich se fue con la Alianza|trans-title= Bullrich left with the Alliance|language= Spanish|author= |date= 10 February 1999|publisher= Clarín|accessdate=31 May 2024}} [[File:Patricia Bullrich.jpg|thumb|left|Patricia Bullrich at the time she served as deputy.]] In 1999, the UPT became part of the [[Alliance for Work, Justice and Education]], which took [[Fernando de la Rúa]] to the Presidency. Bullrich was appointed to office in the Department of Criminal Policy and Penitentiary Matters. In 2001, she was made a cabinet minister, as [[Secretariat of Labour, Employment and Social Security (Argentina)|Secretary of Labour, Employment and Human Resources]] and, later that year, as Secretary of Social Security. In 2001 she announced a cut of 13% on retirement wages above $574, 16% of all retirees, which would reduce the [[Deficit spending|deficit]] by 68.6 millions of pesos.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/el-ajuste-afecta-a-533401-jubilados-nid322304/|title= El ajuste afecta a 533.401 jubilados|trans-title= The cut involves 533,401 retirees|language= Spanish|author= |date= 24 July 2001|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=3 June 2024}} De la Rúa resigned, and eventually the governor of Buenos Aires [[Eduardo Duhalde]] was appointed president by the Congress. Bullrich relaunched the UPT in 2002, aiming for the [[2003 Argentine general election|2003 presidential elections]]. The party platform announced reforms on labor and education.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/bullrich-llamo-a-crecer-con-ideas-nid428615/|title= Bullrich llamó a "crecer con ideas"|trans-title= Bullrich called to "grow with ideas"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 5 September 2002|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=4 June 2024}} The UPT did not take part in the elections, supporting instead [[Ricardo López Murphy]] of the [[Recreate for Growth]] party,{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/recrear-y-bullrich-con-mas-acuerdos-nid486560/|title= Recrear y Bullrich, con más acuerdos|trans-title= Recreate and Bullrich, with more deals|language= Spanish|author= |date= 6 April 2003|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=4 June 2024}} who ended in the third place. Both parties united in the alliance "Unión para Recrear Buenos Aires", proposing Bullrich for mayor of Buenos Aires. She ended in the fourth place, with 9.76% of the vote.{{cite web |url= https://www.tsjbaires.gov.ar/index.php?option=com_flexicontent&view=items&id=3278&catid=65&Itemid=96|title= Elecciones 2003 - RESULTADOS FINALES DE LOS COMICIOS DEL 24 DE AGOSTO DE 2003|trans-title= Elections 2003 - Final results of the elections of 24 August 2003|language= Spanish|author= |date= |publisher= Tribunal Superior de Justicia|accessdate=4 June 2024}} The UPT joined forces with the [[Support for an Egalitarian Republic]] (ARI) and created the [[Civic Coalition (Argentina)|Civic Coalition]] to run for the [[2007 Argentine general election]], with [[Elisa Carrió]] as a candidate for president. Bullrich ran instead for deputy for Buenos Aires.{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/2007/04/12/310952-carrio-prensento-su-coalicion-civica-cara-elecciones/|title= Carrió prensentó su Coalición Cívica de cara a elecciones|trans-title= Carrió introduced her Civic Coalition ahead of the elections|language= Spanish|author= |date= 23 October 2007|publisher= Infobae |accessdate=5 June 2024}} Her centrist politics and polemical history as a government minister, however, contributed to the disenchantment of a group of left-wing members of ARI who left the Civic Coalition.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/ari-respaldo-a-carrio-y-desautorizo-a-los-ocho-rebeldes-nid965428/|title= ARI respaldó a Carrió y desautorizó a los ocho rebeldes|trans-title= ARI supported Carrió and rejected the eight rebels|language= Spanish|first=Jaime|last=Rosemberg|date= 25 November 2007|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=4 June 2024}} ===Minister of Security (2015–2019)=== [[File:Informe ram 2.jpg|thumb|Patricia Bullrich presents a report about the ''[[Resistencia Ancestral Mapuche]]'' organization.]] [[Mauricio Macri]] was elected president in 2015 and Patricia Bullrich was appointed Minister of Security.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/entre-bromas-y-guinos-complices-juraron-27-funcionarios-nacionales-nid1853267/|title= Entre bromas y guiños cómplices, juraron 27 funcionarios nacionales|trans-title= Amid jokes and winks, 27 national officers were sworn in|language= Spanish|author= Mariana Veron|date= 11 December 2015|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=6 June 2024}} Within the first days, three criminals convicted for [[triple crime|torturing and killing three pharmaceutical businessmen]] escaped from a high-security prison, leading to a nation-wide operation to recapture them. Bullrich announced on 9 January that the three criminals had been captured, but, in reality, only one had been captured. She tendered her resignation to Macri because of the mistake, but Macri rejected it. The other two criminals were captured a few days later.{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/2016/03/11/1796591-patricia-bullrich-esta-claro-que-los-condenados-del-triple-crimen-escaparon-todas-las-facilidades/|title= Patricia Bullrich: "Está claro que los condenados del triple crimen escaparon con todas las facilidades"|trans-title= Bullrich: "it's clear that the convicts of the triple crime escaped with all the inside help"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 1 December 2017|publisher= Infobae|accessdate=10 June 2024}} Bullrich proposed a protocol to remove roadblocks caused by [[piquetero]]s. Such blocks should be announced in advance, restrict only part of the roads, and last for a short time. They should cease if ordered so by the police, and those who commit violent actions shall be detained. This protocol was approved by a meeting of the ministers of security of all provinces of Argentina.{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/2016/02/17/1790860-se-aprobo-el-protocolo-antipiquetes-habra-una-negociacion-antes-desalojar-un-corte-calle/|title= Se aprobó el protocolo antipiquetes: habrá una negociación antes de desalojar un corte de calle|trans-title= The anti-piquetero protocol has been approved: there will be a negotiation before breaking a roadblock|language= Spanish|author= |date= 1 December 2017|publisher= Infobae|accessdate=13 June 2024}} [[Horacio Rodríguez Larreta]], chief of government of the Buenos Aires city, and [[María Eugenia Vidal]], governor of the Buenos Aires province, refused to endorse it, as they thought that doing so could prove controversial. Instead, they preferred to negotiate and stay on good terms with organizations of piqueteros such as the [[Evita Movement]].{{cite web |url= https://www.clarin.com/politica/Ciudad-Provincia-juntas-protocolo-antipiquetes_0_NyOBxjSEZ.html|title= La Ciudad y la Provincia, juntas contra el protocolo antipiquetes|trans-title= The city and the province, together against the anti-piquetero protocol|language= Spanish|author= Ignacio Miri|date= 12 June 2016|publisher= Clarín|accessdate=13 June 2024}} Larreta also explained that the city does not need to endorse the protocols because it already has regulations for roadblocks.{{cite web |url= https://www.clarin.com/politica/larreta-tono-protocolo-antipiquete_0_Sktab6W8g.html|title= Larreta le bajó el tono al protocolo antipiquete|trans-title= Larreta lowered the tone to the dispute over the anti-piquetero protocol|language= Spanish|author= |date= 9 January 2017|publisher= Clarín|accessdate=13 June 2024}} [[Resistencia Ancestral Mapuche]] (RAM) and the [[Coordinadora Arauco-Malleco]] (CAM), two [[Mapuche]] organizations that normally operate in [[Patagonia]], expanded their attacks to the city of Buenos Aires. Their goal is to create a Mapuche nation, seceding from Argentina and Chile. Bullrich said that "We won't allow an autonomous mapuche republic inside Argentina; that's the logic of their request, the denial of the Argentine state, the anarchist logic. Our resolution is total and absolute in not allowing a group that uses violence as a way of action in Argentina."{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/politica/2017/08/08/patricia-bullrich-de-ninguna-manera-vamos-a-permitir-una-republica-mapuche-en-medio-de-la-argentina/|title= Patricia Bullrich: "De ninguna manera vamos a permitir una república mapuche en medio de la Argentina"|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich: "no way we would allow a mapuche republic in the middle of Argentina"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 8 August 2017|publisher= Infobae|accessdate=13 June 2024}} Facundo Jones Huala, leader of RAM, claimed to be a political prisoner, and that the RAM only acts in self-defense on lands that used to belong to them.{{cite web |url= https://www.clarin.com/politica/patricia-bullrich-ram-financiada-organizacion-inglesa_0_H1RLCRvwb.html|title= Patricia Bullrich: "La RAM está financiada por una organización inglesa"|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich: "The RAM is financed by an English organization"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 8 August 2017|publisher= Clarín|accessdate=13 June 2024}} Bullrich organized a joint command with the ministers of security of the [[Neuquén Province|Neuquén]], [[Río Negro Province|Río Negro]] and [[Chubut Province|Chubut]] provinces against the RAM, and delivered a report of the actions taken against the organization, including 96 lawsuits, 2 of them for murder.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/conforman-un-comando-conjunto-para-combatir-a-ram-en-la-patagonia-nid2095980/|title= Conforman un "comando conjunto" para combatir a RAM en la Patagonia|trans-title= They create a "joint command" to fight the RAM at the Patagonia|language= Spanish|author= Jaime Rosemberg|date= 28 December 2017|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=13 June 2024}} The [[Argentine National Gendarmerie]] forced a group of Mapuche demonstrators to cease a roadblock next to the [[Chubut River]], and the demonstrator [[Santiago Maldonado]] went missing after that. The case became a national scandal, with the Gendarmerie (by extension their political authorities, Bullrich and Macri) being accused of an [[enforced disappearance]]. Bullrich gave her full support to the Gendarmerie. Maldonado was found dead some months later, drowned in the Chubut river. Bullrich said "The whole world saw what happened with Maldonado. He stayed in the same place where he drowned, without being seen or touched by anyone. Neither the Gendarmerie nor our government would have made a person disappear". A tourist was stabbed ten times by thieves, who then ran away. The policeman [[Luis Chocobar]] shot one of the thieves, claiming that the thief made a movement suggesting he was about to fire a gun. However, a video released by [[Telefe Noticias]] showed that the criminal had his back to him. Bullrich, however, pointed out that the video was edited and did not show the exact moment of the gunshot.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2018/02/08/macri-denuncia-policia-chocobar/|title= Opositor argentino denuncia a Macri por defender a policía que mató a delincuente|trans-title= Argentine opposition denounces Macri for defending the cop that shot down a thief|language= Spanish|author= |date= 8 February 2018|publisher= CNN|accessdate=7 June 2024}} Bullrich and Macri supported the actions of Chocobar, on the grounds that it would be a [[right of self-defense]], while human rights organizations considered it a case of [[police brutality]]. Chocobar was sentenced for homicide four years later. In November 2018, Bullrich said that "those who wanted to be armed should be allowed to do so", prompting strong criticism and opposition.{{cite news |last1=Romero |first1=Nicolás |title=“EL QUE QUIERA ESTAR ARMADO, QUE ANDE ARMADO” |url=https://www.pagina12.com.ar/152921-el-que-quiera-estar-armado-que-ande-armado/ |access-date=22 January 2026 |work=[[Página 12]] |date=3 November 2018 |language=es}}{{cite news |title=Polémicos dichos de Bullrich: "El que quiere estar armado que ande armado" |url=https://www.perfil.com/noticias/politica/polemicos-dichos-de-bullrich-el-que-quiere-estar-armado-que-ande-armado.phtml |access-date=22 January 2026 |work=[[Perfil]] |date=2 November 2018 |language=es}} Adrián Marcenac, father of Alfredo Marcenac, a student from [[Necochea]] who was murdered by serial shooter [[Martín Ríos (murderer)|Martín Ríos]] in July 2006 in [[Belgrano, Buenos Aires]], labelled Bullrich's comments as "enormously grave" for Argentine society.{{cite news |title=Falleció Adrián Marcenac, incansable defensor del desarme civil en Argentina |url=https://nden.com.ar/nota/29190/fallecio-adrian-marcenac-incansable-defensor-del-desarme-civil-en-argentina |access-date=22 January 2026 |work=Noticias de Necochea |date=23 December 2024 |language=es}} === Presidential candidate === [[File:Abrazo Milei y Bullrich, pato y león.jpg|thumb|[[AI image]] posted by [[Javier Milei]] to announce his alliance with Bullrich. Milei associates himself with imagery of lions, and "pato" ("duck") is a common nickname in Spanish for people named "Patricia".]] The [[2021 Argentine legislative election]] resulted in a victory for [[Juntos por el Cambio]] (JXC), with the libertarian party [[La Libertad Avanza]] (LLA) led by [[Javier Milei]], emerging as a significant [[Third party (politics)|third party]].{{cite web |author= |date=18 April 2023 |title=Milei, dispuesto a ir a una primaria con Bullrich en las próximas elecciones |trans-title=Milei, ready to go to primary elections with Bullrich in the next elections |url=https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/video/milei-bullrich-juntos-cambio-internas-cnn-primera/ |accessdate=10 June 2024 |publisher=CNN |language=Spanish}} Bullrich, who was appointed president of the PRO in 2020,{{cite web |last1=Mercado |first1=Silvia |title=Con Patricia Bullrich como presidente, el PRO renovó su conducción y premió a los distritos que le ganaron al peronismo |url=https://www.infobae.com/politica/2020/02/05/con-patricia-bullrich-como-presidente-el-pro-renovo-su-conduccion-y-premio-a-los-distritos-que-le-ganaron-al-peronismo/ |publisher=Infobae |access-date=6 May 2025 |language=Spanish |date=5 February 2020}} maintained a positive relationship with Milei, and at one point considered forming an alliance for the [[2023 Argentine general election]]. However, Milei ultimately decided to run independently with his own party.{{cite web |author= |date=13 April 2023 |title=Patricia Bullrich, sobre las PASO: "Creo que Milei ha tomado su decisión, que es su fuerza política, y espero que nos apoye" |trans-title=Patricia Bullrich, about the PASO: "I think that Milei has taken a decision, which is his political force, and I hope he sides with us" |url=https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/video/patricia-bullrich-paso-milei-politica-cnn-primera/ |accessdate=10 June 2024 |publisher=CNN |language=Spanish}} There was an expectation that Mauricio Macri would run for president once again, as even in defeat he had gotten 41% of the vote in 2019 and his image had improved since then. However, he declined to do so, so the party had primary elections between Bullrich and Horacio Rodríguez Larreta. They represented the "hawks" and "doves" of the party. Macri did not openly side with any of them.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/mauricio-macri-no-sera-candidato-nid26032023/|title= Mauricio Macri no será candidato a presidente en las elecciones de 2023|trans-title= Mauricio Macri will not be candidate for president in the 2023 elections|language= Spanish|author= Joaquín Morales Solá|date= 26 March 2023|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=13 June 2024}} Bullrich chose the former legislator [[Luis Petri]], from the UCR, as the candidate for vice-president. Petri had lost the primary elections for governor of Mendoza to [[Alfredo Cornejo (politician)|Alfredo Cornejo]] a short time before. She chose him because he was young, sided with the "hawks", and was not from Buenos Aires; and also because he worked with her during her time as minister of security. As Petri was from the UCR, Bullrich would secure the support of the radicals that were not pleased with the leadership of [[Gerardo Morales (politician)|Gerardo Morales]] (who supported Larreta), and with the whole party if Larreta was defeated in the primaries. Other politicians briefly considered to run for vice-president under her were [[Carlos Melconian]], [[Maximiliano Abad]], [[Luis Naidenoff]], and [[Ricardo López Murphy]].{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/elecciones-2023-patricia-bullrich-eligio-al-radical-luis-petri-como-companero-de-formula-nid21062023/|title= Elecciones 2023: Patricia Bullrich eligió al radical Luis Petri como compañero de fórmula|trans-title= Elections 2023: Patricia Bullrich selected the radical Luis Petri as her running mate|language= Spanish|author= Matías Moreno|date= 21 June 2023|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=13 June 2024}} Carlos Melconian was proposed instead as Bullrich's potential minister of economy. In contrast with Milei, who proposed a full [[dollarization]] of the economy, Melconian proposed instead a bimonetary economy, where both the [[Argentine peso]] and the [[United States dollar]] would be legal tender. Before implementing it, the fiscal deficit would be eliminated, the by-laws of the Central Bank would be amended to prohibit [[Debt monetization|money creation]], and the restrictions on the use of US dollars would be removed.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2023/08/31/patricia-bullrich-designa-carlos-melconian-ministro-de-economia-orix/|title= La candidata opositora Patricia Bullrich designa a Carlos Melconian como su ministro de Economía|trans-title= The candidate from the opposition Patricia Bullrich appoints Carlos Melconian as her minister of economy|language= Spanish|author= Ignacio Grimaldi|date= 31 August 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=14 June 2024}} She also proposed the creation of a maximum security prison named after former president Cristina Kirchner.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/video/patricia-bullrich-penal-carcel-cristina-fernandez-kirchner-conclusiones-tv-del-rincon-cnn-cnne/|title= Patricia Bullrich afirma que, de ser presidenta, inaugurará la cárcel "Cristina Fernández de Kirchner"|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich says that, if she becomes president, she will open the "Cristina Fernández de Kirchner" prison|language= Spanish|author= |date= 27 September 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=14 June 2024}} The primary elections ended with a decisive victory of Bullrich over Larreta.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/las-claves-del-triunfo-de-patricia-bullrich-sobre-horacio-rodriguez-larreta-en-la-interna-de-juntos-nid14082023/|title= Las claves del triunfo de Patricia Bullrich sobre Horacio Rodríguez Larreta en la interna de Juntos por el Cambio|trans-title= The keys of the victory of Patricia Bullrich over Horacio Rodríguez Larreta in the primaries of Juntos por el Cambio|language= Spanish|author= Matias Moreno|date= 14 August 2023|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=10 June 2024}} Once the primaries were concluded, the candidates took part in the [[Presidential debates in Argentina|presidential debates]], explaining their positions and proposals over several topics.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2023/10/01/ultima-hora-primer-debate-presidencial-argentina-orix/|title= Resumen del primer debate presidencial en Argentina desde Santiago del Estero|trans-title= Summary of the first presidential debate in Argentina from Santiago del Estero|language= Spanish|author= |date= 2 October 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=14 June 2024}} Bullrich promised to end inflation, and to end the roadblocks caused by piqueteros.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2023/10/02/elecciones-argentina-2023-momentos-frases-clave-debate-orix/|title=Los momentos y frases clave del primer debate presidencial en Argentina |trans-title= The key moments and phrases of the first presidential debate in Argentina|language= Spanish|author= |date= 2 October 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=14 June 2024}} Although the primary elections ended with the three main candidates, Bullrich, Milei and Peronist [[Sergio Massa]] in a close tie, Massa got ahead in the general election and won with 36% of the vote, followed by Milei with 30%. As a result, both candidates went to a third [[ballotage]] election. Bullrich did not take part in it, as JXC ended in third place, but both candidates sought to get the support of her voters. Massa made a speech about institutional stability and public security, usual topics of the UCR and PRO respectively, and Milei focused on the need to join forces against Kirchnerism.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2023/10/23/massa-milei-argentina-presidente-balotaje-orix/|title= Massa sorprende como el más votado en primera vuelta y muestra que el peronismo sigue en carrera en Argentina|trans-title= Massa surprises as the most voted in the first round and shows that Peronism is still in the game|language= Spanish|author= Germán Padinger|date= 23 October 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=14 June 2024}} On 25 October 2023, Bullrich officially endorsed Javier Milei for the runoff election.{{cite news |last1=Politi |first1=Daniel |title=Argentina's third-place presidential candidate Bullrich endorses right-wing populist Milei in runoff |url=https://abcnews.go.com/International/wireStory/argentinas-place-presidential-candidate-bullrich-endorses-wing-populist-104285699 |access-date=25 October 2023 |agency=ABC News |date=25 October 2023}} She did not ask the other parties within JXC, the UCR and the CC, for their opinion, as they have conflicting views over Milei. Both of them met the Wednesday after the elections to negotiate this support, alongside Macri. Milei announced the alliance on his [[X (Twitter)|X]] account, with an [[AI image]] of an embrace between a lion and a duck. Milei associates himself with imagery of lions, and "pato" ("duck") is a common nickname in Spanish for people named "Patricia".{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/2023/10/25/patricia-bullrich-anuncia-que-apoyara-a-javier-milei-para-el-balotaje-presidencial-en-argentina-orix-arg/|title= Patricia Bullrich anuncia que apoyará a Javier Milei para el balotaje presidencial en Argentina|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich announced that she will support Javier Milei in the presidential ballotage|language= Spanish|author= |date= 25 October 2023|publisher= CNN|accessdate=14 June 2024}} ===Minister of Security (2023–2025)=== [[File:Bullrich Plan Bandera 1.jpg|thumb|Patricia Bullrich announced a decrease in homicides in [[Rosario]].]] In December 2023, Patricia Bullrich returned to government as security minister in president [[Presidency of Javier Milei|Javier Milei's Cabinet.]]{{Cite web |title=Bullrich returns to government as security minister in Milei's Cabinet {{!}} Buenos Aires Times |url=https://www.batimes.com.ar/news/argentina/bullrich-returns-to-government-as-security-minister-in-mileis-cabinet.phtml |access-date=2023-12-29 |website=www.batimes.com.ar}} Bullrich immediately issued a protocol to deal with demonstrations and roadblocks made by [[piquetero]]s. The [[Workers' Party (Argentina)|Workers' Party]] made an ''[[habeas corpus]]'' request to prevent the protocol from coming into force, which judge Gustavo Pierretti rejected. Several groups of Piqueteros called for a massive demonstration on 20 December, a week after the [[inauguration of Javier Milei]]. The government announced that demonstrators who committed crimes during demonstrations, such as roadblocks or vandalism, would lose their welfare payments, and provided a phone number to denounce piquetero leaders who would be coercing people to take part in such demonstrations. The demonstration had very little attendance, and the phone calls were used to start 660 criminal complaints.{{cite web |url= https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/patricia-bullrich-cree-que-los-controles-le-quitan-fuerza-a-la-marcha-piquetera-y-pone-la-mira-en-el-nid20122023/|title= Patricia Bullrich celebró el resultado del fuerte operativo para evitar cortes en la marcha piquetera y recibió el apoyo de Milei|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich celebrated the result of the strong operation to prevent blockades in the piquetero march and received the support of Milei|language= Spanish|author= Matías Moreno|date= 20 December 2023|publisher= La Nación|accessdate=12 November 2024}} The policies were kept for all later protests. The welfare aid that was outsourced to piquetero organizations returned to the direct state control, and social leaders that worked at the [[Ministry of Social Development (Argentina)|ministry of Social Development]] despite the conflict of interest were fired. Several audits also revealed that several social organizations that used to control the outsourced welfare aid only used a fraction of it for such a purpose. All this decreased the power of piqueteros, and protests and demonstrations rapidly decreased in size.{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/politica/2024/08/27/se-diluye-el-poder-de-los-piqueteros-sin-el-manejo-de-los-planes-y-arrinconados-por-denuncias-ya-van-4-meses-sin-cortes-de-calles/|title= Se diluye el poder de los piqueteros sin el manejo de los planes y arrinconados por denuncias: ya van 4 meses sin cortes de calles|trans-title= The power of the picketers is diluted without the management of the plans and cornered by complaints: it has already been 4 months without street cuts|language= Spanish|author= Andrés Klipphan|date= 27 August 2024|publisher= Infobae|accessdate=12 November 2024}} The city of [[Rosario]] had ongoing problems with narcotics trafficking cartels, who had moved beyond the [[illegal drug trade]] into other illicit activities, such as [[extortion]] and [[contract killing]]. Bullrich announced the "Plan Bandera" ("Flag plan") with [[governor of Santa Fe Province]] [[Maximiliano Pullaro]] to deal with the problem. One of the initial actions was to enforce stricter conditions at the local prisons, as several crime bosses were already jailed but managed to control the criminal operations nonetheless. The national government installed [[mobile phone jammer]]s at those prisons, which led to death threats against Pullaro and his family.{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/politica/2023/12/18/bullrich-y-pullaro-ponen-en-marcha-el-operativo-bandera-contra-las-bandas-narcos/|title= Bullrich y Pullaro ponen en marcha el operativo “Bandera” contra las bandas narcos|trans-title= Bullrich and Pullaro launch the "Bandera" operation against drug gangs|language= Spanish|author= |date= 17 December 2023|publisher= Infobae|accessdate=14 November 2024}} Bullrich provided a report of the results of the operation six months later: homicides decreased by 73%, they detained 400 people linked to drug trade, 118 kilograms of cocaine and dope, 50,000 parts of [[synthetic drug]]s, 900 kilograms of ammonia, 57,005,062 [[Argentine peso|pesos]], 18,050 US dollars and 70 weapons.{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/politica/2024/06/11/bullrich-y-pullaro-presentaron-avances-del-plan-bandera-las-organizaciones-narco-tenian-el-aval-del-poder-politico-anterior/|title= Bullrich y Pullaro presentaron avances del Plan Bandera: “Las organizaciones narco tenían el aval del poder político anterior”|trans-title= Bullrich and Pullaro presented progress on Plan Bandera: "The drug trafficking organizations had the backing of the previous political power"|language= Spanish|author= Robertino Sánchez Flecha|date= 11 June 2024|publisher= Infobae|accessdate=14 November 2024}} Bullrich stayed president of PRO despite being a minister of a government for another party. Initially, both PRO and LLA were allies, but as both parties began to drift apart she sided with Milei's party instead of PRO. Both parties ran for the 2025 legislative elections on their own after the failure of the negotiations to create an electoral alliance. Bullrich took part in electoral parades of LLA, and encouraged politicians close to her move to the party. In May 2025 she formally left the PRO and joined LLA.{{cite web |title=Patricia Bullrich rompe definitivamente con Pro y se afilia a La Libertad Avanza |url=https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/patricia-bullrich-rompe-definitivamente-con-pro-y-se-afilia-a-la-libertad-avanza-nid06052025/ |publisher=La Nación |access-date=6 May 2025 |language=Spanish |date=6 May 2025}}{{cite web |last1=Devita |first1=Marina |title=“Fue fácil”: Patricia Bullrich se afilió a La Libertad Avanza con el picante respaldo de Karina Milei |url=https://www.clarin.com/politica/patricia-bullrich-rompe-oficialmente-pro-afilia-libertad-avanza-apoyo-adorni-respaldo-karina-milei_0_deNEebeF2w.html |publisher=Clarín |access-date=26 June 2025 |language=Spanish |date=6 May 2025 |trans-title="It was easy": Patricia Bullrich joined La Libertad Avanza with the spicy support of Karina Milei}} Following her election as senator in the 2025 legislative election, on 22 November 2025 President Javier Milei confirmed that [[Alejandra Monteoliva]] would be her successor.{{cite news |title=Quiénes son Carlos Presti y Alejandra Monteoliva, los nuevos ministros de Javier Milei |url=https://www.perfil.com/noticias/politica/quienes-son-carlos-presti-y-alejandra-monteoliva-los-nuevos-ministros-de-javier-milei.phtml |access-date=4 January 2026 |work=[[Perfil]] |date=22 November 2025}} Bullrich resigned on 1 December 2025 and Monteoliva took office the following day.{{cite news |title=Oficializaron la salida de Patricia Bullrich y designaron a Alejandra Monteoliva como la nueva ministra de Seguridad |url=https://www.infobae.com/politica/2025/12/02/oficializaron-la-salida-de-patricia-bullrich-y-designaron-a-alejandra-monteoliva-como-la-nueva-ministra-de-seguridad/ |access-date=4 January 2026 |work=Infobae |date=2 December 2025}}{{cite news |last1=Suazo |first1=Camilo |title=Reemplaza a Bullrich: quién es Alejandra Monteoliva, la nueva ministra de Seguridad Nacional de Milei |url=https://www.biobiochile.cl/noticias/internacional/america-latina/2025/12/02/reemplaza-a-bullrich-quien-es-alejandra-monteoliva-la-nueva-ministra-de-seguridad-nacional-de-milei.shtml |access-date=4 January 2026 |work=[[Radio Bío-Bío]] |date=2 December 2025}} == Political positions == [[File:La ministra de seguridad Patricia Bullrich anuncio el nuevo protocolo de actuación antipiquetes.jpg|thumb|Patricia Bullrich announces new policies against [[traffic obstruction]]s by [[piquetero]]s.]] Patricia Bullrich was a member of Peronist guerrillas in her youth, but grew critical of them as an adult. She described the ideas held by those organizations as an improvised blend of [[Argentine nationalism|nationalism]], [[Christianity in Argentina|Christianity]] and [[Socialism in Argentina|socialism]]. She explained that back then she thought that she was taking part in a [[revolution]] and that the people would eventually get used to it, but eventually realized that revolutions rarely have a positive outcome. She also thinks that she deluded herself by thinking that [[Peronism]] had goals that were not its actual goals. She started to have doubts over her allegiance to the guerrillas when she found out that Montoneros had killed the priest [[Carlos Mugica]], and the aide of Perón [[José Ignacio Rucci]]. She realized that Montoneros and the [[People's Revolutionary Army (Argentina)|People's Revolutionary Army]] killed people, even if they had not said so openly, and that she was helping that even if from a passive role. In her book ''Yo Propongo'', written during the presidency of Cristina Kirchner, Bullrich talks about [[populism]] and [[authoritarianism]]. She considers that democracy in Argentina has been weakened by a [[dominant-party system]] coupled with the dismantling of the lead party, which led to a system where only the president has authority, the party is an extension of the president's will, and government and state lost any meaningful difference. She considers that [[political polarization]] grew beyond only politics and engulfed other areas as well, such as academics and unions. She also pointed out that the Kirchners may want to organize a set of controlled media to advance a "narrative" that is positive towards their policies and hostile against the opposition. She also considers that this style of government was used by the Kirchners to advance policies that are long outdated in the rest of the world, and to conceal its negative results.{{cite book |last= Bullrich|first= Patricia|date= 2013|title= Yo propongo|trans-title= I propose|url= |language= Spanish|location= |publisher= |isbn=|pages=12–13}} In contrast, she proposes to dismantle in politics the dominant-party system, and in economics to advance a sustainable capitalist model.{{cite book |last= Bullrich|first= Patricia|date= 2013|title= Yo propongo|trans-title= I propose|url= |language= Spanish|location= |publisher= |isbn=|page=16}} In relation to the severity of [[law enforcement]], she supports a hardline policy against crime, rejecting the policies of [[Kirchnerism]] that grant too many rights to criminals and place rigid controls and obstacles on police work. Although those policies are largely a reaction against the policies of the military dictatorship, Bullrich says that "Argentina must leave the past and look to the future". In particular, she had conflicting views with [[Sabina Frederic]], minister of security of Alberto Fernández, over the use of [[taser]] guns, as Frederic restricted the police of the [[Argentine Federal Police]] from using either those or regular guns.{{cite web |url= https://www.infobae.com/politica/2020/10/01/patricia-bullrich-cruzo-a-sabina-frederic-por-sus-dichos-sobre-la-policia-de-la-ciudad-su-comentario-genera-odio-y-no-soluciones/|title= Patricia Bullrich cruzó a Sabina Frederic por sus dichos sobre la Policía de la Ciudad: "Su comentario genera odio y no soluciones"|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich confronted Sabina Frederic over her sayings about the city police: "her comment generates hate and not solutions"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 1 October 2020|publisher= Infobae|accessdate=28 May 2024}} When she was appointed minister of security by Javier Milei, she said "True change is possible if the law is applied in every corner of the country, equally for everyone and without privileges. Argentina needs order. We will be relentless against crime and we will wage a relentless fight against drug trafficking. It's simple: whoever does something, pays for it". Patricia Bullrich supported [[abortion]] during the debate in 2018 for a bill that would make it legal. She said that it did not make sense that only the woman could go to jail for it.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/video/patricia-bullrich-aborto-ministra-seguridad-argentina-macri-provida-sot2-perspectivas-buenos-aires|title= Patricia Bullrich: Estoy totalmente a favor de la despenalización del aborto en Argentina|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich: I'm fully in support of depenalizing abortion in Argentina|language= Spanish|author= |date= March 2018|publisher= CNN|accessdate=31 May 2024}} However, she also supported a clause for [[conscientious objection to abortion]] from medics that may refuse to do it, and to set a [[Minors and abortion|minimum age]] higher than in the proposed bill.{{cite web |url= https://www.lacapital.com.ar/politica/patricia-bullrich-se-pronuncio-favor-la-legalizacion-del-aborto-n1618865.html|title= Patricia Bullrich se pronunció a favor de la legalización del aborto|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich supports the legalization of abortion|language= Spanish|author= |date= 5 June 2018|publisher= La Capital|accessdate=31 May 2024}} The bill was not approved, and was proposed again in 2020. That time, Bullrich refused to comment her personal opinion on abortion, and considered instead that the country had more pressing economic and societal priorities.{{cite web |url= https://cnnespanol.cnn.com/radio/2020/12/09/patricia-bullrich-rechaza-el-debate-sobre-el-aborto-no-voy-a-dar-mi-opinion/|title= Patricia Bullrich y el debate sobre el aborto: "No voy a dar mi opinión personal"|trans-title= Patricia Bullrich, over the abortion: "I'll not share my personal opinion"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 9 December 2020|publisher= CNN|accessdate=31 May 2024}} For similar reasons, she did not agree with the proposal of [[Javier Milei]] to celebrate a referendum to abolish the bill approved in 2020: she said that it would halt the whole country for a couple of months, and that the priority was to solve the economic crisis.{{cite web |url= https://www.cadena3.com/noticia/cadena-3-elecciones-2023/bullrich-se-diferencio-de-milei-la-ley-del-aborto-se-ha-votado-y-esta-vigente_366036|title= Bullrich se diferenció de Milei: "La ley del aborto se ha votado y está vigente"|trans-title= Bullrich set herself apart from Milei: "The abortion law has been voted and it's in force"|language= Spanish|author= |date= 15 August 2023|publisher= Cadena 3|accessdate=31 May 2024}} ==Electoral history== ===Executive=== {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;" |+ {{sronly|Electoral history of Patricia Bullrich}} ! Esteban Bullrich (second cousin) }} Patricia Bullrich (; born 11 June 1956) is an Argentine politician. She currently serves as a National Senator for the Autonomous City of Buenos Aires since December 2025, representing La Libertad Avanza. She previously served as the Minister of National Security from 2023 to 2025 under president Javier Milei and from 2015 to 2019 under president Mauricio Macri. She was the chairwoman of the Republican Proposal until 2025. She was born in Buenos Aires, part of the Pueyrredón family. Bullrich graduated from the University of Palermo, and as a young woman she was involved with the Peronist Youth. She married Marcelo Langieri, secretary of Rodolfo Galimberti; Galimberti was a leader of the Montoneros guerrilla and her brother-in-law. They went into exile in 1977, during the Dirty War, and she returned in 1982, after the Falklands War. She was elected deputy for the Justicialist Party in 1993. She left the party and started her own, but could not secure a re-election. She joined the cabinet of president Fernando De la Rúa in 2001. Following the election of Mauricio Macri to the presidency in the 2015 Argentine general election, she became the Minister of Security. She placed regulations against roadblocks from piqueteros, and opposed the secessionist organization Resistencia Ancestral Mapuche. Leading the "hawk" sector of the Juntos por el Cambio coalition, she was appointed its president in 2020. She ran for the 2023 Argentine primary and general elections. She won the primaries against Horacio Rodríguez Larreta, but ended third in the general elections behind Sergio Massa and Javier Milei. She supported Milei on the ballotage with Massa. After Milei was elected President, he chose Bullrich to serve again as Minister of Security. On November 30, 2025, Bullrich resigned from her post as Minister of National Security after she was sworn in as a Senator in the Argentine Senate. Bullrich was born on 11 June 1956 in Buenos Aires, daughter of Alejandro Bullrich, a cardiologist, and Julieta Luro Pueyrredon. She belongs to two wealthy families on each of her parents' sides. On her mother's side, she belongs to the Pueyrredón family, a traditional lineage of Spanish, French, and Irish descent whose members featured prominently in the early years of Argentine Independence (such as Juan Martín de Pueyrredón and Honorio Pueyrredon). On her father's side, she descends from Adolfo Bullrich, a businessman and politician of German ancestry, who served as Mayor of Buenos Aires from 1898 to 1902. Although it was a wealthy family, Alejandro and Julieta divorced, and Patricia adjusted to a simpler lifestyle when her mother got a job as publicist. She also worked at the Cheburger fast food joint and became a member of the food workers' union, encouraging coworkers to unionise as well. She is the cousin of the singer-songwriter Fabiana Cantilo, and introduced her to Argentine rock. In the TV program "Almorzando con Mirtha Legrand" Cantilo explained that their mothers were close as siblings, and that Bullrich invited her to a concert of the band Pescado Rabioso, led by Luis Alberto Spinetta. This was the first concert that Fabiana Cantilo had attended, years before becoming a rock singer herself. Patricia skipped school classes one day to attend the music competition TV program "Si lo sabe cante", where she sang the song "El extraño de pelo largo" of La Joven Guardia. She was defeated in the competition. She was also present at the Plaza de Mayo on the International Workers' Day of 1974, when Perón, who had once again become president of Argentina, expelled the Montoneros and the left-wing youth groups from the celebrations. Although both the Peronist unions and guerrillas such as Montoneros had worked together against the Argentine Revolution dictatorship, they had begun a dispute over their political clout on the new democratic government; with the aforementioned expulsion Perón openly sided with the unions. Bullrich herself was a member of the Montoneros under the nom-de-guerre of "Cali", and active in the Columna Norte subgroup commanded by Rodolfo Galimberti. Galimberti was Bullrich's brother-in-law, as he was married to Bullrich's sister, Julieta. Bullrich has denied being a Montoneros member, and maintains that she was just a member of the JP. Perón died in 1974 and the Dirty War, an armed conflict between the Montoneros and the military, worsened: the guerrillas resumed the tactics used years before to attempt to remove the current authorities from power. In 1975, Bullrich was arrested for spray-painting political messages on the entrance of the University of Buenos Aires Faculty of Philosophy and Letters, and spent six months in prison. Upon being released, she dropped out of her sociology degree at the University of Buenos Aires and enrolled at the Universidad de Belgrano to study law. The 1976 coup d'état established a military dictatorship, the National Reorganization Process, that continued the armed conflict against the Montoneros. Montoneros organized an attack on 4 September 1976, that intended to kill a manager of the Sudamtex textile firm. On her way to participate, Bullrich felt that she was being followed and fled. The military had abducted one of the masterminds of the plan and learned about the attack, which allowed them to surprise the Montoneros and kill the whole unit. She was still a member of Montoneros living abroad, until Galimberti cut ties with the organization in 1979. Peronism lost the 1983 Argentine general election, so she joined the internal faction of Antonio Cafiero that sought to renew the party. Peronism returned to power in 1989 when Carlos Menem won the 1989 Argentine general election. She was elected deputy for the Justicialist Party (PJ) in 1993, alongside Erman González. Patricia Bullrich left the PJ in 1996 and started New Leadership alongside Gustavo Béliz. She had disagreements with him because she thought that he managed the party unilaterally, without making her part of the decisions. She left the party as a result and, after briefly considering joining the Frepaso, she started another party. Initially named Cambio 97, it was soon renamed "Unión por Todos" (UPT) as the Cambio 97 name had already been registered. The new party did not get enough votes to allow her to be reelected as deputy in the 1997 Argentine legislative election. She then worked for the Buenos Aires Province on security matters, assisting the municipality of Hurlingham. She resigned in 1998, because of conflicts with León Arslanián, minister of justice of the province. thumb|left|Patricia Bullrich at the time she served as deputy. In 1999, the UPT became part of the Alliance for Work, Justice and Education, which took Fernando de la Rúa to the Presidency. Bullrich was appointed to office in the Department of Criminal Policy and Penitentiary Matters. In 2001, she was made a cabinet minister, as Secretary of Labour, Employment and Human Resources and, later that year, as Secretary of Social Security. De la Rúa resigned, and eventually the governor of Buenos Aires Eduardo Duhalde was appointed president by the Congress. Bullrich relaunched the UPT in 2002, aiming for the 2003 presidential elections. The party platform announced reforms on labor and education. The UPT did not take part in the elections, supporting instead Ricardo López Murphy of the Recreate for Growth party, who ended in the third place. Both parties united in the alliance "Unión para Recrear Buenos Aires", proposing Bullrich for mayor of Buenos Aires. She ended in the fourth place, with 9.76% of the vote. The UPT joined forces with the Support for an Egalitarian Republic (ARI) and created the Civic Coalition to run for the 2007 Argentine general election, with Elisa Carrió as a candidate for president. Bullrich ran instead for deputy for Buenos Aires. Her centrist politics and polemical history as a government minister, however, contributed to the disenchantment of a group of left-wing members of ARI who left the Civic Coalition. thumb|Patricia Bullrich presents a report about the [[Resistencia Ancestral Mapuche organization.]] Mauricio Macri was elected president in 2015 and Patricia Bullrich was appointed Minister of Security. Within the first days, three criminals convicted for torturing and killing three pharmaceutical businessmen escaped from a high-security prison, leading to a nation-wide operation to recapture them. Bullrich announced on 9 January that the three criminals had been captured, but, in reality, only one had been captured. She tendered her resignation to Macri because of the mistake, but Macri rejected it. The other two criminals were captured a few days later. Bullrich proposed a protocol to remove roadblocks caused by piqueteros. Such blocks should be announced in advance, restrict only part of the roads, and last for a short time. They should cease if ordered so by the police, and those who commit violent actions shall be detained. This protocol was approved by a meeting of the ministers of security of all provinces of Argentina. Horacio Rodríguez Larreta, chief of government of the Buenos Aires city, and María Eugenia Vidal, governor of the Buenos Aires province, refused to endorse it, as they thought that doing so could prove controversial. Instead, they preferred to negotiate and stay on good terms with organizations of piqueteros such as the Evita Movement. Larreta also explained that the city does not need to endorse the protocols because it already has regulations for roadblocks. Resistencia Ancestral Mapuche (RAM) and the Coordinadora Arauco-Malleco (CAM), two Mapuche organizations that normally operate in Patagonia, expanded their attacks to the city of Buenos Aires. Their goal is to create a Mapuche nation, seceding from Argentina and Chile. Bullrich said that "We won't allow an autonomous mapuche republic inside Argentina; that's the logic of their request, the denial of the Argentine state, the anarchist logic. Our resolution is total and absolute in not allowing a group that uses violence as a way of action in Argentina." Facundo Jones Huala, leader of RAM, claimed to be a political prisoner, and that the RAM only acts in self-defense on lands that used to belong to them. Bullrich organized a joint command with the ministers of security of the Neuquén, Río Negro and Chubut provinces against the RAM, and delivered a report of the actions taken against the organization, including 96 lawsuits, 2 of them for murder. The Argentine National Gendarmerie forced a group of Mapuche demonstrators to cease a roadblock next to the Chubut River, and the demonstrator Santiago Maldonado went missing after that. The case became a national scandal, with the Gendarmerie (by extension their political authorities, Bullrich and Macri) being accused of an enforced disappearance. Bullrich gave her full support to the Gendarmerie. Maldonado was found dead some months later, drowned in the Chubut river. Bullrich said "The whole world saw what happened with Maldonado. He stayed in the same place where he drowned, without being seen or touched by anyone. Neither the Gendarmerie nor our government would have made a person disappear". Bullrich and Macri supported the actions of Chocobar, on the grounds that it would be a right of self-defense, Adrián Marcenac, father of Alfredo Marcenac, a student from Necochea who was murdered by serial shooter Martín Ríos in July 2006 in Belgrano, Buenos Aires, labelled Bullrich's comments as "enormously grave" for Argentine society. thumb|[[AI image posted by Javier Milei to announce his alliance with Bullrich. Milei associates himself with imagery of lions, and "pato" ("duck") is a common nickname in Spanish for people named "Patricia". Bullrich, who was appointed president of the PRO in 2020, maintained a positive relationship with Milei, and at one point considered forming an alliance for the 2023 Argentine general election. However, Milei ultimately decided to run independently with his own party. There was an expectation that Mauricio Macri would run for president once again, as even in defeat he had gotten 41% of the vote in 2019 and his image had improved since then. However, he declined to do so, so the party had primary elections between Bullrich and Horacio Rodríguez Larreta. They represented the "hawks" and "doves" of the party. Macri did not openly side with any of them. Bullrich chose the former legislator Luis Petri, from the UCR, as the candidate for vice-president. Petri had lost the primary elections for governor of Mendoza to Alfredo Cornejo a short time before. She chose him because he was young, sided with the "hawks", and was not from Buenos Aires; and also because he worked with her during her time as minister of security. As Petri was from the UCR, Bullrich would secure the support of the radicals that were not pleased with the leadership of Gerardo Morales (who supported Larreta), and with the whole party if Larreta was defeated in the primaries. Other politicians briefly considered to run for vice-president under her were Carlos Melconian, Maximiliano Abad, Luis Naidenoff, and Ricardo López Murphy. Carlos Melconian was proposed instead as Bullrich's potential minister of economy. In contrast with Milei, who proposed a full dollarization of the economy, Melconian proposed instead a bimonetary economy, where both the Argentine peso and the United States dollar would be legal tender. Before implementing it, the fiscal deficit would be eliminated, the by-laws of the Central Bank would be amended to prohibit money creation, and the restrictions on the use of US dollars would be removed. She also proposed the creation of a maximum security prison named after former president Cristina Kirchner. The primary elections ended with a decisive victory of Bullrich over Larreta. Once the primaries were concluded, the candidates took part in the presidential debates, explaining their positions and proposals over several topics. Bullrich promised to end inflation, and to end the roadblocks caused by piqueteros. Although the primary elections ended with the three main candidates, Bullrich, Milei and Peronist Sergio Massa in a close tie, Massa got ahead in the general election and won with 36% of the vote, followed by Milei with 30%. As a result, both candidates went to a third ballotage election. Bullrich did not take part in it, as JXC ended in third place, but both candidates sought to get the support of her voters. Massa made a speech about institutional stability and public security, usual topics of the UCR and PRO respectively, and Milei focused on the need to join forces against Kirchnerism. On 25 October 2023, Bullrich officially endorsed Javier Milei for the runoff election. She did not ask the other parties within JXC, the UCR and the CC, for their opinion, as they have conflicting views over Milei. Both of them met the Wednesday after the elections to negotiate this support, alongside Macri. Milei announced the alliance on his X account, with an AI image of an embrace between a lion and a duck. Milei associates himself with imagery of lions, and "pato" ("duck") is a common nickname in Spanish for people named "Patricia". thumb|Patricia Bullrich announced a decrease in homicides in [[Rosario.]] In December 2023, Patricia Bullrich returned to government as security minister in president Javier Milei's Cabinet. Bullrich immediately issued a protocol to deal with demonstrations and roadblocks made by piqueteros. The Workers' Party made an habeas corpus request to prevent the protocol from coming into force, which judge Gustavo Pierretti rejected. Several groups of Piqueteros called for a massive demonstration on 20 December, a week after the inauguration of Javier Milei. The government announced that demonstrators who committed crimes during demonstrations, such as roadblocks or vandalism, would lose their welfare payments, and provided a phone number to denounce piquetero leaders who would be coercing people to take part in such demonstrations. The demonstration had very little attendance, and the phone calls were used to start 660 criminal complaints. The policies were kept for all later protests. The welfare aid that was outsourced to piquetero organizations returned to the direct state control, and social leaders that worked at the ministry of Social Development despite the conflict of interest were fired. Several audits also revealed that several social organizations that used to control the outsourced welfare aid only used a fraction of it for such a purpose. All this decreased the power of piqueteros, and protests and demonstrations rapidly decreased in size. The city of Rosario had ongoing problems with narcotics trafficking cartels, who had moved beyond the illegal drug trade into other illicit activities, such as extortion and contract killing. Bullrich announced the "Plan Bandera" ("Flag plan") with governor of Santa Fe Province Maximiliano Pullaro to deal with the problem. One of the initial actions was to enforce stricter conditions at the local prisons, as several crime bosses were already jailed but managed to control the criminal operations nonetheless. The national government installed mobile phone jammers at those prisons, which led to death threats against Pullaro and his family. Bullrich provided a report of the results of the operation six months later: homicides decreased by 73%, they detained 400 people linked to drug trade, 118 kilograms of cocaine and dope, 50,000 parts of synthetic drugs, 900 kilograms of ammonia, 57,005,062 pesos, 18,050 US dollars and 70 weapons. Bullrich stayed president of PRO despite being a minister of a government for another party. Initially, both PRO and LLA were allies, but as both parties began to drift apart she sided with Milei's party instead of PRO. Both parties ran for the 2025 legislative elections on their own after the failure of the negotiations to create an electoral alliance. Bullrich took part in electoral parades of LLA, and encouraged politicians close to her move to the party. In May 2025 she formally left the PRO and joined LLA. Following her election as senator in the 2025 legislative election, on 22 November 2025 President Javier Milei confirmed that Alejandra Monteoliva would be her successor. Bullrich resigned on 1 December 2025 and Monteoliva took office the following day. thumb|Patricia Bullrich announces new policies against [[traffic obstructions by piqueteros.]] Patricia Bullrich was a member of Peronist guerrillas in her youth, but grew critical of them as an adult. She described the ideas held by those organizations as an improvised blend of nationalism, Christianity and socialism. She explained that back then she thought that she was taking part in a revolution and that the people would eventually get used to it, but eventually realized that revolutions rarely have a positive outcome. She also thinks that she deluded herself by thinking that Peronism had goals that were not its actual goals. She started to have doubts over her allegiance to the guerrillas when she found out that Montoneros had killed the priest Carlos Mugica, and the aide of Perón José Ignacio Rucci. She realized that Montoneros and the People's Revolutionary Army killed people, even if they had not said so openly, and that she was helping that even if from a passive role. In contrast, she proposes to dismantle in politics the dominant-party system, and in economics to advance a sustainable capitalist model. In relation to the severity of law enforcement, she supports a hardline policy against crime, rejecting the policies of Kirchnerism that grant too many rights to criminals and place rigid controls and obstacles on police work. Although those policies are largely a reaction against the policies of the military dictatorship, Bullrich says that "Argentina must leave the past and look to the future". When she was appointed minister of security by Javier Milei, she said "True change is possible if the law is applied in every corner of the country, equally for everyone and without privileges. Argentina needs order. We will be relentless against crime and we will wage a relentless fight against drug trafficking. It's simple: whoever does something, pays for it". However, she also supported a clause for conscientious objection to abortion from medics that may refuse to do it, and to set a minimum age higher than in the proposed bill. The bill was not approved, and was proposed again in 2020. That time, Bullrich refused to comment her personal opinion on abortion, and considered instead that the country had more pressing economic and societal priorities. For similar reasons, she did not agree with the proposal of Javier Milei to celebrate a referendum to abolish the bill approved in 2020: she said that it would halt the whole country for a couple of months, and that the priority was to solve the economic crisis. {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;" |+ !
