web|url=https://www.gamespot.com/articles/devil-may-crys-future-isnt-tied-to-how-well-devil-/1100-6428432/|archive-url=https://archive.today/20180209073552/https://www.gamespot.com/articles/devil-may-crys-future-isnt-tied-to-how-well-devil-/1100-6428432/|archive-date=February 9, 2018|title=Devil May Cry's Future Isn't Tied to How Well Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition Sells|website=[[GameSpot]]|access-date=August 8, 2018|url-status=live}} Trish was added to ''[[Marvel vs. Capcom 3: Fate of Two Worlds]]'' because Capcom wanted to have a "sexy character" in the game, according to producer Ryota Niitsuma.{{cite web|url=http://www.siliconera.com/2010/07/26/marvel-vs-capcom-3-trish-and-super-skrull-hands-on/|title=Marvel Vs. Capcom 3 Trish And Super-Skrull Hands On|date=July 26, 2010 |publisher=Siliconera|access-date=August 8, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180810011123/http://www.siliconera.com/2010/07/26/marvel-vs-capcom-3-trish-and-super-skrull-hands-on/|archive-date=August 10, 2018|url-status=live}} Trish's English-language dialog is voiced by [[Sarah Lafleur]] in ''Devil May Cry'' and ''[[Viewtiful Joe: Red Hot Rumble]]'', Danielle Burgio in ''Devil May Cry 4'' and ''Marvel vs. Capcom 3'', and [[Luci Christian]] in ''[[Devil May Cry: The Animated Series|The Animated Series]]''. In Japanese, she is voiced by [[Atsuko Tanaka (voice actress)|Atsuko Tanaka]] in ''The Animated Series'', ''Marvel vs. Capcom 3'' / ''Ultimate Marvel vs. Capcom 3'', and ''Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition''. Tanaka said she liked the relationship between Trish, [[Lady (Devil May Cry)|Lady]], and Dante.{{cite web|url=https://www.famitsu.com/anime/news/2007/04/18/681,1176883264,70410,0,0.html|title=2007年6月より放送が開始されるアニメ『デビル メイ クライ』アフレコレポート！|publisher=[[Famitsu]]|language=Japanese|access-date=August 11, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180613063907/https://www.famitsu.com/anime/news/2007/04/18/681,1176883264,70410,0,0.html|archive-date=June 13, 2018|url-status=live}} Tanaka further liked Trish's portrayal in the anime series due to the action displayed in her fight against Lady. She was satisfied with being included in the anime series.{{cite web|url=http://www.dmc-tv.com/blog/2007/07/tvdevil_may_cry.php|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071025005900/http://www.dmc-tv.com/blog/2007/07/tvdevil_may_cry.php|title=アフレコ取材キャストコメント|website=DMC|archive-date=October 25, 2007|access-date=August 30, 2020}} ==Appearances== ===In ''Devil May Cry''=== Debuting in ''Devil May Cry'', Trish hires Dante to stop [[Mundus (Devil May Cry)|Mundus]]' plan to conquer the human world by opening a gate on Mallet Island.{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry |developer= [[Capcom]]|date= 2001|platform=|quote='''Trish:''' It seems that way, but I'm not your enemy. My name is Trish. I came here to seek your help. To put an end to the Underworld. / '''Dante: '''What?}} Later in the game, Dante learns she is Mundus' servant and that her resemblance to his "mother" is part of a plan to lure him to the island so Mundus' servants can kill him.{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry |developer = [[Capcom]] |date= 2001|platform=|quote='''Mundus:''' Trish, Vergil has been defeated. You know what you must do. Now go. / '''Trish:''' Yes, master.}}{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry|developer=[[Capcom]]|date=2001|platform=|quote='''Mundus:''' Those eyes... deep in them I see the same light as in Sparda's eyes. / '''Dante:''' Why my mother? / '''Mundus:''' That useless being? If you need a mother, I can create as many as you want. Just like I created Trish. / '''Dante:''' Silence!}} Despite Trish showing her true agenda, Dante saves her life. Deeming her unworthy to serve him, Mundus crucifies Trish to antagonise Dante. During Dante's last fight against the demon lord, Trish reappears and offers her power so he can finish the enemy. The two then become partners.{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry |developer = [[Capcom]] |date= 2001|platform=|quote='''Trish:''' (on the telephone) Devil Never Cry? Yes. OK, great! Where's the place? We'll be right there! This one has the password. Sounds heavy. / '''Dante:''' Okay! Let's get over there within 10 minutes and don't let one of those suckers live.}} She appears in the ''[[Devil May Cry (novels)#Devil May Cry Volume 2|Devil May Cry 2]]'' novel as an alternative persona from a parallel world serving Mundus. The original Dante tries to persuade her to ally with demons fighting against the Lord of Darkness, but he eventually has to kill her.{{cite book|title=Devil May Cry 2|year=2006|publisher=[[TokyoPop]]|isbn=978-1598164510|author=Gokeida, Shinya|page=143}} She is a [[Player character|playable character]] in a bonus section of ''Devil May Cry 2'', separate from the game's plot as she is not present in the story. In the [[anime]] ''The Animated Series'', Trish mostly works alone, and occasionally with Dante, who invited her to return. She first appears while accidentally fighting demon hunter Lady who views her as an enemy at first though they become friends after Dante interrupts the battle.{{cite episode|title=Rolling Thunder|series=[[Devil May Cry: The Animated Series]]|airdate=July 5, 2007|publisher=[[Madhouse (company)|Madhouse]]}} Trish and Lady help Dante stop a new demon invasion in the finale of the anime.{{cite episode|title=Stylish!|series=[[Devil May Cry: The Animated Series]]|airdate=September 6, 2007|publisher=[[Madhouse (company)|Madhouse]]}} Trish reappears in ''Devil May Cry 4'' to work again with Dante. She disguises herself as Gloria, an executive with the Holy Knights of the Order of Sparda—a religious organization that worships [[Sparda (Devil May Cry)|Sparda]]. After the Order's leader [[Sanctus (Devil May Cry)|Sanctus]] obtains the power to summon demons from another dimension, Trish goes to protect Fortuna's citizens while Dante goes to dispatch the enemy.{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry 4|publisher=[[Capcom]]|year=2008|developer=[[Capcom]]|quote='''Trish:''' I'll sweep the city and evacuate the people. / '''Dante:''' Hey! Is this your way of ditching and dumping this mess on- / '''Trish:''' You wanna switch? / '''Dante:''' It's cool. Let's stick to the plan.}} In the epilogue, the two receive no payment from Lady, who states Trish made the situation worse.{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry 4|publisher=[[Capcom]]|year=2008|developer=[[Capcom]]|quote='''Trish:''' If it was a lifesaver gig then don't you think we deserve a little more? You call this sincerity? / '''Lady:''' More? Was it not your fault that things escalated to the level that they did? Bringing Sparda into the spotlight?}} In the updated version of the game, ''Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition'', Trish is once again a playable character. She is also present in ''[[Devil May Cry 5]]'' but unplayable.{{cite web|url=http://www.siliconera.com/2018/09/19/devil-may-cry-5-shows-off-trish-lady-v-and-neros-mega-buster/|title=Devil May Cry 5 Shows Off Trish, Lady, V, And Nero's Mega Buster|publisher=Siliconera|access-date=September 20, 2018|date=September 20, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180920091313/http://www.siliconera.com/2018/09/19/devil-may-cry-5-shows-off-trish-lady-v-and-neros-mega-buster/|archive-date=September 20, 2018|url-status=live}} During the game's beginning, Trish, Lady and Dante are defeated by the Demon King Urizen. Trish and Lady are sealed inside the demons [[Cavaliere (Devil May Cry)|Cavaliere]] and [[Artemis (Devil May Cry)|Artemis]] respectively, with Dante later saving her once defeating it.{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry 5|publisher=[[Capcom]]|developer=[[Capcom]]|year=2019|quote='''Urizen:''' I have plans for the both of you. You will exist to give me strength. How fortunate to serve such divine purpose."}}{{cite video game|title=Devil May Cry 5|publisher=[[Capcom]]|developer=[[Capcom]]|year=2019|quote='''Dante''': You okay? / '''Trish:''' I've been better.}} ===Other appearances=== Trish appears alongside Dante in the [[PlayStation 2]] version of ''[[Viewtiful Joe (video game)|Viewtiful Joe]]'', hitting on him until an enemy captures her.{{cite video game|title=Viewtiful Joe |developer = [[Capcom]]|date= 2004|platform=PlayStation 2|quote='''Trish:''' You can't fool me, you know. You latest girlfriend kicked you out? Sure you're only interested in human girls, Dante? / '''Dante:''' Trish, it's not like that! /.../ '''Trish:''' Tonight it's just you and me...}} In the [[PlayStation Portable]] game ''Viewtiful Joe: Red Hot Rumble'', Trish appears as an alternative skin for the character Sexy Silvia. She is also present in the [[List of crossovers in video games|crossover]] fighting games ''Marvel vs. Capcom 3: Fate of Two Worlds'' and ''Ultimate Marvel vs. Capcom 3''. Her Gloria outfit also appears in the fighting game ''[[Street Fighter V]]'' as an alternative costume for [[List of Street Fighter characters#Laura|Laura]].{{cite web|url=http://www.siliconera.com/2018/06/20/street-fighter-v-arcade-edition-getting-devil-may-cry-costumes-air-man-costume/|title=Street Fighter V: Arcade Edition Is Getting Devil May Cry Costumes And An Air Man Costume|date=June 20, 2018 |publisher=Siliconera|access-date=August 9, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180720110047/http://www.siliconera.com/2018/06/20/street-fighter-v-arcade-edition-getting-devil-may-cry-costumes-air-man-costume/|archive-date=July 20, 2018|url-status=live}} The character has also appeared in ''Onimusha Soul''.{{cite web|url=https://www.4gamer.net/games/150/G015000/20130321035/|title=綾里真宵やジルが「鬼武者Soul」に参戦。最新ブースター「カプコンヒロインズ」の武将15名が公開に|website=4Gamer|language=Japanese|access-date=May 24, 2025}} ==Reception== Critical reception of Trish has been positive. ''[[Complex (magazine)|Complex]]'' called her "charming" and said they liked her abilities, and enthusiastically awaited her appearance in ''Marvel vs. Capcom 3''.{{cite magazine|url=https://www.complex.com/pop-culture/2012/06/the-50-hottest-video-game-characters/rayne|title=The 50 Hottest Video Game Characters|magazine=[[Complex (magazine)|Complex]]|access-date=August 8, 2018|date=June 27, 2012|author=Hester, Larry|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180810011614/https://www.complex.com/pop-culture/2012/06/the-50-hottest-video-game-characters/rayne|archive-date=August 10, 2018|url-status=live}} Her alter ego Gloria was called one of the most sexually appealing characters in gaming due to her skimpy design.{{cite web|url=http://www.manolith.com/2009/12/09/hottest-videogame-babes|title=25 Hottest Female Video Game Protagonists|date=December 9, 2009|archive-date=December 12, 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091212225855/http://www.manolith.com/2009/12/09/hottest-videogame-babes}} Despite enjoying Trish's redesign and Gloria in ''Devil May Cry 4'', ''[[GamesTM]]'' was disappointed by her lack of appearances in the sequel and expected to see her as a playable character in an update.{{cite journal|journal=[[GamesTM]]|page=113|issn=1478-5889|publisher=Future Publishing|issue=15|year=2008|title=Ladies' Night}} Trish and Dante were included in ''[[The Inquirer]]''{{'}}s list of the most memorable video-game love teams; comments focused on the way they join forces to defeat their enemies.{{cite web|url=http://technology.inquirer.net/infotech/infotech/view/20070202-47164/The_most_memorable_video_game_love_teams |title=The most memorable video game love teams |publisher=[[The Inquirer]] |author=Villafania, Alexander |date=February 2, 2007 |access-date=February 2, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101019171506/http://technology.inquirer.net/infotech/infotech/view/20070202-47164/The_most_memorable_video_game_love_teams |archive-date=October 19, 2010}} The ''[[Official Xbox Magazine]]'' regarded Trish as an interesting sidekick to Dante based on her personality.{{cite magazine|title=Official Xbox Magazine|issn=1534-7850|date=May 2019|publisher=Future plc}} ''Siliconera'' found her techniques "unique", citing the way she adds traps to the arena and their impact on fighting enemies. In contrast to the positive reception, ''Play'' listed Gloria's look as one of the most inappropriate outfits in gaming, describing it as "slutty clubbing gear" rather than a hunter costume.{{cite web|url=https://www.play-mag.co.uk/features/top-10-inappropriate-outfits/|title=Top 10 inappropriate outfits|date=August 17, 2010|access-date=November 19, 2018|publisher=Play|archive-date=November 14, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181114230401/https://www.play-mag.co.uk/features/top-10-inappropriate-outfits/|url-status=live}} Despite criticizing several parts from Trish' character in her debut, Bloody Disgusting was surprised by the character's remorse involving how she betrays Dante but ends up joining him later following her fake death.{{cite web|url=https://bloody-disgusting.com/editorials/3555469/demons-swords-sons-brief-look-devil-may-cry-series-story/|title=Demons, Swords and Sons: A Brief Look Into the 'Devil May Cry' Series' Story|access-date=January 18, 2023|website=BloodyDisgusting|date=April 13, 2019 |archive-date=September 15, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220915190841/https://bloody-disgusting.com/editorials/3555469/demons-swords-sons-brief-look-devil-may-cry-series-story/|url-status=live}} Trish's inclusion in ''Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition'' has received a positive response. ''[[PC Magazine|PCMag]]'' stated that her moves seemed to be inspired by her appearances in ''Devil May Cry 2'' and ''Marvel vs. Capcom 3'', describing her as "a very accessible" but difficult-to-master character.{{cite magazine|url=https://www.pcmag.com/article2/0,2817,2487109,00.asp|title=Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition (for PC)|magazine=[[PC Magazine|PCMag]]|access-date=August 8, 2018|date=July 14, 2015|author=Zamora, Gabriel|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180726005539/https://www.pcmag.com/article2/0,2817,2487109,00.asp|archive-date=July 26, 2018|url-status=live}} ''[[Game Informer]]'' enjoyed the way Trish can change weapons during fights, saying that while she might be regarded as simple to control, mastering all of her abilities might be so challenging she may be overshadowed by [[Vergil (Devil May Cry)|Vergil]].{{cite magazine|magazine=[[Game Informer]]|url=https://www.gameinformer.com/games/devil_may_cry_4_special_edition/b/playstation4/archive/2015/06/23/devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review-game-informer.aspx|title=Game Informer: Devil May Cry 4 Special Edition|access-date=August 8, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180116013656/https://www.gameinformer.com/games/devil_may_cry_4_special_edition/b/playstation4/archive/2015/06/23/devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review-game-informer.aspx|archive-date=January 16, 2018|url-status=dead|author=Juba, Joe|date=June 23, 2015}} ''[[IGN]]'' liked her moves because of the balance between them and Lady's techniques.{{cite web| website=[[IGN]]| url=http://www.ign.com/articles/2015/06/23/devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review| title=IGN: Devil May Cry 4 Special Edition (PS5)| date=June 23, 2018| access-date=August 8, 2018| author=Otero, Jose| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180725214940/http://www.ign.com/articles/2015/06/23/devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review| archive-date=July 25, 2018| url-status=live}} ''[[Game Revolution]]'' liked the ''Marvel vs. Capcom 3''-inspired moves, describing them as appealing.{{cite web| publisher=[[Game Revolution]]| url=http://www.gamerevolution.com/review/68330-devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review#/slide/1| title=Devil May Cry 4 Special Edition (PS4)| access-date=August 8, 2018| date=June 23, 2015| author=Paras, Peter| archive-date=October 11, 2018| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181011234412/https://www.gamerevolution.com/review/68330-devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review#/slide/1| url-status=live}} ''[[GameSpot]]'' offered a less positive opinion, saying Trish might be less entertaining than Lady and Vergil.{{cite web|url=https://www.gamespot.com/reviews/devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review/1900-6416173/|title=Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition Review|website=[[GameSpot]]|access-date=August 8, 2018|date=June 23, 2015|author=Renaudin, Josiah|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180623004822/https://www.gamespot.com/reviews/devil-may-cry-4-special-edition-review/1900-6416173/|archive-date=June 23, 2018|url-status=live}} PushSquare highly praised Trish' new moves, finding them impressive especially when compared with her ''Devil May Cry 2'' persona.{{cite web|url=https://www.pushsquare.com/news/2015/05/playable_trish_is_rocking_some_impressive_firepower_in_devil_may_cry_4_special_edition|title=Playable Trish Is Rocking Some Impressive Firepower in Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition|access-date=January 18, 2023|website=PushSquare|date=May 11, 2015 |archive-date=January 18, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240118225627/https://www.pushsquare.com/news/2015/05/playable_trish_is_rocking_some_impressive_firepower_in_devil_may_cry_4_special_edition|url-status=live}} Her character has also been explored in the anime series. Voice actor [[Fumiko Orikasa]] said the fight between Trish and Lady was appealing.{{cite web|url=https://www.famitsu.com/anime/news/1193164_1558.html|title=2007年6月より放送が開始されるアニメ『デビル メイ クライ』アフレコレポート！|publisher=[[Famitsu]]|access-date=July 29, 2018|language=Japanese|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729200528/https://www.famitsu.com/anime/news/1193164_1558.html|archive-date=July 29, 2018|url-status=live}} ''[[Hyper (magazine)|Hyper]]'' had mixed thoughts regarding the episode in which Lady meets Trish through a fight but compared the style of the two female characters to the film ''[[The Matrix]]'' because both of them are stylish.{{cite journal|journal=[[Hyper (magazine)|Hyper]]|page=62|issn=1320-7458|publisher=Next Publishing Pty Ltd|title=Movies & Anime|issue=9|year=2007}} ''[[Anime News Network]]'' expressed disappointment at the brevity of Trish's appearances in the anime but still found her English voice acting charming.{{cite news|url=https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/review/devil-may-cry/dvd-complete-box-set|title=Devil May Cry DVD - Complete Box Set|work=[[Anime News Network]]|access-date=July 25, 2018|date=February 17, 2010|author=Chapman, Jacob|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180708185905/https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/review/devil-may-cry/dvd-complete-box-set|archive-date=July 8, 2018|url-status=live}} ''[[GroundReport]]'' described Trish and Lady as foils for Dante and compared them with the main characters from the [[manga]] and anime series ''[[GetBackers]]'' due to Dante's financial issues, often receiving jobs from Lady.{{cite web|url=https://www.groundreport.com/anime-review-of-devil-may-cry/|title=Anime Review of Devil May Cry 3|publisher=[[GroundReport]]|access-date=July 25, 2018|date=October 14, 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180725214439/https://www.groundreport.com/anime-review-of-devil-may-cry/|archive-date=July 25, 2018|url-status=live}} ''The Fandom Post'' appreciated the episode in which Lady fights Trish because of a misunderstanding but they befriend each other. The writer also enjoyed the duo's role in the following episodes, most notably in the finale.{{cite web|url=https://www.fandompost.com/2017/09/14/devil-may-cry-complete-collection-uk-anime-dvd-review/|title=Devil May Cry Complete Collection UK Anime DVD Review|publisher=The Fandom Post|access-date=July 25, 2018|date=September 14, 2017|author=Homer, Chris|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180707202346/https://www.fandompost.com/2017/09/14/devil-may-cry-complete-collection-uk-anime-dvd-review/|archive-date=July 7, 2018|url-status=live}} ''[[DVD Talk]]'' enjoyed the interactions between Trish, Dante, and Lady and wanted to see more of them in the anime rather than stories focused on Dante facing enemies.{{cite web|url=https://www.dvdtalk.com/reviews/38302/devil-may-cry-the-complete-series-box-set/|title=Devil May Cry: The Complete Series Box Set (Blu-ray)|publisher=[[DVD Talk]]|access-date=July 7, 2018|date=August 25, 2009|author=Douglass Jr., Todd|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180707172720/https://www.dvdtalk.com/reviews/38302/devil-may-cry-the-complete-series-box-set/|archive-date=July 7, 2018|url-status=live}} ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} {{Devil May Cry}} [[Category:Demon characters in video games]] [[Category:Devil May Cry characters]] [[Category:Female characters in video games]] [[Category:Fictional characters with electric or magnetic abilities]] [[Category:Fictional demon hunters]] [[Category:Fictional gunfighters]] [[Category:Fictional hunters in video games]] [[Category:Video game characters introduced in 2001]] [[Category:Fictional characters with superhuman durability or invulnerability]]
stable.clean_text: {{Infobox character | name = Trish | image = TrishDevilmayCry.png | caption = Trish as seen in Devil May Cry 5 (2019) | alt = A blonde woman wearing skimpy clothes while wielding guns. | series = Devil May Cry | firstgame = Devil May Cry (2001) | creator = Hideki Kamiya | designer = Sawaki TakeyasuDaigo Ikeno (Devil May Cry 2)Tatsuya Yoshikawa (Devil May Cry 4 and 5) | voice = | motion_actor = Danielle Burgio (Devil May Cry 4)Ariana Diamant (Devil May Cry 5) | species = Demon }} is a character in Devil May Cry, an action-adventure game series by Japanese developer and publisher Capcom. She debuted in the first installment of the series, released in 2001. Trish is a demon taking a female human form to look like the mother of Dante, Eva. She seeks the aid of protagonist Dante in eliminating her creator, Mundus. Besides being a supporting character in subsequent games, she also appears in the other titles created by Capcom. Hideki Kamiya, with the support of the other designers at Capcom, created Trish. Her long hair caused difficulties for 3D and motion graphic designers; this aspect of her character was revised on multiple occasions: in Devil May Cry 4 (2008), Trish adopted the alter-ego Gloria, which also proved difficult to design. Developers designed Gloria as a sexually explicit character to appeal to the franchise's casual fan-base, though they sought to avoid any vulgarity. Various voice actors have voiced the character throughout the series, including Sarah Lafleur, Luci Christian and Atsuko Tanaka. Critical reception of Trish has been mostly positive. Reviewers have praised her role in the story, her design, and her relationship with the cast explored in the franchise. Despite the criticism of her movesets in Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition (2015), her inclusion in that game and in the fighting game Marvel vs. Capcom 3: Fate of Two Worlds / Ultimate Marvel vs. Capcom 3 (2011) as a playable character have received positive responses. Created as the heroine of Devil May Cry, her long hair caused extensive problems for designers when creating her 3D model, most notably during the ending sequence. Motion designer Tomoya Othsubo said making Trish's hairstyle was challenging due its movements in-game; the designers had to add multiple joints to her strands of hair, a process which Othsubo described as a "nightmare". Sawaki Takeyasu, the main artist for Trish, said that she was the first human-like character he had made. Trish was named after Beatrice Portinari from the Divine Comedy. When asked about the relationship between Dante and Trish, Hideki Kamiya stated their bond was more than love. Character designer Daigo Ikeno stated that for Devil May Cry 2, the team aimed for a contrast between Trish and that game's new heroine, Lucia, making them look as though they were from different races. Hiroyuki Nara was responsible for Trish's inclusion in the game as an unlockable character, and borrowed elements and fight moves from Dante's campaign in the original Devil May Cry for her gameplay in Devil May Cry 2. Nara apologized to the team that created the first game due to the poor quality the sequel had. Nara said multiple designers helped him during the making of Trish's appearance, including in the creation of the flame effects used for her moves. According to Nara, since Devil May Cry 2 had received a negative response from fans and critics, he wanted to redeem himself with the next game, Devil May Cry 3. For this title, he used some of Trish's elements for Dante's new model though he remained ambiguous about which parts. After being absent from Devil May Cry 3, Trish returned as a supporting character in Devil May Cry 4. Writer Bingo Morihashi said he had no clear understanding of Trish and Dante because these two characters were created by Kamiya. Nevertheless, Morihashi included her in the story as a form of fan service. Morihashi stated that if the story had been focused more on Dante rather than the new protagonist Nero, he might have featured Trish more. Character designer Tatsuya Yoshikawa received notes from director Hideaki Itsuno to make Trish "cute"; Yoshikawa had no difficulty designing her. A fellow designer said Trish had become "the series' sexiest gal". In Devil May Cry 4, Trish appears under the alter-ego Gloria; she poses as an enemy to Dante before revealing herself to the player. Producer Hiroyuki Kobayashi stated that Gloria was meant to be a new, sexually appealing character who replaced the previous heroines in the franchise. Capcom staff members were afraid "to make her sexuality vulgar in any way" while noticing her design was popular with the fanbase. Itsuno said Capcom wanted to make Trish unique in terms of gameplay for the Special Edition. Trish was added to Marvel vs. Capcom 3: Fate of Two Worlds because Capcom wanted to have a "sexy character" in the game, according to producer Ryota Niitsuma. Trish's English-language dialog is voiced by Sarah Lafleur in Devil May Cry and Viewtiful Joe: Red Hot Rumble, Danielle Burgio in Devil May Cry 4 and Marvel vs. Capcom 3, and Luci Christian in The Animated Series. In Japanese, she is voiced by Atsuko Tanaka in The Animated Series, Marvel vs. Capcom 3 / Ultimate Marvel vs. Capcom 3, and Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition. Tanaka said she liked the relationship between Trish, Lady, and Dante. Tanaka further liked Trish's portrayal in the anime series due to the action displayed in her fight against Lady. She was satisfied with being included in the anime series. Debuting in Devil May Cry, Trish hires Dante to stop Mundus' plan to conquer the human world by opening a gate on Mallet Island. Later in the game, Dante learns she is Mundus' servant and that her resemblance to his "mother" is part of a plan to lure him to the island so Mundus' servants can kill him. Despite Trish showing her true agenda, Dante saves her life. Deeming her unworthy to serve him, Mundus crucifies Trish to antagonise Dante. During Dante's last fight against the demon lord, Trish reappears and offers her power so he can finish the enemy. The two then become partners. She appears in the Devil May Cry 2 novel as an alternative persona from a parallel world serving Mundus. The original Dante tries to persuade her to ally with demons fighting against the Lord of Darkness, but he eventually has to kill her. She is a playable character in a bonus section of Devil May Cry 2, separate from the game's plot as she is not present in the story. Trish and Lady help Dante stop a new demon invasion in the finale of the anime. Trish reappears in Devil May Cry 4 to work again with Dante. She disguises herself as Gloria, an executive with the Holy Knights of the Order of Sparda—a religious organization that worships Sparda. After the Order's leader Sanctus obtains the power to summon demons from another dimension, Trish goes to protect Fortuna's citizens while Dante goes to dispatch the enemy. In the epilogue, the two receive no payment from Lady, who states Trish made the situation worse. In the updated version of the game, Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition, Trish is once again a playable character. During the game's beginning, Trish, Lady and Dante are defeated by the Demon King Urizen. Trish and Lady are sealed inside the demons Cavaliere and Artemis respectively, with Dante later saving her once defeating it. Trish appears alongside Dante in the PlayStation 2 version of Viewtiful Joe, hitting on him until an enemy captures her. In the PlayStation Portable game Viewtiful Joe: Red Hot Rumble, Trish appears as an alternative skin for the character Sexy Silvia. She is also present in the crossover fighting games Marvel vs. Capcom 3: Fate of Two Worlds and Ultimate Marvel vs. Capcom 3. The character has also appeared in Onimusha Soul. Critical reception of Trish has been positive. Complex called her "charming" and said they liked her abilities, and enthusiastically awaited her appearance in Marvel vs. Capcom 3. Her alter ego Gloria was called one of the most sexually appealing characters in gaming due to her skimpy design. Despite enjoying Trish's redesign and Gloria in Devil May Cry 4, GamesTM was disappointed by her lack of appearances in the sequel and expected to see her as a playable character in an update. Trish and Dante were included in The Inquirers list of the most memorable video-game love teams; comments focused on the way they join forces to defeat their enemies. The Official Xbox Magazine regarded Trish as an interesting sidekick to Dante based on her personality. Siliconera found her techniques "unique", citing the way she adds traps to the arena and their impact on fighting enemies. Despite criticizing several parts from Trish' character in her debut, Bloody Disgusting was surprised by the character's remorse involving how she betrays Dante but ends up joining him later following her fake death. Trish's inclusion in Devil May Cry 4: Special Edition has received a positive response. PCMag stated that her moves seemed to be inspired by her appearances in Devil May Cry 2 and Marvel vs. Capcom 3, describing her as "a very accessible" but difficult-to-master character. Game Informer enjoyed the way Trish can change weapons during fights, saying that while she might be regarded as simple to control, mastering all of her abilities might be so challenging she may be overshadowed by Vergil. IGN liked her moves because of the balance between them and Lady's techniques. Game Revolution liked the Marvel vs. Capcom 3-inspired moves, describing them as appealing. GameSpot offered a less positive opinion, saying Trish might be less entertaining than Lady and Vergil. PushSquare highly praised Trish' new moves, finding them impressive especially when compared with her Devil May Cry 2 persona. Her character has also been explored in the anime series. Voice actor Fumiko Orikasa said the fight between Trish and Lady was appealing. Hyper had mixed thoughts regarding the episode in which Lady meets Trish through a fight but compared the style of the two female characters to the film The Matrix because both of them are stylish. Anime News Network expressed disappointment at the brevity of Trish's appearances in the anime but still found her English voice acting charming. GroundReport described Trish and Lady as foils for Dante and compared them with the main characters from the manga and anime series GetBackers due to Dante's financial issues, often receiving jobs from Lady. The Fandom Post appreciated the episode in which Lady fights Trish because of a misunderstanding but they befriend each other. The writer also enjoyed the duo's role in the following episodes, most notably in the finale. DVD Talk enjoyed the interactions between Trish, Dante, and Lady and wanted to see more of them in the anime rather than stories focused on Dante facing enemies. Category:Demon characters in video games Category:Devil May Cry characters Category:Female characters in video games Category:Fictional characters with electric or magnetic abilities Category:Fictional demon hunters Category:Fictional gunfighters Category:Fictional hunters in video games Category:Video game characters introduced in 2001 Category:Fictional characters with superhuman durability or invulnerability

contested.title: Queen Rania of Jordan
contested.raw_text:
{{POV|date=February 2025}}
{{Short description|Queen of Jordan since 1999}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2023}}
{{Use British English|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox royalty
| name         = Rania
| image        = Queen Rania of Jordan Official Release 05 (3x4 cropped).jpg
| caption      = Rania in 2018
| consort      = yes
| succession   = [[Queen consort of Jordan]]
| reign        = 7 February 1999 – present
| coronation   = 22 March 1999
| cor-type     = Proclamation
| birth_name   = Rania Al-Yassin
| birth_date   = {{Birth date and age|1970|8|31|df=yes}}
| birth_place  = [[Kuwait City]], Kuwait
| spouse       = {{marriage|[[Abdullah II of Jordan|Abdullah II]]|10 June 1993}}
| issue        = {{plainlist|
* [[Hussein, Crown Prince of Jordan|Crown Prince Hussein]]
* [[Princess Iman bint Abdullah|Princess Iman]]
* [[Princess Salma bint Abdullah|Princess Salma]]
* [[Prince Hashem bin Abdullah|Prince Hashem]]}}
| regnal name  = Rania Al Abdullah
| house        = <!--Do not add a royal house or dynasty unless you have an official source. People are born into dynasties of ruling families. Whether or not a person is confirmed to belong to a dynasty by marriage requires a source that proves that fact. -->
| father       = Faisal Al-Yassin
| mother       = Ilham Yassin
| religion     = [[Islam]]
| signature    = Queen Rania of Jordan signature.png
}}

'''Rania Al Abdullah'''<ref>{{cite web|url=https://rhc.jo/en/media/news/statement-royal-hashemite-court-12|title=Statement from the Royal Hashemite Court|work=The Royal Hashemite Court|date=1 June 2023|accessdate=1 June 2023|archive-date=1 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230601173046/https://rhc.jo/en/media/news/statement-royal-hashemite-court-12|url-status=live}}</ref>{{efn|{{langx|ar|رانيا العبد الله|translit=''Rāniyā al-ʻAbd Allāh''}}}} (born '''Rania Al-Yassin''', 31 August 1970) is [[List of Jordanian royal consorts|Queen of Jordan]] as the wife of [[Abdullah II of Jordan|King Abdullah II]]. 

Rania's domestic activities include education, youth, environmental, and health initiatives.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Rania Al-Abdullah |url=https://worldleaders.columbia.edu/directory/rania-al-abdullahhttps://worldleaders.columbia.edu/directory/rania-al-abdullah |access-date=2024-11-28 |website=Columbia University World Leaders Forum }}{{Dead link|date=August 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> Globally, she has campaigned for education and cross-cultural dialogue. She has authored three children's books: ''The Sandwich Swap'', ''The King's Gift'', and ''Eternal Beauty''.

==Early life==
[[File:Queen Rania of Jordan Official Release 02.jpg|thumb|right|Rania in a [[Palestinian traditional costumes|traditional Palestinian dress]], 2017]]
{{Jordanian Royal Family}} 
Rania Al-Yassin was born on 31 August 1970 in Kuwait, to [[Palestinian people|Palestinian]] parents.<ref name=born>{{cite web |title='Dedem Türk'tü Türk kültürüyle büyüdüm'|quote=Aslında dedem Türk'tür. Annemin babası. Bu nedenle Türk müziği dinleyerek büyüdüm. Türk yemekleriyle yetiştim. Bunların hepsi benim geçmişimin bir parçası. Çocukluğumdan beri Türk kültürünün tüm unsurlarıyla iç içe büyüdüm. |trans-quote=Actually my grandfather was Turkish. My mother's father. That's why I grew up listening to Turkish music. I grew up with Turkish food. This is all part of my past. Since my childhood, I grew up with all the elements of Turkish culture.|url=https://www.sabah.com.tr/gundem/2009/12/26/dedem_turktu_turk_kulturuyle_buyudum|website=Sabah |language=Turkish|access-date=4 January 2021|date=26 December 2009|archive-date=9 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210109043617/https://www.sabah.com.tr/gundem/2009/12/26/dedem_turktu_turk_kulturuyle_buyudum|url-status=live}}</ref> Her father, Faisal Al-Yassin, was originally from [[Tulkarm]] in the [[West Bank]], Palestine.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web |url=https://www.biography.com/royalty/queen-rania |title=Queen Rania |website=Biography.com |access-date=15 July 2024 |archive-date=11 August 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200811065157/https://www.biography.com/royalty/queen-rania |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name=born/> Rania attended the [[New English School (Kuwait)|New English School]] in Kuwait and subsequently earned a degree in [[business administration]] from the [[American University in Cairo]]. Following her graduation, she worked briefly in marketing for [[Citibank]], followed by a job with [[Apple Inc.]] in [[Amman]], Jordan.<ref>{{cite news|title=Profile: Jordan's Queen Rania|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/middle_east/1632614.stm|website=BBC|access-date=15 July 2024|date=7 November 2001|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171107032257/http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/middle_east/1632614.stm|archive-date=7 November 2017}}</ref>

==Activities==
Since her marriage, Rania has used her position to advocate for various sectors of society in Jordan and beyond.<ref name=":0">{{cite web|title=Queen Rania launches new, advanced educational online platform|url=https://en.royanews.tv/news/15183/Queen-Rania-launches-new--advanced-educational-online-platform|access-date=18 November 2020|website=Roya News |date=18 September 2018|language=en|archive-date=13 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201113024558/https://en.royanews.tv/news/15183/Queen-Rania-launches-new--advanced-educational-online-platform|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Domestic agenda===

====Education====
[[File:Queenraniacopy8452.jpg|thumb|left|Rania during a dinner celebrating the partnership between the Sesame Workshop and the [[Mosaic Foundation]] in Washington, D.C., US, May 2006]]
Queen Rania has launched and championed several initiatives in education and learning. She has stated that an essential aspect of education is to equip young people with the necessary skills to perform well in the workplace.<ref>{{cite web|title=Queen of Jordan on Economic Progress|url=https://www.cnbc.com/video/2009/07/30/queen-of-jordan-on-economic-progress.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180513231911/https://www.cnbc.com/video/2009/07/30/queen-of-jordan-on-economic-progress.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=13 May 2018|access-date=5 October 2021|website=CNBC |date=30 July 2009}}</ref> She has said that education is a tool for individuals to empower themselves, allows the poor to improve their lives, and is an investment Jordan makes to protect its people from being exploited and falling victim to  extremist ideology.<ref name=":2" />

In July 2005 in partnership with the Ministry of Education, the King and Queen launched an annual teachers' award, the Queen Rania Award for Excellence in Education.<ref name="Queen Rania Teachers Award">{{cite web |url=http://www.queenraniaaward.org |trans-title=Queen Rania Award for Excellence in Education |script-title=ar:جمعية جائزة الملكة رانيا العبدالله للتميز التربوي |language=ar |url-status=usurped |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160123042138/http://www.queenraniaaward.org/ |archive-date=23 January 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www1.queenrania.jo/media/articles/queen-launches-award-honour-school-principals |title=Queen launches award to honor school principals |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110722073206/http://www1.queenrania.jo/media/articles/queen-launches-award-honour-school-principals |archive-date=22 July 2011 |date=16 April 2009 |website=Queen Rania Al Abdullah}}</ref>

The Queen is chairperson of Jordan's first interactive children's museum, which opened in May 2007.<ref name="Opening Children's Museum">{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/king-queen-join-jordanian-children-opening-childrens-museum |title=King, Queen join Jordanian children at opening of children's museum |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107023519/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/king-queen-join-jordanian-children-opening-childrens-museum |archive-date=7 January 2016 |publisher=Jordan Times |date=23 May 2007 |website=queenrania.jo |access-date=15 July 2024 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Children's Museum">{{cite web|url=https://cmj.jo/en/a-letter-from-her-majesty/|title=A Letter from Her Majesty |website=Children's Museum Jordan|access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref> In April 2008, the Queen launched "Madrasati" ("My School"), a public-private initiative aimed at refurbishing 500 of Jordan's public schools over a five-year period.<ref name="Madrasati">{{cite web|url=https://www.madrasati.jo/about-us|title=About Us|website=Madrasati|access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref> Rania also established The Queen Rania Al Abdullah Center for Educational Technology on 6 June 2001, aiming to use modern technology to serve and develop education in Jordan.<ref>{{cite web |title=Who we are |url=https://www.qrf.org/en/who-we-are |website=Queen Rania Foundation |access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref>

The Queen Rania Teacher Academy, which was launched in June 2009, provides professional development programs for current and new teachers in partnership with the Ministry of Education. The Queen Rania Scholarship Program<ref name="Scholarships">{{cite web|first=Craig|last=Mead|url=http://www.queenrania.jo/education/students/scholarships|title=Queen Rania Scholarship Program|publisher=Queen Rania|date=27 May 2010|access-date=20 June 2010|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100102065404/http://www.queenrania.jo/education/students/scholarships|archive-date=2 January 2010}}</ref> partners with several universities from around the world to support scholarships and training for Jordanian students and workers in management, marketing, design, business administration, psychology, engineering, law, and other fields.<ref name="Scholarships" /><ref name="RHAS">{{cite web |url=http://www.rhas.org.jo/ |title=Royal Health Awareness Society |publisher=RHAS |access-date=20 June 2010 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100612210717/http://www.rhas.org.jo/ |archive-date=12 June 2010}}</ref>

====Community and youth empowerment====
[[File:Queen Rania - World Economic Forum on the Middle East Dead Sea Jordan 2007.jpg|thumb|right|Rania at the [[World Economic Forum]] on the Middle East held at the [[Dead Sea, Jordan]], in 2007]]
Queen Rania's first venture was the establishment of the [[Jordan River Foundation]] (JRF) in 1995.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://jordantimes.com/news/local/queen-rania-honours-jordan-river-foundation-supporters|title=Queen Rania honours Jordan River Foundation supporters|date=25 February 2015|work=Jordan Times|access-date=15 July 2024|language=en|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180315003809/http://jordantimes.com/news/local/queen-rania-honours-jordan-river-foundation-supporters|archive-date=15 March 2018}}</ref>

The Jordan River Children Program (JRCP) was developed by Rania to place children's welfare above political agendas and cultural taboos.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/interviews/il-messaggero-newspaper-lucia-pozzi |title=Queen Rania's Interview with Il Messaggero |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107023519/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/interviews/il-messaggero-newspaper-lucia-pozzi |archive-date=7 January 2016 |date=13 May 2008 |access-date=15 July 2024 |website=queenrania.jo}}</ref> This led to the launch, in 1998, of JRF's Child Safety Program, which addresses the immediate needs of children at risk from abuse and initiated a long-term campaign to increase public awareness about violence against children. The deaths of two children in Amman as a result of child abuse in early 2009 led Rania to call for an emergency meeting of government and non-government (including JRF) stakeholders to discuss where the system was failing.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=16383 |title=Queen calls emergency meeting to discuss child abuse cases |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927110546/http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=16383 |archive-date=27 September 2011 |work=Jordan Times |date=24 July 2009}}</ref>

In 2009, to celebrate the 10th anniversary of her husband's accession to the throne, Rania launched a community champion award ([[Ahel Al Himmeh]]) to highlight the accomplishments of groups and individuals who have helped their local communities.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.himmeh.jo/?q=english |title=Ahel Al Himmeh |publisher=Himmeh.jo |access-date=20 June 2010 |url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616062126/http://www.himmeh.jo/?q=english |archive-date=16 June 2010}}</ref> Rania initiated the [[Al-Aman Fund for the Future of Orphans]] in 2003.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.jordanembassyus.org/01222004002.htm |title=Orphans' future security depends on society's commitment, contributions |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120314164456/http://www.jordanembassyus.org/01222004002.htm |archive-date=14 March 2012 |work=Jordan Times |date=22 January 2004}}</ref>

In her capacity as regional ambassador of [[INJAZ Al-Arab]], Rania has taught classes and engaged in dialogue with young people in other countries; she also launched INJAZ Al-Arab's presence elsewhere in the Arab world.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/injaz-kuwait-launch |title=Queen Rania joins in launching Injaz in Kuwait |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107023519/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/injaz-kuwait-launch |archive-date=7 January 2016 |date=24 November 2006 |access-date=15 July 2024 |url-status=live |website=queenrania.jo}}</ref> She chaired a discussion with entrepreneurs in celebration of INJAZ Al-Arab's 10th anniversary, showcasing alumni's success stories<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/press/queen-rania-chairs-discussion-entrepreneurs-injaz-al-arab-celebrating-its-10th-anniversa |title=Queen Rania chairs a discussion with entrepreneurs from INJAZ Al-Arab celebrating its 10th anniversary |date=January 2015 |website=Queen Rania Al Abdullah |access-date=15 July 2024 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150528104912/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/press/queen-rania-chairs-discussion-entrepreneurs-injaz-al-arab-celebrating-its-10th-anniversa |archive-date=28 May 2015}}</ref> At the 2008 World Economic Forum in [[Davos]], she launched the "Empowering One Million Arab Youth by 2018" campaign, which was conceived by INJAZ Arabia.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/empowering-one-million-arab-youth-2018-campaign-launch |title=Queen Rania Launches Campaign to Prepare 1 Million Arab Youth for Workforce |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107023519/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/empowering-one-million-arab-youth-2018-campaign-launch |archive-date=7 January 2016 |date=24 January 2008 |access-date=15 July 2024 |url-status=live |website=queenrania.jo}}</ref>

==== Health ====
In 2005, Rania established the [[Royal Health Awareness Society]] (RHAS) to educate parents and children about the basics of nutrition and hygiene, the benefits of exercise, the harms of smoking, and other areas related to health.<ref>{{cite web|title=About Us|url=https://rhas.org.jo/Contents/About_RHAS.aspx#.X7WIvC2B3RY|access-date=15 July 2024|website=Royal Health Awareness Society|archive-date=25 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201125082731/https://rhas.org.jo/contents/About_RHAS.aspx#.X7WIvC2B3RY|url-status=live}}</ref>

In 2011, the first specialized medical building for children was built in Jordan, Queen Rania Children's Hospital, established to improve the medical service for Jordanian children. The hospital provides for the care of children, especially complex medical cases including organ transplants and endoscopic operations.<ref name=":0" />

===Global agenda===

====Global education====
[[File:Queen Rania - World Economic Forum Annual Meeting Davos 2003.jpg|thumb|right|Speaking at the 2003 [[World Economic Forum]] in [[Davos]], Switzerland]]
In November 2000, in recognition of her commitment to the cause of children and youth, the [[United Nations Children's Fund]] (UNICEF) invited Rania to join its Global Leadership Initiative.<ref name="UNICEF GLI">{{cite web |url=http://www.unwire.org/unwire/20001115/11791_story.asp |title=Queen Rania Joins UNICEF Leadership Initiative |website=U.N. Wire |date=15 November 2000}}{{dead link|date=July 2016 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes}}</ref> In early 2002 Rania joined the board of directors of the International Youth Foundation, based in [[Baltimore]], Maryland, in the United States.<ref name="IYF">{{cite web |url=http://www.iyfnet.org/document.cfm/30/467 |title=HM Queen Rania Al-Abdullah of Jordan Joins International Youth Foundation's Board |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080430223307/http://www.iyfnet.org/document.cfm/30/467|archive-date=30 April 2008 |date=22 March 2002 |website=International Youth Foundation}}</ref> In January 2007, Rania was named UNICEF's first Eminent Advocate for Children.<ref name="UNICEF EAC">{{cite web |url=http://www.unicef.org/media/media_38153.html |title=Queen Rania becomes UNICEF's first Eminent Advocate for Children at the World Economic Forum |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303165255/http://www.unicef.org/media/media_38153.html |archive-date=3 March 2016 |website=UNICEF Press Centre |date=26 January 2007}}</ref> In August 2009, Rania became Honorary Global Chair of the [[United Nations Girls' Education Initiative]] (UNGEI).<ref name="UNGEI">{{cite web |url=http://www.ungei.org/news/index_2181.html |title=Queen Rania designated as Honorary Global Chair of the United Nations Girls' Education Initiative (UNGEI) |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303175515/http://www.ungei.org/news/index_2181.html |archive-date=3 March 2016 |website=UNGEI |date=15 July 2009}}</ref>

[[File:Rania of Jordan at Davos.jpg|right|thumb|Rania at [[Davos]], 2009]]

As a longtime supporter of the Global Campaign for Education (GCE),<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.campaignforeducation.org/ |title=Global Campaign for Education |publisher=Campaignforeducation.org |access-date=20 June 2010 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100706205314/http://www.campaignforeducation.org/ |archive-date=6 July 2010}}</ref> Rania met with children and inspirational women in South Africa, both in the cities of [[Johannesburg]] and [[Soweto]], in March 2009.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.huffingtonpost.com/rania-al-abdullah/a-promising-glimpse-of-af_b_180040.html |title=A Promising Glimpse of Africa's Future Can Be Found in Its Children |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131172058/http://www.huffingtonpost.com/rania-al-abdullah/a-promising-glimpse-of-af_b_180040.html |archive-date=31 January 2016 |website=Huffington Post |date=27 March 2009 |url-status=live |access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref> One of the stories in the book, "Maha of the Mountains", was contributed by Rania.<ref name="Maha">{{cite web |url=http://www.campaignforeducation.org/bigread/stories/queen-rania/index/index.html |title=Maha of the Mountains |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303172430/http://www.campaignforeducation.org/bigread/stories/queen-rania/index/index.html |archive-date=3 March 2016 |website=The Big Read |publisher=The Global Campaign for Education |date=2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.campaignforeducation.org/docs/pressrelease/Media%20Alert%20-%20Queen%20Rania%20annouced%20as%20honorary%20chair%20of%20Global%20Action%20Week.pdf |title=Queen Rania of Jordan, reads her story to children and announces her role as of Action Week |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303180126/http://www.campaignforeducation.org/docs/pressrelease/Media%20Alert%20-%20Queen%20Rania%20annouced%20as%20honorary%20chair%20of%20Global%20Action%20Week.pdf |archive-date=3 March 2016 |publisher=Global Campaign for Education |date=27 March 2009}}</ref>

During her April 2009 US trip, Rania joined leading education advocates Congresswoman [[Nita Lowey]] and Counsellor to the Secretary of the Treasury [[Gene Sperling]] to launch "The Big Read" as part of Global Campaign for Education's global action week calling for quality basic education for all children.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2009/04/21/jordans-queen-rania-congr_n_189690.html |title=Jordan's Queen Rania, Congresswoman Nita Lowey Launch 'The Big Read' Global Education Campaign |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303221227/http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2009/04/21/jordans-queen-rania-congr_n_189690.html |archive-date=3 March 2016 |work=Huffington Post |date=21 April 2009 |url-status=live |access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref> She was also hosted by first lady of the United States, [[Michelle Obama]], during that same trip.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://obamawhitehouse.archives.gov/blog/09/04/23/The-First-Lady-and-Queen-Rania/ |title=The First Lady and Queen Rania |website=White House Blog |date=23 April 2009 |access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref>

[[File:Michelle Obama & Queen Rania of Jordan in the Yellow Oval Room 4-23-09.JPG|left|thumb|US First Lady [[Michelle Obama]] hosts Queen Rania in the [[Yellow Oval Room]], April 2009.]]
On 20 August 2009, Rania co-founded and led the launch of the "[[1GOAL Education for All|1GOAL: Education for All]]" campaign alongside [[Gary Lineker]], and with the help of top international footballers at [[Wembley Stadium]], London.<ref name="1GOAL">{{cite web |url=http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=19393&searchFor=1GOAL |title=Queen lends support to 1GOAL initiative |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927110552/http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=19393&searchFor=1GOAL |archive-date=27 September 2011 |work=Jordan Times |date=21 August 2009}}</ref> On 6 October 2009, Rania was joined by Prime Minister [[Gordon Brown]] of the UK, the president of [[FIFA]], [[Sepp Blatter]], President [[Jacob Zuma]] of South Africa, and other heads of state, for the Global Launch of 1GOAL, which took place across six locations worldwide.<ref name="1GOAL Global Launch">{{cite web |url=http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=20517 |title=Queen joins world leaders in launching 1GOAL campaign |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927110557/http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=20517 |archive-date=27 September 2011 |work=Jordan Times |date=7 October 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.huffingtonpost.com/david-flumenbaum/celebrities-join-youtube_b_129649.html |title=Celebrities Join YouTube at UN to Launch Poverty Campaign |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160212051319/http://www.huffingtonpost.com/david-flumenbaum/celebrities-join-youtube_b_129649.html |archive-date=12 February 2016 |work=Huffington Post |date=26 September 2008 |url-status=live |access-date=15 July 2024 |first=David |last=Flumenbaum}}</ref>

====Cross-cultural dialogue====
[[File:Queen Rania Al Abdullah of Jordan and UK minister Sajid Javid (24182367514).jpg|thumb|right|With [[Sajid Javid]] at the Supporting Syria and the Region conference, 2016]]
Rania has also been particularly vocal about the importance of cross-cultural and interfaith dialogue to foster greater understanding, tolerance and acceptance across the world.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.jordanembassyus.org/09192005001.htm |title=Queen underscores need to promote cross-cultural dialogue |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120301023123/http://www.jordanembassyus.org/09192005001.htm|archive-date=1 March 2012 |website=jordanembassyus.org |publisher=Jordan Times |date=19 September 2005}}</ref> She has used her status to correct what she sees as misconceptions in the West about the [[Arab world]].<ref name="forbes-100">{{cite web |url=https://www.forbes.com/wealth/power-women/list |title=The World's 100 Most Powerful Women |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101008201657/http://www.forbes.com/wealth/power-women/list |archive-date=8 October 2010 |work=[[Forbes]] |date=2010}}</ref>

Rania has played a significant role in reaching out to the global community to foster values of tolerance and acceptance, and increase cross-cultural dialogue. For example, regionally and internationally, Rania has campaigned for a greater understanding between cultures in such high-profile forums as the [[Jeddah Economic Forum]],<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/queen-rania-addresses-jeddah-economic-forum-urges-global-community-plant-seeds-accept |title=Queen Rania addresses Jeddah Economic Forum, urges global community to plant seeds of acceptance, dialogue, peace |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107023519/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/articles/queen-rania-addresses-jeddah-economic-forum-urges-global-community-plant-seeds-accept |archive-date=7 January 2016 |date=25 February 2007 |url-status=live |access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref> [[Harvard Kennedy School]] at [[Harvard University]],<ref name="Harvard">{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/speeches/john-f-kennedy-school-government-harvard-university |title=Queen Rania's Speech at Harvard University's John F. Kennedy School of Government – Boston, USA |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107023519/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/speeches/john-f-kennedy-school-government-harvard-university |archive-date=7 January 2016 |date=4 May 2007 |website=queenrania.jo |access-date=15 July 2024 |url-status=live}}</ref> and the [[Skoll Foundation]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.queenrania.jo/media/speeches/her-majesty-queen-rania-al-abdullah-remarks-skoll-world-forum-2007 |title=Queen Rania Al Abdullah Remarks at Skoll World Forum |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107023519/http://www.queenrania.jo/media/speeches/her-majesty-queen-rania-al-abdullah-remarks-skoll-world-forum-2007 |archive-date=7 January 2016 |date=27 March 2007 |url-status=live |access-date=15 July 2024 |website=queenrania.jo}}</ref> in the UK. She has also made public appearances, including a half-hour television interview on ''[[The Oprah Winfrey Show]]'' on 17 May 2006, where she spoke about misconceptions about Islam and especially [[women in Islam]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.oprah.com/dated/oprahshow/oprahshow_20060517 |title=Meet the World's Youngest Queen |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090609012143/http://www.oprah.com/dated/oprahshow/oprahshow_20060517 |archive-date=9 June 2009 |website=Oprah.com |date=17 May 2006}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=4057220760334335567&ei=lHAJSujEHobw2gKSi5jABg&q=queen+rania&hl=en |title=Queen Rania on Oprah Winfrey, Part 1 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110629070712/http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=4057220760334335567&ei=lHAJSujEHobw2gKSi5jABg&q=queen+rania&hl=en |archive-date=29 June 2011 |date=17 May 2006}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=7397412902320079243&ei=lHAJSujEHobw2gKSi5jABg&q=queen+rania&hl=en |title=Queen Rania on Oprah Winfrey, Part 2 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110629070657/http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=7397412902320079243&ei=lHAJSujEHobw2gKSi5jABg&q=queen+rania&hl=en |archive-date=29 June 2011 |date=17 May 2006}}</ref>

In September 2006, Rania also joined the [[United Nations Foundation]] board of directors.<ref name="UNF">{{cite web |url=http://www.unfoundation.org/press-center/press-releases/2006/her-majesty-queen-rania-joins-unf-board.html |title=Her Majesty Queen Rania Joins Board of United Nations Foundation |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110808030752/http://www.unfoundation.org/press-center/press-releases/2006/her-majesty-queen-rania-joins-unf-board.html |archive-date=8 August 2011 |date=13 September 2006}}</ref> The UN Foundation builds and implements public-private partnerships to address the world's most pressing problems, and broadens support for the UN through advocacy and public outreach.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.unfoundation.org/about-unf/ |title=About UNF |website=United Nations Foundation |date=16 June 2010 |access-date=20 June 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616100144/http://www.unfoundation.org/about-unf/ |archive-date=16 June 2010}}</ref>

====Microfinance====
In September 2003, Rania accepted an invitation to join the board of directors of the [[Foundation for International Community Assistance]] (FINCA).<ref name="FINCA">{{cite web |url=http://www.villagebanking.org/site/c.erKPI2PCIoE/b.2394309/k.2E2B/Press_Wire.htm |title=FINCA'S FINCA International Welcomes Queen Rania Al Abdullah, First Lady of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, to Its Board of Directors |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071109084553/http://www.villagebanking.org/site/c.erKPI2PCIoE/b.2394309/k.2E2B/Press_Wire.htm |archive-date=9 November 2007 |date=15 September 2003}}</ref>

An emissary for the United Nations' [[International Year of Microcredit]] in 2005, Rania's belief in microfinance and her partnership with FINCA<ref name="FINCA" /> has generated more Jordanian micro-businesses, with the official opening of FINCA Jordan in February 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=5963&searchFor=FINCA%20Jordan |title=Queen highlights power of microfinance, tours FINCA Jordan microbusinesses |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081010122146/http://www.jordantimes.com/?news=5963&searchFor=FINCA%20Jordan |archive-date=10 October 2008 |date=26 February 2008}}</ref>

====Environment====
In October 2020, Rania was named as a member of the [[Earthshot Prize]] Council, an initiative of [[Prince William, Duke of Cambridge|Prince William]] to find solutions to environmental issues.<ref>{{cite web|title=Prince William launches ambitious 'Earthshot Prize' to save the planet|url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/prince-william-launches-ambitious-earthshot-prize-with-david-attenborough-to-save-the-planet/|access-date=15 July 2024|website=CBS News|date=8 October 2020|language=en-US|archive-date=20 December 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201220043150/https://www.cbsnews.com/news/prince-william-launches-ambitious-earthshot-prize-with-david-attenborough-to-save-the-planet/|url-status=live |first=Charlie |last=D'Agata}}</ref>

===Social media===
====YouTube====
[[File:DE JORDANIE Rania-24x30-2005(2).jpg|thumb|upright|Rania has a large audience across multiple social media platforms.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Henni |first1=Janine |title=Queen Rania of Jordan Changes Her Profile Pictures to Black as War Continues: 'This Has to Stop' |url=https://people.com/queen-rania-jordan-changes-profile-pictures-black-war-continues-israel-palestine-8363298 |website=people.com |date=October 18, 2023 |access-date=28 October 2023 |archive-date=28 October 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231028212529/https://people.com/queen-rania-jordan-changes-profile-pictures-black-war-continues-israel-palestine-8363298 |url-status=live}}</ref>]]
Rania has used [[YouTube]], stated as a way to promote intercultural dialogue by calling on young people around the world to engage in a global dialogue to dismantle stereotypes of Muslims and the Arab world.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TFf897bUW2Y |title=YOUTUBE EXCLUSIVE: Send me your stereotypes |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161215045604/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TFf897bUW2Y|archive-date=15 December 2016 |publisher=Queen Rania |website=YouTube |date=30 March 2008}}</ref> On 30 March 2008, Rania launched her own YouTube channel, initially to invite viewers to give their opinions of the Middle East and talk about [[stereotypes]] they may have of Arabs and Muslims.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.usatoday.com/tech/world/2008-03-31-queen-rania-youtube_N.htm |title=Queen Rania Launched YouTube Channel |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081104110713/http://www.usatoday.com/tech/world/2008-03-31-queen-rania-youtube_N.htm |archive-date=4 November 2008 |work=USA Today |date=31 March 2008}}</ref> Between 30 March and 12 August ([[International Youth Day]]), Rania posted videos on YouTube in which she asked people to send her their questions about [[Islam]] and the Arab world.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/middle_east/7524933.stm |title=Queen Rania takes on stereotypes |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304222034/http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/middle_east/7524933.stm |archive-date=4 March 2016 |website=BBC |date=25 July 2008 |url-status=live |access-date=15 July 2024}}</ref> She provided responses to those questions and explained her view of the truth about various Arab and Muslim stereotypes. Over five months she posted videos on subjects that included [[honour killing]]s, terrorism and the rights of Arab women.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/worldlatest/story/0,,-7716861,00.html |title=Jordan queen wraps up YouTube plan on stereotypes |work=The Guardian |date=11 August 2008}}{{dead link |date=July 2024 |fix-attempted=yes}}</ref> International personalities such as [[Dean Obeidallah]],<ref>{{cite news |url=http://newsweek.washingtonpost.com/postglobal/vivian_salama/2008/07/queen_comedians_use_youtube_to.html |title=Queen, Comedians Use YouTube To Fight Stereotypes |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304054830/http://newsweek.washingtonpost.com/postglobal/vivian_salama/2008/07/queen_comedians_use_youtube_to.html |archive-date=4 March 2016 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=31 July 2008}}</ref> [[Maz Jobrani]],<ref>{{cite web |url=http://chicago.timeout.com/articles/comedy/71413/maz-jobrani-profile |title=Blowing up: Maz Jobrani pokes dangerous fun at Middle Eastern stereotypes |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101111053313/http://chicago.timeout.com/articles/comedy/71413/maz-jobrani-profile |archive-date=2010-11-11 |website=Time Out, Chicago |date=2008}}</ref> and YouTube star [[Mia Rose]]<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dbJ9fh9hnRY |title=Mia Rose and Hanna Gargour sing "Waiting on the World to Change" |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160427024745/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dbJ9fh9hnRY |archive-date=27 April 2016 |website=YouTube |date=30 June 2008 |url-status=live}}</ref> also contributed videos to the campaign.

In 2008, Rania participated in YouTube's In My Name<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.youtube.com/user/inmyname |title=In My Name |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160915030324/https://www.youtube.com/user/inmyname|archive-date=15 September 2016 |website=YouTube Channel |url-status=live}}</ref> campaign. She appeared alongside [[The Black Eyed Peas]] member [[will.i.am]] in the video, "End Poverty – Be the Generation,"<ref>{{cite web|date=22 September 2008|title=End Poverty – Be the Generation|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pRyMxSS-KI0|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101101012538/http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pRyMxSS-KI0|archive-date=1 November 2010|access-date=20 June 2010|publisher=Youtube.com}}</ref> which urged world leaders to keep the promises they made in 2000 at the United Nations [[Millennium Summit]].

====Twitter====
To coincide with the visit of [[Pope Benedict XVI]] to Jordan on 8 May 2009, Rania joined Twitter with the username @QueenRania.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/scienceandtechnology/technology/twitter/5298148/Queen-of-tweets-Jordans-Rania-announces-Popes-arrival-on-Twitter.html |title=Queen of tweets: Jordan's Rania announces Pope's arrival on Twitter |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090514044258/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/scienceandtechnology/technology/twitter/5298148/Queen-of-tweets-Jordans-Rania-announces-Popes-arrival-on-Twitter.html |archive-date=14 May 2009 |date=8 May 2009}}</ref> She conducted her first Twitter interview, answering questions from the public via her Twitter account at the [[World Economic Forum]] at the [[Dead Sea]] in Jordan in June 2009.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.forumblog.org/blog/2009/05/twitter-interview-with-queen-rania-suggest-your-questions-and-vote.html |title=Twitter interview with Queen Rania |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111218080350/http://www.forumblog.org/blog/2009/05/twitter-interview-with-queen-rania-suggest-your-questions-and-vote.html |archive-date=18 December 2011 |website=World Economic Forum |date=12 May 2009}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Rania met Jordanian Prince [[Abdullah II of Jordan|Abdullah bin Al-Hussein]] at a dinner party in January 1993. On 10 June 1993, they were married at [[Zahran Palace]].<ref name=":1" /> Their wedding ceremony was considered a national holiday. The couple has four children:<ref name=":1">{{cite web|url=http://www.hellomagazine.com/royalty/2013083114363/queen-raina-jordan-43-birthday-facts/|title=Ten facts about Queen Rania of Jordan on her 43rd birthday|work=[[Hello! (magazine)|Hello Magazine]]|date=31 August 2013|access-date=15 July 2024|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924051335/http://www.hellomagazine.com/royalty/2013083114363/queen-raina-jordan-43-birthday-facts/|archive-date=24 September 2015}}</ref>

*[[Hussein, Crown Prince of Jordan|Crown Prince Hussein]] (born 28 June 1994) who married [[Princess Rajwa Al Hussein|Rajwa Al-Saif]] on 1 June 2023 at [[Zahran Palace]] in Amman; they have one daughter:
**[[Princess Iman bint Hussein bin Abdullah|Princess Iman bint Al Hussein]] (born 3 August 2024)
*[[Princess Iman bint Abdullah|Princess Iman]] (born 27 September 1996) who married Jameel Alexander Thermiótis on 12 March 2023 at [[Beit Al Urdun Palace]] in Amman; they have one daughter: 
**Amina Thermiótis (born 16 February 2025)<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.arabnews.com/node/2590432/lifestyle|title=Jordan's Princess Iman welcomes first child|work=[[Arab News]]|date=16 February 2025|access-date=16 February 2025}}</ref>
*[[Princess Salma bint Abdullah|Princess Salma]] (born 26 September 2000)
*[[Prince Hashem bin Abdullah|Prince Hashem]] (born 30 January 2005) 
Abdullah ascended the throne on 7 February 1999, and proclaimed Rania queen on 22 March 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.jordanembassyus.org/032299002.htm |title=King proclaims Rania Queen |website=Jordan Times |date=22 March 1999 |access-date=20 June 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605171654/http://www.jordanembassyus.org/032299002.htm |archive-date=5 June 2011}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.businessinsider.com/the-incredible-life-of-queen-rania-of-jordan-2018-1#in-february-1999-abdullah-ascended-the-throne-and-proclaimed-rania-as-queen-in-march-now-she-travels-the-world-representing-jordan-but-she-certainly-doesnt-stand-in-her-husbands-shadow-5|title=The incredible life of Jordan's Instagram-famous Queen, an ex-Apple employee, human rights activist, and global style icon|work=[[Business Insider]]|access-date=15 July 2024 |date=4 January 2018 |first=Alison |last=Millington|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180315003756/http://www.businessinsider.com/the-incredible-life-of-queen-rania-of-jordan-2018-1#in-february-1999-abdullah-ascended-the-throne-and-proclaimed-rania-as-queen-in-march-now-she-travels-the-world-representing-jordan-but-she-certainly-doesnt-stand-in-her-husbands-shadow-5|archive-date=15 March 2018}}</ref>

Queen Rania is Muslim.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web |last=James |first=Hannah |date=December 4, 2016 |title=Queen Rania of Jordan on religion and the role of women in the Middle East |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/lifestyle/stellar/queen-rania-of-jordan-opens-up-about-faith-family-and-her-hopes-for-women-in-the-middle-east/news-story/474619a41ac952de2672c6b36935bef0 |access-date=14 September 2024 |website=[[The Daily Telegraph]]}} </ref> Describing Islam, Rania stated that "Islam is a religion of peace, tolerance and mercy. It is a source of comfort and strength for more than 1.6 billion Muslims."<ref>{{cite web |title=Queen Rania's Post |url=https://www.facebook.com/QueenRania/posts/pfbid0XKK9BuTLLfkf27Kuv66KLcSMfo3b1oak4rz9XQfTmn8EjSuQqqGwjEZUQMppY4D2l |website=[[Facebook]] |access-date=19 March 2025 |date=11 January 2015}}</ref>

==Publications==
* As a tribute to King Hussein, and on the first anniversary of his death, Rania produced ''The King's Gift'', a children's book about him.<ref>{{cite book |title=Hibat al-malik |trans-title=The King's Gift |date=2000 |publisher=[[Michael O'Mara Books]] |isbn=1854795724}}</ref> Proceeds of the book go to the benefit of underprivileged children across Jordan.
* Her second book, entitled ''Eternal Beauty'', which she wrote in celebration of [[Mother's Day]] 2008 tells the story of a young girl's conversation with a little sheep as she searches for the most beautiful thing in the world. The book was released as part of the Greater Amman Municipality's contest, called "Mama's Story".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.jordanembassyus.org/new/newsarchive/2009/03202009004.htm |title=Ahead of Mother's Day, Queen Rania announces winners of "Mama's Story" competition highlighting importance of reading |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120301023127/http://www.jordanembassyus.org/new/newsarchive/2009/03202009004.htm |archive-date=1 March 2012 |work=Jordan Times |date=20 March 2009}}</ref>
* For the 2009 Big Read event, Rania wrote ''[[Maha of the Mountains]]'', a short story which tells of a young girl's determination to get an education and the challenges she faced.<ref name="Maha" />
* ''[[The Sandwich Swap]]'' is a book inspired by an incident in her childhood. It tells the story of Lily and Salma, two best friends, who argue over the "yucky" taste of their respective [[Peanut butter and jelly sandwich|peanut butter and jelly]] and hummus sandwiches. The girls then overcome and embrace their differences. The book was co-authored by Queen Rania and Kelly DiPucchio.<ref>{{cite book |title=The Sandwich Swap |isbn=978-1423124849 |publisher=[[Hachette Books#Hyperion Books|Hyperion Books]] |date=2010 |oclc=367418549}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.csmonitor.com/Books/Book-Reviews/2010/0427/The-Sandwich-Swap |title=The Sandwich Swap |website=Christian Science Monitor |first=Marjorie |last=Kehe |date=27 April 2010 |access-date=19 July 2024 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100613020010/http://www.csmonitor.com/Books/Book-Reviews/2010/0427/The-Sandwich-Swap |archive-date=13 June 2010}}</ref> In May 2010, the book went to the top of [[The New York Times Best Seller list|the ''New York Times'' Best Seller list]] for children's books.<ref>{{cite news |last=Schuessler |first=Jennifer |url=https://www.nytimes.com/pages/books/bestseller/ |title=Books – Best-Seller Lists |work=[[The New York Times]] |access-date=20 June 2010 |url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100523060724/http://www.nytimes.com/pages/books/bestseller/ |archive-date=23 May 2010}}</ref>

==Affiliations==
Queen Rania campaigned for [[Petra]] to be voted as one of the [[New 7 Wonders of the World]], including welcoming New7Wonders<ref>{{cite web |title=World Tour: Petra, Jordan |url=https://world.new7wonders.com/events/#petra_jordan |website=New 7 Wonders |access-date=19 July 2024}}</ref> to Petra during its world tour.<ref>{{cite news | title= Petra, Jordan – New7Wonders World Tour | url= https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ermYvx-Tbho | date= 14 November 2016 | access-date= 22 January 2022 | archive-date= 22 January 2022 | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20220122125047/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ermYvx-Tbho | url-status= live |website=YouTube}}</ref>

==International roles and positions==
*In November 2000, the [[United Nations Children's Fund]] (UNICEF) invited Rania to join its Global Leadership Initiative.<ref name="UNICEF GLI" />
*At the World Economic Forum in Davos in January 2007, Rania was named UNICEF's first Eminent Advocate for Children.<ref name="UNICEF EAC" />
*In August 2009, Rania was named co-founder and global co-chair of [[1GOAL]].<ref name="1GOAL" />
*In July 2009, the United Nations made Rania Honorary Chairperson for the [[United Nations Girls' Education Initiative]] (UNGEI).<ref name="UNGEI" />
*For their Global Action Week in April 2009, the Global Campaign for Education named Rania their honorary chairperson.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.campaignforeducation.org/docs/pressrelease/Media%20Alert%20-%20Queen%20Rania%20annouced%20as%20honorary%20chair%20of%20Global%20Action%20Week.pdf |title=Queen Rania announced as Honorary Chair of Global Action Week |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303180126/http://www.campaignforeducation.org/docs/pressrelease/Media%20Alert%20-%20Queen%20Rania%20annouced%20as%20honorary%20chair%20of%20Global%20Action%20Week.pdf |archive-date=3 March 2016 |website=Global Campaign for Education |date=April 2009}}</ref>
*In early 2002, Rania joined the board of directors of the [[International Youth Foundation]], based in Baltimore, Maryland, in the United States.<ref name="IYF" />
*In September 2002, Rania became a member of the World Economic Forum (WEF) Foundation Board.<ref name="WEF">{{cite web |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130416034050/http://www.weforum.org/en/KNContributors/index.htm?personid=95350 |title=The World Economic Forum Leadership Team |website=World Economic Forum |url=http://www.weforum.org/content/leadership-team |archive-date=16 April 2013}}</ref> She is also on the Foundation Board of the Forum of Young Global Leaders (YGL) and has been the chairperson for the Nominations and Selection Committee since July 2004, when the forum was established.<ref name="YGL">{{cite web |url=http://www.weforum.org/en/Communities/Young%20Global%20Leaders/Whoweare/YGLFoundationBoard/index.htm |title=Foundation Board for Young Global Leaders|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091208162706/http://www.weforum.org/en/Communities/Young%20Global%20Leaders/Whoweare/YGLFoundationBoard/index.htm |archive-date=8 December 2009 |website=World Economic Forum}}</ref>
*In September 2006, Rania joined the [[United Nations Foundation]] board of directors.<ref name="UNF" />
*Rania was a member of the [[Every Child Council]] for the GAVI Alliance.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://everychild.gavialliance.org/Page.aspx?pid=270 |title=Every Child Council |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110718001123/http://everychild.gavialliance.org/Page.aspx?pid=270 |archive-date=18 July 2011 |website=GAVI}}</ref>
*Rania was an Honorary Member of the International Advisory Council for the [[International Center for Research on Women]] (ICRW).<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.icrw.org/html/about/ICRW-Leadership-Council.html |title=ICRW Leadership Council |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091216062933/http://www.icrw.org/html/about/ICRW-Leadership-Council.html |archive-date=16 December 2009}}</ref>
*Rania is co-chair of the [[Arab Open University]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.aou.edu.jo/page.php?id=224 |website=Arab Open University |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091204202557/http://www.aou.edu.jo/page.php?id=224 |archive-date=4 December 2009 |title=Board of Trustees}}</ref>
*She was Honorary Chairperson of the Jordanian Chapter of [[Operation Smile]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.operationsmile.jo/aboutus/facts/ |title=Quick Facts |website=Operation Smile |access-date=20 June 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100704021207/http://www.operationsmile.jo/aboutus/facts/ |archive-date=4 July 2010}}</ref>
