*As of 2024, she is a member of the board of trustees of the [[World Economic Forum]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Leadership and Governance |website=World Economic Forum |url=https://www.weforum.org/about/leadership-and-governance/ |access-date=19 July 2024}}</ref>

contested.clean_text: {{Infobox royalty | name = Rania | image = Queen Rania of Jordan Official Release 05 (3x4 cropped).jpg | caption = Rania in 2018 | consort = yes | succession = Queen consort of Jordan | reign = 7 February 1999 – present | coronation = 22 March 1999 | cor-type = Proclamation | birth_name = Rania Al-Yassin | birth_date = | birth_place = Kuwait City, Kuwait | spouse = | issue = | regnal name = Rania Al Abdullah | house = | father = Faisal Al-Yassin | mother = Ilham Yassin | religion = Islam | signature = Queen Rania of Jordan signature.png }} Rania Al Abdullah{{efn|}} (born Rania Al-Yassin, 31 August 1970) is Queen of Jordan as the wife of King Abdullah II. Rania's domestic activities include education, youth, environmental, and health initiatives. Globally, she has campaigned for education and cross-cultural dialogue. She has authored three children's books: The Sandwich Swap, The King's Gift, and Eternal Beauty. thumb|right|Rania in a [[Palestinian traditional costumes|traditional Palestinian dress, 2017]] Rania Al-Yassin was born on 31 August 1970 in Kuwait, to Palestinian parents. Her father, Faisal Al-Yassin, was originally from Tulkarm in the West Bank, Palestine. Since her marriage, Rania has used her position to advocate for various sectors of society in Jordan and beyond. thumb|left|Rania during a dinner celebrating the partnership between the Sesame Workshop and the [[Mosaic Foundation in Washington, D.C., US, May 2006]] Queen Rania has launched and championed several initiatives in education and learning. She has stated that an essential aspect of education is to equip young people with the necessary skills to perform well in the workplace. She has said that education is a tool for individuals to empower themselves, allows the poor to improve their lives, and is an investment Jordan makes to protect its people from being exploited and falling victim to extremist ideology. The Queen is chairperson of Jordan's first interactive children's museum, which opened in May 2007. In April 2008, the Queen launched "Madrasati" ("My School"), a public-private initiative aimed at refurbishing 500 of Jordan's public schools over a five-year period. Rania also established The Queen Rania Al Abdullah Center for Educational Technology on 6 June 2001, aiming to use modern technology to serve and develop education in Jordan. The Queen Rania Teacher Academy, which was launched in June 2009, provides professional development programs for current and new teachers in partnership with the Ministry of Education. The Queen Rania Scholarship Program partners with several universities from around the world to support scholarships and training for Jordanian students and workers in management, marketing, design, business administration, psychology, engineering, law, and other fields. thumb|right|Rania at the [[World Economic Forum on the Middle East held at the Dead Sea, Jordan, in 2007]] Queen Rania's first venture was the establishment of the Jordan River Foundation (JRF) in 1995. The Jordan River Children Program (JRCP) was developed by Rania to place children's welfare above political agendas and cultural taboos. This led to the launch, in 1998, of JRF's Child Safety Program, which addresses the immediate needs of children at risk from abuse and initiated a long-term campaign to increase public awareness about violence against children. The deaths of two children in Amman as a result of child abuse in early 2009 led Rania to call for an emergency meeting of government and non-government (including JRF) stakeholders to discuss where the system was failing. In 2009, to celebrate the 10th anniversary of her husband's accession to the throne, Rania launched a community champion award (Ahel Al Himmeh) to highlight the accomplishments of groups and individuals who have helped their local communities. Rania initiated the Al-Aman Fund for the Future of Orphans in 2003. In her capacity as regional ambassador of INJAZ Al-Arab, Rania has taught classes and engaged in dialogue with young people in other countries; she also launched INJAZ Al-Arab's presence elsewhere in the Arab world. She chaired a discussion with entrepreneurs in celebration of INJAZ Al-Arab's 10th anniversary, showcasing alumni's success stories At the 2008 World Economic Forum in Davos, she launched the "Empowering One Million Arab Youth by 2018" campaign, which was conceived by INJAZ Arabia. In 2005, Rania established the Royal Health Awareness Society (RHAS) to educate parents and children about the basics of nutrition and hygiene, the benefits of exercise, the harms of smoking, and other areas related to health. In 2011, the first specialized medical building for children was built in Jordan, Queen Rania Children's Hospital, established to improve the medical service for Jordanian children. The hospital provides for the care of children, especially complex medical cases including organ transplants and endoscopic operations. In early 2002 Rania joined the board of directors of the International Youth Foundation, based in Baltimore, Maryland, in the United States. In January 2007, Rania was named UNICEF's first Eminent Advocate for Children. In August 2009, Rania became Honorary Global Chair of the United Nations Girls' Education Initiative (UNGEI). right|thumb|Rania at [[Davos, 2009]] As a longtime supporter of the Global Campaign for Education (GCE), Rania met with children and inspirational women in South Africa, both in the cities of Johannesburg and Soweto, in March 2009. One of the stories in the book, "Maha of the Mountains", was contributed by Rania. During her April 2009 US trip, Rania joined leading education advocates Congresswoman Nita Lowey and Counsellor to the Secretary of the Treasury Gene Sperling to launch "The Big Read" as part of Global Campaign for Education's global action week calling for quality basic education for all children. She was also hosted by first lady of the United States, Michelle Obama, during that same trip. left|thumb|US First Lady [[Michelle Obama hosts Queen Rania in the Yellow Oval Room, April 2009.]] On 20 August 2009, Rania co-founded and led the launch of the "1GOAL: Education for All" campaign alongside Gary Lineker, and with the help of top international footballers at Wembley Stadium, London. On 6 October 2009, Rania was joined by Prime Minister Gordon Brown of the UK, the president of FIFA, Sepp Blatter, President Jacob Zuma of South Africa, and other heads of state, for the Global Launch of 1GOAL, which took place across six locations worldwide. thumb|right|With [[Sajid Javid at the Supporting Syria and the Region conference, 2016]] Rania has also been particularly vocal about the importance of cross-cultural and interfaith dialogue to foster greater understanding, tolerance and acceptance across the world. She has used her status to correct what she sees as misconceptions in the West about the Arab world. Rania has played a significant role in reaching out to the global community to foster values of tolerance and acceptance, and increase cross-cultural dialogue. For example, regionally and internationally, Rania has campaigned for a greater understanding between cultures in such high-profile forums as the Jeddah Economic Forum, Harvard Kennedy School at Harvard University, and the Skoll Foundation in the UK. She has also made public appearances, including a half-hour television interview on The Oprah Winfrey Show on 17 May 2006, where she spoke about misconceptions about Islam and especially women in Islam. In September 2006, Rania also joined the United Nations Foundation board of directors. The UN Foundation builds and implements public-private partnerships to address the world's most pressing problems, and broadens support for the UN through advocacy and public outreach. In September 2003, Rania accepted an invitation to join the board of directors of the Foundation for International Community Assistance (FINCA). An emissary for the United Nations' International Year of Microcredit in 2005, Rania's belief in microfinance and her partnership with FINCA In October 2020, Rania was named as a member of the Earthshot Prize Council, an initiative of Prince William to find solutions to environmental issues. thumb|upright|Rania has a large audience across multiple social media platforms. Rania has used YouTube, stated as a way to promote intercultural dialogue by calling on young people around the world to engage in a global dialogue to dismantle stereotypes of Muslims and the Arab world. On 30 March 2008, Rania launched her own YouTube channel, initially to invite viewers to give their opinions of the Middle East and talk about stereotypes they may have of Arabs and Muslims. Between 30 March and 12 August (International Youth Day), Rania posted videos on YouTube in which she asked people to send her their questions about Islam and the Arab world. She provided responses to those questions and explained her view of the truth about various Arab and Muslim stereotypes. Over five months she posted videos on subjects that included honour killings, terrorism and the rights of Arab women. International personalities such as Dean Obeidallah, Maz Jobrani, and YouTube star Mia Rose also contributed videos to the campaign. In 2008, Rania participated in YouTube's In My Name campaign. She appeared alongside The Black Eyed Peas member will.i.am in the video, "End Poverty – Be the Generation," which urged world leaders to keep the promises they made in 2000 at the United Nations Millennium Summit. To coincide with the visit of Pope Benedict XVI to Jordan on 8 May 2009, Rania joined Twitter with the username @QueenRania. She conducted her first Twitter interview, answering questions from the public via her Twitter account at the World Economic Forum at the Dead Sea in Jordan in June 2009. Rania met Jordanian Prince Abdullah bin Al-Hussein at a dinner party in January 1993. On 10 June 1993, they were married at Zahran Palace. *Crown Prince Hussein (born 28 June 1994) who married Rajwa Al-Saif on 1 June 2023 at Zahran Palace in Amman; they have one daughter: **Princess Iman bint Al Hussein (born 3 August 2024) *Princess Iman (born 27 September 1996) who married Jameel Alexander Thermiótis on 12 March 2023 at Beit Al Urdun Palace in Amman; they have one daughter: **Amina Thermiótis (born 16 February 2025) *Princess Salma (born 26 September 2000) *Prince Hashem (born 30 January 2005) Abdullah ascended the throne on 7 February 1999, and proclaimed Rania queen on 22 March 1999. Queen Rania is Muslim. Describing Islam, Rania stated that "Islam is a religion of peace, tolerance and mercy. It is a source of comfort and strength for more than 1.6 billion Muslims." * As a tribute to King Hussein, and on the first anniversary of his death, Rania produced The King's Gift, a children's book about him. Proceeds of the book go to the benefit of underprivileged children across Jordan. * Her second book, entitled Eternal Beauty, which she wrote in celebration of Mother's Day 2008 tells the story of a young girl's conversation with a little sheep as she searches for the most beautiful thing in the world. The book was released as part of the Greater Amman Municipality's contest, called "Mama's Story". * For the 2009 Big Read event, Rania wrote Maha of the Mountains, a short story which tells of a young girl's determination to get an education and the challenges she faced. In May 2010, the book went to the top of the New York Times Best Seller list for children's books. Queen Rania campaigned for Petra to be voted as one of the New 7 Wonders of the World, including welcoming New7Wonders to Petra during its world tour. *In November 2000, the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) invited Rania to join its Global Leadership Initiative. *In early 2002, Rania joined the board of directors of the International Youth Foundation, based in Baltimore, Maryland, in the United States. She is also on the Foundation Board of the Forum of Young Global Leaders (YGL) and has been the chairperson for the Nominations and Selection Committee since July 2004, when the forum was established. *In September 2006, Rania joined the United Nations Foundation board of directors. *Rania was an Honorary Member of the International Advisory Council for the International Center for Research on Women (ICRW). *Rania is co-chair of the Arab Open University. *She was Honorary Chairperson of the Jordanian Chapter of Operation Smile. *As of 2024, she is a member of the board of trustees of the World Economic Forum. *: ** 55px Knight Grand Cordon with Collar of the Order of Al-Hussein bin Ali. (9 June 1999) ** 55px Knight Grand Cordon, Special Class of the Supreme Order of the Renaissance (6 March 2024). *: Dame Grand Cross of the Order of Leopold I (18 May 2016). *: DK of The Most Esteemed Royal Family Order of Laila Utama (13 May 2008). *: Grand Cross, Special Class of the Order of Merit of the Federal Republic of Germany (21 October 2002). *: Grand Cross of the Order of Merit of the Italian Republic (19 October 2009). *: Dame Grand Cordon of the Order of the Precious Crown (4 December 1999). *: 1st Class of the Order of the Montenegrin Grand Star (23 July 2015). *: Dame Grand Cross of the Order of the Lion of the Netherlands (30 October 2006). *: Dame Grand Cross of the Order of Saint Olav (2 March 2000). *: ** Grand Cross of the Order of Saint James of the Sword (16 March 2009). ** Grand Cross of the Order of Infante Henry (5 March 2008). ** Dame Grand Cross of the Royal Order of Isabella the Catholic (18 October 1999). *: Member Grand Cross of the Order of the Seraphim (7 October 2003) *2001: Life Achievement Award of International Osteoporosis Foundation, Italy *2002: Ambrogino D'Oro Award from the Municipality of Milan, Italy *2002: Gold Medal of the President of the Italian Republic from Pio Manzù International Research Centre, Italy *2003: German Media Award from , Germany *2005: Golden Plate Award, Academy of Achievement, USA *2005: Sesame Workshop Award from Sesame Workshop, USA *2007: Mediterranean Prize for Social Solidarity from the Mediterranean Foundation in Italy *2007: Global Humanitarian Action Award from UNSA-USA and the Business Council of the UN, USA *2007: Bambi Award for Attention Based Charity by Hubert Burda Media, Germany *2007: John Wallach Humanitarian Award and PeaceMaker Award from the non-profit Seeds of Peace, USA *2008: World Savers Award from Conde Nast Traveler, USA *2008: David Rockefeller Bridging Leadership Award from Synergos University, USA *2008: First ever YouTube Visionary Award *2009: The Marisa Bellisario International Award from the Fondazione Bellisario, Italy *2009: North-South Prize by the North-South Prize, Portugal *2009: FIFA Presidential Award, Switzerland *2010: Arab Knight of Giving Award from Arab Giving Forum, UAE *2010: The Leadership Award from White Ribbon Alliance for Safe Motherhood, USA *2010: James C. stable.title: Black Country, New Road
stable.raw_text:
{{Short description|English rock band}}
{{good article}}
{{About||the English major road|Black Country New Road}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=February 2023}}

'''Black Country, New Road''' (commonly abbreviated to '''BC,NR''' or '''BCNR''') are an English rock band formed in [[Cambridge]] in 2018. Originally founded by Tyler Hyde (vocals, bass), Lewis Evans (vocals, flute, saxophone), [[Georgia Ellery]] (violin, backing vocals), May Kershaw (vocals, keyboards), Charlie Wayne (drums, backing vocals) and [[Isaac Wood (musician)|Isaac Wood]] (guitar, lead vocals), they added their then-seventh member, guitarist Luke Mark, in 2019.

The band gained initial attention through singles "Athens, France" and "[[Sunglasses (Black Country, New Road song)|Sunglasses]]" in 2019. Their debut album ''[[For the First Time (Black Country, New Road album)|For the First Time]]'', released in 2021, received widespread critical acclaim, was nominated for the [[Mercury Prize]] and reached No. 4 on the [[UK Albums Chart]]. Four days before the release of their 2022 second album ''[[Ants from Up There]]'', Wood left the band, citing mental health struggles. The album received further critical acclaim and commercial success, debuting at No. 3 on the UK Albums Chart.

Following the departure of their lead vocalist Isaac Wood, the band immediately began work on new material, with Hyde, Ellery and Kershaw sharing lead vocal duties. After touring throughout 2022, the band released the [[concert film]] ''[[Live at Bush Hall]]'', in February 2023, featuring several new compositions and songs that had been written since Wood's departure. In 2025, the band announced their third studio album ''[[Forever Howlong]]'', which was released in April 2025.

==History==
===2018–2021: Early career and ''For the First Time''===
{{Main|For the First Time (Black Country, New Road album)}}

In 2014, Tyler Hyde, Lewis Evans, Georgia Ellery, May Kershaw, Charlie Wayne, and Isaac Wood formed the [[East Cambridgeshire]]-based band Nervous Conditions, along with frontman Connor Browne and drummer Jonny Pyke.<ref name=":10" /> The group became known for their intense live performances and an eclectic sound.<ref name=":10" /> The band had recorded a full-length album in 2017, provisionally titled ''Zak's Anniversary;'' however, the album was subsequently shelved and remains officially unreleased.<ref>{{Cite web |last=González |first=Jesús |date=2021-08-23 |title=Black Country, New Road: Cuando el jazz, math rock y post-punk se unen para crear tensión |url=https://www.sopitas.com/musica/black-country-new-road-disco-for-the-first-time-nominacion-mercury-prize-2021/ |access-date=2025-06-06 |website=Sopitas.com |language=es}}</ref> Following multiple allegations of [[sexual assault]] against Browne, Nervous Conditions split in early 2018.<ref>{{Cite web |date=30 January 2018 |title=Nervous Conditions split following sexual assault allegations against frontman |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/news/latest-news/nervous-conditions-split-following-sexual-assault-allegations-against-front |access-date=2025-05-18 |website=[[The Line of Best Fit]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Clarke |first=Patrick |date=2018-01-30 |title=Nervous Conditions Frontman Accused Of Sexual Assault |url=https://thequietus.com/news/nervous-conditions-sexual-assault-allegations-statement/ |access-date=2025-05-18 |website=[[The Quietus]] |language=en-GB}}</ref> Browne issued a public apology, and the remaining members—unaware of the accusations at the time—soon formed a new project.<ref name=":10" /> 

The group, without Browne and Pyke, reconvened soon after as Black Country, New Road in 2018.<ref name=":10" /> Formed by graduates of art and music schools,<ref name=":11">{{Cite web |last=Balmont |first=James |date=2020-12-15 |title=Class of 2021: Black Country, New Road |url=https://diymag.com/interview/class-of-2021-black-country-new-road-interview |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230201193147/https://diymag.com/interview/class-of-2021-black-country-new-road-interview |archive-date=2023-02-01 |access-date=2025-06-11 |website=[[DIY (magazine)|DIY]] |language=en}}</ref> the six-piece was named after [[Black Country New Road|the eponymous road]] in the [[West Midlands (county)|West Midlands]] in England,<ref name=":1">{{Cite news |last=Power |first=Ed |date=22 January 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road's tale of triumph unfolds in the shadow of tragedy |url=https://www.irishtimes.com/culture/music/black-country-new-road-s-tale-of-triumph-unfolds-in-the-shadow-of-tragedy-1.4778528 |access-date=5 February 2022 |newspaper=The Irish Times |language=en}}</ref> which Wood suggested after using Wikipedia's "random article" button.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Black Country, New Road + O. |url=https://www.ica.art/live/black-country-new-road |access-date=2023-03-04 |publisher=[[Institute of Contemporary Arts]] |quote=Black Country, New Road found their name on a random Wikipedia generator}}</ref> They performed several live shows at the [[The Windmill, Brixton|Brixton Windmill]] venue and gained a small level of notoriety within the [[South London]] music scene.<ref name="paste">{{cite web |last1=Freedman |first1=Max |date=5 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road's For the first time Isn't Their First Rodeo |url=https://www.pastemagazine.com/music/black-country-new-road/for-the-first-time-album-review/ |access-date=8 February 2021 |website=[[Paste (magazine)|Paste]]}}</ref> According to drummer Charlie Wayne, the band first performed with [[Black Midi]] in October 2017, before Black Country, New Road had officially formed.<ref name="Smith-2021">{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Maddy |date=16 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road are poised and ready to stomp on your face |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/features/longread/black-country-new-road-poised-ready-stomp-on-your-face-interview |access-date=2025-05-18 |website=The Line of Best Fit |language=en-US}}</ref> Although the new band shared much of Nervous Conditions' personnel, their musical direction was distinct from previous efforts.<ref name=":10" />
[[File:Black Country, New Road (49387468462).jpg|alt=Black Country, New Road performing live on stage in January 2020 under colourful lighting, with all seven band members playing instruments like guitars, drums, a violin, a saxophone and a keyboard.|thumb|Black Country, New Road performing in January 2020]]Their debut single, "Athens, France", was released on 18 January 2019 through the independent label Speedy Wunderground.<ref>{{cite web |last=Murray |first=Robin |date=18 January 2019 |title=Listen: Black Country, New Road - 'Athens, France' |url=https://www.clashmusic.com/news/listen-black-country-new-road-athens-france |accessdate=18 November 2020 |website=[[Clash (magazine)|Clash]]}}</ref> In early 2019, Luke Mark joined Black Country, New Road as a second guitarist, expanding the band to a seven-piece lineup.<ref name=":10" /> This was followed with "[[Sunglasses (Black Country, New Road song)|Sunglasses]]", the band's second single, released on 26 July 2019.<ref name=":8" /> Both releases garnered attention from critics, for experimenting and blending of a variety of sounds,<ref name="cos">{{cite web |last1=Kaye |first1=Ben |date=28 October 2020 |title=Black Country, New Road Announce Debut Album For the first time, Share "Science Fair": Stream |url=https://consequence.net/2020/10/black-country-new-road-announce-debut-album-for-the-first-time-share-science-fair-stream/ |access-date=2 February 2021 |website=[[Consequence of Sound]]}}</ref><ref name="nmeinterview">{{cite web |last1=Neale |first1=Matthew |date=1 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road: sax and violins from Britain's most prestigious new band |url=https://www.nme.com/blogs/nme-radar/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time-interview-2868057 |access-date=2 February 2021 |website=[[NME]]}}</ref> and developed a strong [[cult following]],<ref name=":11" /> with physical copies reportedly being resold for over {{Currency|100|GBP}}.<ref name="exclaim">{{cite web |last1=McQuaid |first1=Charlie |date=2 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road Live Up to the Hype on 'For the first time' |url=https://exclaim.ca/music/article/black_country_new_road_for_the_first_time_album_review |access-date=2 February 2021 |website=[[Exclaim!]]}}</ref> Peter Helman of ''[[Stereogum]]'' noted the wide variety of genre influences on the track, describing it as a "wild ride",<ref name=":8">{{cite web |last=Helman |first=Peter |date=25 July 2019 |title=Black Country, New Road – "Sunglasses" |url=https://www.stereogum.com/2052485/black-country-new-road-sunglasses/music/ |accessdate=18 December 2020 |website=[[Stereogum]]}}</ref> John Doran of ''[[The Quietus]]'' described the band, following the release of "Sunglasses", as "the best in the entire world".<ref name=":10">{{Cite web |last=Doran |first=John |date=4 December 2019 |title=Making Good Their Escape: Black Country New Road Interviewed |url=https://thequietus.com/articles/27546-black-country-new-road-interview |access-date=1 February 2022 |website=[[The Quietus]] |language=en-us}}</ref>

On October 2020, Black Country, New Road signed to [[Ninja Tune]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kenneally |first=Cerys |date=28 October 2020 |title=Black Country, New Road sign to Ninja Tune and announce debut album |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/news/latest-news/black-country-new-road-sign-to-ninja-tune-and-announce-debut-album |access-date=2025-05-18 |website=The Line of Best Fit |language=en-US}}</ref> Later that month, on the 28th, the band announced their debut album, ''[[For the First Time (Black Country, New Road album)|For the First Time]]'', which was released on 5 February 2021 through Ninja Tune,<ref name="nme1">{{cite web |last1=Skinner |first1=Tom |date=28 October 2020 |title=Black Country, New Road announce debut album 'For The First Time' |url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/black-country-new-road-announce-debut-album-for-the-first-time-2802393 |access-date=2 February 2021 |website=[[NME]]}}</ref> alongside the release of its [[lead single]], "Science Fair".<ref name="cos" /> On 11 January 2021, "Track X" was released as the second single from the album.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Russell |first1=Scott |date=11 January 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road Share New Single, "Track X" |url=https://www.pastemagazine.com/music/black-country-new-road/black-country-new-road-new-single-track-x/ |access-date=2 February 2021 |website=[[Paste (magazine)|Paste]]}}</ref>  The album was produced by Andy Savours and recorded in March 2020 during the early stages of [[COVID-19 lockdown in the United Kingdom|the first UK COVID-19 lockdown]].<ref name="pitchfork">{{cite web |last1=Monroe |first1=Jazz |date=9 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road - For the first time |url=https://pitchfork.com/reviews/albums/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time/ |access-date=9 February 2021 |website=[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]]}}</ref> Following the album's release, it was met with critical acclaim<ref>{{cite web |title=For the First Time by Black Country, New Road |url=https://www.metacritic.com/music/for-the-first-time/black-country-new-road |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210303162208/https://www.metacritic.com/music/for-the-first-time/black-country-new-road |archive-date=3 March 2021 |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=[[Metacritic]] |language=en}}</ref> and charted at number four on the [[UK Albums Chart]], and it became their first number-one release on the [[UK Independent Albums Chart]].<ref name="UK-Albums2">{{cite web |title=Black Country, New Road {{!}} full Official Chart History |url=https://www.officialcharts.com/artist/59502/black-country-new-road/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250523151409/https://www.officialcharts.com/artist/59502/black-country-new-road/ |archive-date=23 May 2025 |accessdate=23 May 2025 |publisher=[[Official Charts Company]]}}</ref> Their growing popularity led to bookings at major music festivals such as [[Glastonbury Festival|Glastonbury]], [[Primavera Sound]], and [[South by Southwest]] in 2020, although these appearances were cancelled due to the COVID-19 pandemic.<ref name="exclaim" />

===2021–2022: ''Ants from Up There'' and Isaac Wood's departure===
{{Main|Ants from Up There}}

On 12 October 2021, the band announced their second album ''[[Ants from Up There]]'' and released its lead single "Chaos Space Marine".<ref name=":4">{{cite web|author=Minsker, Evan|date=12 October 2021|title=Black Country, New Road Announce New Album, Share New Song|url=https://pitchfork.com/news/black-country-new-road-announce-new-album-share-new-song-listen/|work=[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]]|accessdate=12 October 2021}}</ref> Four of the album's songs were released as singles: "Chaos Space Marine", "Bread Song" on 2 November 2021,<ref name="pfbs">{{cite web |last=Minsker |first=Evan |date=2 November 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road Share New "Bread Song": Listen |url=https://pitchfork.com/news/black-country-new-road-share-new-bread-song-listen/ |access-date=15 November 2021 |work=[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]]}}</ref> "Concorde" on 30 November 2021<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-11-30 |title=Black Country, New Road - "Concorde" |url=https://www.stereogum.com/2168942/black-country-new-road-concorde/music/ |access-date=2021-12-11 |website=Stereogum |language=en}}</ref> and "Snow Globes" on 19 January 2022.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Qureshi |first1=Arusa |date=19 January 2022 |title=Listen to Black Country, New Road's arresting new single 'Snow Globes' |url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/listen-to-black-country-new-roads-arresting-new-single-snow-globes-3141341 |access-date=20 May 2025 |work=[[NME]]}}</ref> On 3 November 2021, Black Country, New Road cancelled their upcoming European tour due to a band member's illness.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Black Country, New Road cancel European tour after band member falls ill|url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/news/latest-news/black-country-new-road-cancel-european-tour-after-band-member-falls-ill|access-date=4 February 2022|website=The Line of Best Fit|language=en}}</ref> The band released the ''Never Again'' EP on 3 December, a [[Rough Trade Records]] exclusive limited to 1,500 copies which features covers of songs by [[MGMT]], [[ABBA]] and [[Adele]].<ref name="neveragainLOBF">{{cite web|last1=Kenneally|first1=Cerys|date=16 November 2021|title=Black Country, New Road releasing covers of ABBA, Adele and more on vinyl|url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/news/latest-news/black-country-new-road-releasing-covers-of-abba-adele-and-more-on-vinyl|access-date=17 November 2021|website=The Line of Best Fit}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Watch Black Country, New Road cover MGMT's "Time To Pretend"|url=https://www.thefader.com/2021/02/08/black-country-new-road-mgmt-fated-to-pretend|access-date=31 January 2022|website=The FADER|language=en}}</ref>

On 31 January 2022, lead vocalist and guitarist Isaac Wood announced his sudden departure from the band four days before the release of ''Ants From Up There'' via social media, citing struggles with his mental health.<ref name="Pareles" /> This resulted in the cancelling of the band's planned first US tour.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Pearis|first=Bill|title=Black Country, New Road singer Isaac Wood leaves band; tour canceled|url=https://www.brooklynvegan.com/black-country-new-road-singer-isaac-wood-leaves-band-tour-canceled/|access-date=4 February 2022|website=BrooklynVegan|language=en}}</ref> Black Country, New Road announced that the remaining six members had already begun to work on new music without Wood, as they were aware of Wood's planned exit long before it was announced.<ref name="IsaacDeparturePitchfork">{{cite web|last=Strauss|first=Matthew|date=31 January 2022|title=Black Country, New Road Frontman Isaac Wood Departs Band Days Before New Album Release|url=https://pitchfork.com/news/black-country-new-road-frontman-isaac-wood-departs-band-days-before-new-album-release/|access-date=1 February 2022|work=[[The Guardian]]}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=King |first=Pat |date=3 February 2022 |title=What Lies Ahead for Black Country, New Road? |url=https://www.pastemagazine.com/music/black-country-new-road/interview-ants-from-up-there/ |access-date=4 February 2022 |website=Paste |language=en}}</ref> Out of respect for Wood, the band has chosen not to perform material from their first two albums live, but has expressed openness to his potential return in the future if he wishes to rejoin.<ref name=":0" /> Prior to Wood's departure, Hyde noted that the band's next release might not take the form of a studio album: "I know it's not going to be an album in its normal form. It would be cool to work with an orchestra; it would be cool to do a film score. These are just some of the ideas we're bouncing around at the moment".<ref name="UncutPrintInterview">{{cite magazine|last=Foster |first=Rosie |date= March 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road: Q&A |magazine= [[Uncut (magazine)|Uncut]] }}</ref> The idea of other band members taking on vocal duties had already been discussed. According to Hyde, the change "wasn't totally new".<ref name=":2">{{Cite web |last=Griffin |first=Louis |date=2025-05-06 |title=Black Country, New Road: Whole New World |url=https://diymag.com/interview/black-country-new-road-forever-howlong |access-date=2025-06-12 |website=[[DIY (magazine)|DIY]] |language=en}}</ref> Since then, vocal duties within the band were shared among Hyde, Evans, Ellery, and Kershaw.<ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" />

''Ants from Up There'' was released on 4 February 2022 to universal critical acclaim.<ref name="Metacritic">{{Cite web |title=Ants from up There by Black Country, New Road |url=https://www.metacritic.com/music/ants-from-up-there/black-country-new-road |access-date=4 February 2022 |website=[[Metacritic]]}}</ref> It also debuted at number three on the UK Albums Chart, the band's highest placement yet.<ref name="UK-Albums2" /> The band was scheduled to begin a tour in support of the album in February, but the dates were cancelled following Wood's departure.<ref name="nmeannounce">{{cite news |last1=Richards |first1=Will |date=12 October 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road announce second album 'Ants From Up There' and share 'Chaos Space Marine' |url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/black-country-new-road-announce-second-album-ants-from-up-there-and-share-chaos-space-marine-3068425 |access-date=20 May 2025 |work=[[NME]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Richards |first1=Will |date=31 January 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road vocalist Isaac Wood announces departure from the band |url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/black-country-new-road-vocalist-isaac-wood-announces-departure-from-the-band-3150451 |access-date=20 May 2025 |work=[[NME]]}}</ref> A new tour, without Wood, commenced in May and ran through September.<ref name="nmea2">{{cite news |last1=Richards |first1=Will |date=6 April 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road announce tour with new line-up to "road-test" new material |url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/black-country-new-road-announce-tour-with-new-line-up-to-road-test-new-material-3199328 |access-date=20 May 2025 |work=[[NME]]}}</ref> During this period, the band once again road-tested new material.<ref name="nmea2" /> A deluxe edition of the album was released on 10 June, featuring songs recorded live at the [[Queen Elizabeth Hall]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Kenneally |first1=Cerys |date=9 June 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road announce deluxe edition of Ants From Up There |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/news/latest-news/black-country-new-road-announce-deluxe-edition-of-ants-from-up-there |access-date=20 May 2025 |work=[[The Line of Best Fit]]}}</ref>

=== 2022–2024: Transition ===
{{Main|Live at Bush Hall}}

[[File:Black Country, New Road in June 2023.jpg|thumb|Black Country, New Road performing at Glastonbury Festival in June 2023]]With Wood no longer a member, the band scheduled and announced a European tour in summer 2022, as well as an American tour supporting Black Midi.<ref name="nmea2" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=7 June 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road Announce U.S. Tour Dates |url=https://pitchfork.com/news/black-country-new-road-announce-us-tour-dates/ |access-date=21 July 2022 |website=Pitchfork |language=en-US}}</ref> The band performed new, unreleased songs throughout their 2022 concerts, which Lewis Evans said was written specifically to give the band material to tour with and may not appear on the upcoming third album.<ref>{{Cite web |last=MacMillan |first=Jamie |date=16 December 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road's latest chapter proves an exciting prospect at London's Bush Hall |url=https://readdork.com/live/black-country-new-road-15-december-2022-bush-hall/ |access-date=26 December 2022 |website=[[Dork (magazine)|Dork]] |language=en-US}}</ref> On 14 November 2022, the band released a second EP of covers titled ''Never Again Pt. 2'', to celebrate the band's placement on Rough Trade's album of the year list. The EP was limited to 1,500 copies and features covers of songs by [[Regina Spektor]], [[Caroline Polachek]], [[The Magnetic Fields]], and [[Billie Eilish]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=15 November 2022 |title=The Smile, Mitski, Jack White, Rina Sawayama, and More Releasing Rough Trade Exclusive Vinyl Records |url=https://pitchfork.com/news/the-smile-mitski-jack-white-rina-sawayama-releasing-rough-trade-exclusive-vinyl-records/ |access-date=17 November 2022 |website=Pitchfork |language=en-US}}</ref>

On 14 February 2023, the band posted a video on social media containing a teaser for something "coming soon" for release on 20 February.<ref name=":6">{{cite web |url=https://exclaim.ca/music/article/black_country_new_road_are_teasing_something |title=Black Country, New Road Are Teasing Something |first=Sydney |last=Brasil |date=15 February 2023 |website=[[Exclaim!]] |accessdate=16 February 2023}}</ref> It was then announced to be ''[[Live at Bush Hall]]'', a recording of their performances at [[Bush Hall]], London.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bloom |first=Madison |date=2023-02-20 |title=Black Country, New Road Share New Concert Film |url=https://pitchfork.com/news/black-country-new-road-share-new-concert-film-watch/ |access-date=2025-06-06 |website=[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]] |language=en-US}}</ref> The video was recorded over two days<ref>{{Cite web |last=Tibbits |first=Ben |date=2025-04-22 |title=Wonderland Meets: Black Country, New Road |url=https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2025/04/22/wonderland-meets-black-country-new-road/ |access-date=2025-06-06 |website=Wonderland |language=en-GB}}</ref> and was composed entirely of unreleased material that the band had debuted at concerts in 2022, and marks the band's first release of new music since Wood's departure.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Watch: Black Country, New Road - 'Live at Bush Hall' |url=http://pilerats.com/music/bands/watch-black-country-new-road-live-at-bush-hall/ |access-date=2023-02-21 |website=PILERATS}}</ref> An album of the performance was released by the band on 24 March 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kress |first=Bryan |date=2023-03-21 |title=Black Country, New Road announce new live album Live at Bush Hall |url=https://consequence.net/2023/03/black-country-new-road-live-at-bush-hall/ |access-date=2023-03-21 |website=Consequence |language=en-US}}</ref> On 26 September 2023, it was announced that the band had signed a global publishing deal with [[Transgressive Records|Transgressive Publishing]]. Alongside this, it was revealed that the band were working on a follow-up release to ''Ants from Up There'' while touring.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Black Country, New Road sign with Transgressive Publishing |url=https://www.musicweek.com/publishing/read/black-country-new-road-sign-with-transgressive-publishing/088563 |access-date=2024-07-11 |website=[[Music Week]] |language=en}}</ref>

=== 2025–present: ''Forever Howlong'' ===
{{Main|Forever Howlong}}

On 13 January 2025, the band released a 19-second teaser which included a new logo onto various social media platforms. On 22 January, the band announced that a new single titled "Besties" was set to be released on 30 January.<ref name=":7">{{Cite web |title=Black Country, New Road Are Teasing Something │ Exclaim! |url=https://exclaim.ca/music/article/black-country-new-road-are-teasing-something |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=Black Country, New Road Are Teasing Something │ Exclaim! |language=en}}</ref> The band later announced the release of their third album ''[[Forever Howlong]]'', due to be released on 4 April 2025, accompanied by a North American and European tour.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Monroe |first1=Jazz |title=Black Country, New Road Announce Tour and New Album Forever Howlong, Share New Song |url=https://pitchfork.com/news/black-country-new-road-announce-new-album-forever-howlong-share-video-for-new-song-watch/ |access-date=30 January 2025 |work=[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]] |date=30 January 2025}}</ref> The second single from the album, titled "Happy Birthday", was released on 3 March, alongside an accompanying video directed by Lesley-Anne Rose.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-03-03 |title=Black Country, New Road Unveil New Song "Happy Birthday": Stream |url=https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/black-country-road-unveil-song-181113815.html |access-date=2025-03-04 |website=Yahoo Entertainment |language=en-US}}</ref> Their third single from the album, titled "For the Cold Country" was released on 26 March.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Green |first=Walden |date=2025-03-26 |title=Listen to Black Country, New Road's New Song "For the Cold Country" |url=https://pitchfork.com/news/listen-to-black-country-new-road-new-song-for-the-cold-country/ |access-date=2025-03-30 |website=Pitchfork |language=en-US}}</ref>

''Forever Howlong'' was released on multiple formats including on [[Phonograph record|2xLP]], [[Compact disc|CD]] and [[Compact cassette|cassette]],<ref name="fhvariants">{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Damian |date=30 January 2025 |title=Black Country, New Road share single 'Besties' and announce new album 'Forever Howlong' |url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/black-country-new-road-share-jubilant-single-besties-and-announce-new-album-forever-howlong-along-with-2025-uk-and-european-tour-3833203 |access-date=20 March 2025 |work=[[NME]]}}</ref> with various collector's editions offering alternate artwork and tracklists.<ref name="fhvariants" /><ref>{{cite news |last1=Ewing |first1=Jerry |date=30 January 2025 |title="This song was love at first harpsichord!" Black Country, New Road return with video for brand new single Besties |url=https://www.loudersound.com/news/this-song-was-love-at-first-harpsichord-black-country-new-road-return-with-video-for-brand-new-single-besties |access-date=20 March 2025 |work=[[Classic Rock (magazine)|Louder]] |language=en}}</ref> To promote the album, the band embarked on an international tour beginning on 7 April 2025 in [[Stockport]],<ref>{{cite news |last1=Pappis |first1=Konstantinos |date=3 March 2025 |title=Black Country, New Road Drop New Single 'Happy Birthday' |url=https://ourculturemag.com/2025/03/03/black-country-new-road-drop-new-single-happy-birthday/ |access-date=8 April 2025 |work=[[Our Culture Mag|Our Culture]]}}</ref> with performances scheduled through 31 October.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Darville |first1=Jordan |date=30 January 2025 |title=Black Country, New Road announce Forever Howlong, 2025 tour |url=https://www.thefader.com/2025/01/30/black-country-new-road-new-album-forever-howlong-besties-2025-tour-dates |access-date=8 April 2025 |work=[[The Fader]] |language=en}}</ref> ''Forever Howlong'' was released to critical acclaim.<ref>{{cite web |title=Forever Howlong by Black Country, New Road |url=https://www.metacritic.com/music/forever-howlong/black-country-new-road |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=[[Metacritic]] |language=en}}</ref> It debuted at number three on the UK Albums Chart and number one on the UK Independent Albums Chart.<ref name="UK-Albums2" /> The album also charted internationally, reaching number 16 in Germany,<ref name="Germany">{{cite web |title=Offiziellecharts.de – Black Country, New Road – Forever Howlong |url=https://www.offiziellecharts.de/details/album-details-549321 |url-status= |archive-url= |archive-date= |access-date=6 June 2025 |publisher=GFK Entertainment Charts |language=German}}</ref> 22 in Australia,<ref name=":05">{{cite web |title=Discography Black Country, New Road |url=https://australian-charts.com/showinterpret.asp?interpret=Black+Country,+New+Road |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250523151154/https://australian-charts.com/showinterpret.asp?interpret=Black+Country%2C+New+Road |archive-date=23 May 2025 |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=australian-charts.com}}</ref> and 135 on the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]], marking their first appearance on that chart.<ref name="BillBoardTAS">{{cite magazine |title=Top Album Sales: Week of April 19, 2025 |url=https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/2025-04-19/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250415142018/https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/2025-04-19/ |archive-date=15 April 2025 |access-date=16 April 2023 |magazine=[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]}}</ref>

== Artistry ==
Black Country, New Road's musical style has been described as [[experimental rock]],<ref name="bpm">{{cite web |last1=Kohner |first1=Matt |date=5 February 2021 |title=Album Review: Black Country, New Road – For the First Time |url=https://beatsperminute.com/album-review-black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time/ |access-date=8 February 2021 |website=[[Beats Per Minute (website)|Beats Per Minute]]}}</ref><ref name="NME2">{{cite web |last1=Cartledge |first1=Luke |date=4 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road – 'For The First Time' review: an utterly mesmerising debut |url=https://www.nme.com/reviews/album/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time-review-radar-2871737 |access-date=5 February 2021 |website=[[NME]]}}</ref><ref name="Dempsey-2021">{{Cite web |last=Dempsey |first=Colin |date=2021-02-18 |title=Black Country, New Road: For the first time |url=https://spectrumculture.com/2021/02/17/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time-review/ |access-date=2025-05-18 |website=Spectrum Culture |language=en-US}}</ref> [[post-punk]]<ref name="stereogum">{{cite web |last1=Leas |first1=Ryan |date=2 February 2021 |title=Album Of The Week: Black Country, New Road For The First Time |url=https://www.stereogum.com/2114531/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time-review/reviews/album-of-the-week/ |access-date=2 February 2021 |website=[[Stereogum]]}}</ref><ref name=":3" /> and [[post-rock]].<ref name="stereogum" /><ref name="uncut">{{cite magazine |last=Richards |first=Sam |date=4 February 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road – Ants from Up There |url=https://www.uncut.co.uk/reviews/album/black-country-new-road-ants-from-up-there-136872/ |access-date=6 June 2025 |magazine=[[Uncut (magazine)|Uncut]]}}</ref> Their music is characterised by a fusion of diverse genres and compositional approaches.<ref name="Jenkins-2021">{{Cite web |last=Jenkins |first=Dafydd |date=29 January 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road - For The First Time |url=https://www.loudandquiet.com/reviews/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time/ |access-date=2025-05-18 |website=[[Loud and Quiet]] |language=en-US}}</ref> While rooted in the dissonant intensity and angular rhythms of post-punk, the band incorporates a wide array of influences that set them apart from more conventional rock acts.<ref name="Pareles">{{Cite news |last=Pareles |first=Jon |date=7 February 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road's Perilous Triumph |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2022/02/07/arts/music/black-country-new-road-ants-from-up-there-review.html |access-date=6 June 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}</ref> Their early sound was frequently compared to 1990s experimental rock groups such as [[Slint]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Empire |first=Kitty |date=2021-02-07 |title=Black Country, New Road: For the First Time review – one of the best albums of 2021 |url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2021/feb/07/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time-review-debut |access-date=2025-06-07 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> It has also been described as "genre-scrambling"<ref name="Pareles" /> and "deconstructed rock canvas".<ref name="popmattersafut">{{Cite web |last=Morgan |first=Tom |date=4 February 2022 |title='Ants From Up There' Sees Black Country, New Road Thrillingly Solidify Their Unique Vision Reviews |url=https://www.popmatters.com/black-country-new-road-ants |access-date=4 February 2022 |work=[[PopMatters]]}}</ref> 

Black Country, New Road have also incorporated stylistic elements from [[klezmer]], British [[Folk music|folk]], [[progressive rock]], and [[minimal music]].<ref name="Pareles" /> Early in their career, the band were described as a centerpiece of a new scene of British rock bands rooted in experimental post-punk with ''[[Sprechgesang]]'' – or "talk-singing" – vocals, alongside contemporaries Black Midi, [[Squid (band)|Squid]], [[Dry Cleaning (band)|Dry Cleaning]], [[Shame (band)|Shame]], [[Yard Act]] and Irish band [[Fontaines D.C.]]<ref name="Perpetua">{{Cite news |last=Perpetua |first=Matthew |date=6 May 2021 |title=The Post-Brexit New Wave |url=https://www.npr.org/2021/05/06/993931617/new-wave-post-punk-brexit-squid-dry-cleaning-black-country-new-road |access-date=4 February 2022 |work=NPR |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Schonfeld|first=Zach|date=13 October 2021|title=The Eternal Cool of Talk Singing|url=https://www.theringer.com/music/2021/10/13/22723195/talk-singing-dry-cleaning-rock-black-country-new-roads|access-date=5 February 2022|website=The Ringer|language=en}}</ref> They are considered to be part of the emerging wave of [[British rock music]] known as the [[Windmill scene]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Breuer-Weil |first=Asher |date=28 November 2020 |title=Tiña on the fertility of Brixton's local music scene {{!}} Interview |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/features/interviews/how-brixton-windmill-scene-produced-speedy-underground-tina |access-date=2025-06-06 |website=The Line of Best Fit |language=en-US}}</ref>

The band's debut album ''For the First Time'' is rooted in [[experimental rock]], [[post-punk]], and [[post-rock]].<ref name="Dempsey-2021" /> Their instrumentation also draws from American composer [[Steve Reich]] and jazz musician [[Ornette Coleman]].<ref name=":1" /> Wood's vocals were compared to Slint frontman Brian McMahan and [[The Fall (band)|The Fall]] lead singer [[Mark E. Smith]];<ref name="nme.com">{{Cite web|date=3 February 2022|title=Black Country, New Road – 'Ants From Up There' review: majestic, romantic indie-rock|url=https://www.nme.com/reviews/album/black-country-new-road-ants-from-up-there-review-3149019|access-date=5 February 2022|website=NME|language=en-GB}}</ref> his lyrics were influenced by American singer-songwriter [[Father John Misty]], with Wood declaring "he's not the best lyricist in the world, but what he is is entirely, entirely honest".<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mystery men, stage fright and Jewish party music: an interview with…|url=https://theface.com/music/black-country-new-road-for-the-first-time-ninja-tune-london-post-punk-jazz|access-date=7 February 2022|website=The Face|date=7 January 2021 |language=en-gb}}</ref> The band often references other musicians in their songs, such as being associated to "the world's second-best [[Slint]] tribute act" in the song "Science Fair".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Hunter-Tilney |first=Ludovic |date=5 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road forge their own path with For the First Time |url=https://www.ft.com/content/1df8e719-f348-422e-bb94-c05002b2580c |access-date=5 February 2022 |work=Financial Times}}</ref> "Track X" references their company with Black Midi.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 May 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road - "Track X (The Guest)" |url=https://www.stereogum.com/2148092/black-country-new-road-track-x-the-guest/music/ |access-date=5 February 2022 |website=Stereogum |language=en}}</ref> They mention [[Kanye West]] in their song "Sunglasses" with the line "leave Kanye out of this".<ref>{{cite web |last1=Empire |first1=Kitty |date=25 January 2020 |title=Black Country, New Road review – truly a sound less travelled |url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2020/jan/25/black-country-new-road-yes-manchester-live-review-sunglasses |access-date=5 May 2022 |website=The Guardian |publisher=Guardian News & Media Limited}}</ref> "Athens, France" contains lyrics from [[Phoebe Bridgers]]' "[[Motion Sickness (Phoebe Bridgers song)|Motion Sickness]]";<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 February 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road are one of the most exciting and original British bands in years |url=https://www.newstatesman.com/culture/music-theatre/2021/02/black-country-new-road-are-one-most-exciting-and-original-british |access-date=11 February 2022 |website=New Statesman |language=en-US}}</ref> the original single also references [[Ariana Grande]]'s "[[Thank U, Next (song)|Thank U, Next]]".<ref>{{Cite web |title=Black Country, New Road unleash sardonic, mutating debut track "Athen's, France" |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/new-music/song-of-the-day/black-country-new-road-athens-france |access-date=6 February 2022 |website=The Line of Best Fit |language=en}}</ref> The band has also been noted for their unconventional merchandising choices and [[Music video|music videos]] incorporating surreal stock imagery.<ref name="exclaim" />

Their second album ''Ants From Up There'' was written to feature a more accessible sound.<ref name="nme.com" /> It has been described as post-rock, [[chamber pop]]<ref name="TLOBF review">{{Cite web |last=Kohner |first=Kyle |date=3 February 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road push against the fate of oblivion on the excellent Ants From Up Here |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/reviews/albums/black-country-new-road-ants-from-up-there-album-review |access-date=4 February 2022 |website=[[The Line of Best Fit]]}}</ref> and [[indie rock]].<ref name="nme2">{{Cite web |last=Richards |first=Will |date=3 February 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road – 'Ants From Up There' review: majestic, romantic indie-rock |url=https://www.nme.com/en_au/reviews/album/black-country-new-road-ants-from-up-there-review-3153404 |access-date=3 February 2022 |work=[[NME]]}}</ref> The band became "obsessed" with Canadian indie rock band [[Arcade Fire]] during the United Kingdom's [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom|COVID-19 lockdown]]; several critics have compared songs such as "Chaos Space Marine" and "The Place Where He Inserted the Blade" to Arcade Fire.<ref name=":03">{{Cite web |last=Hill |first=Eric |date=1 February 2022 |title=Black Country, New Road Still Soar Above Their Peers on 'Ants from Up There' |url=https://exclaim.ca/music/article/black_country_new_road_ants_from_up_there_album_review |access-date=2022-02-05 |website=Exclaim! |language=en-ca}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Black Country, New Road on love, aliens, and the perfect pop of Ants From Up There|url=https://www.thefader.com/2022/02/04/black-country-new-road-on-love-aliens-and-perfect-pop|access-date=5 February 2022|website=The FADER|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=21 January 2022|title=Black Country, New Road: 'We are really, really deep in debt. We need some hits'|url=https://inews.co.uk/culture/music/black-country-new-road-ants-from-up-there-interview-new-album-1412967|access-date=5 February 2022|website=inews.co.uk|language=en}}</ref> Black Country, New Road also recalled listening to [[Kurt Vile]] and [[Frank Ocean]] for inspiration during the writing process.<ref name=":5">{{Citation|title=Ants From Up There by Black Country, New Road|date=4 February 2022|url=https://music.apple.com/us/album/ants-from-up-there/1586070259|language=en-US|access-date=5 February 2022}}. Apple Music liner notes.</ref> With their third album, following the departure of Wood, ''Forever Howlong'' has signaled a stylistic shift away from their first two albums, with the band going out of their way to avoid being "melodramatic" and aiming to be more "subtle" within their songwriting.<ref name=":3" /> Prior to Wood's departure, vocal performances across ''Forever Howlong'' were taken up by Hyde, Ellery, and Kershaw.<ref name=":3" /> The album, in a [[press release]], has been described as [[Folk music|folk]], [[progressive rock]], [[baroque pop]] and [[alternative rock]], with comparisons to artists such as [[Joanna Newsom]], [[Randy Newman]], and [[Fiona Apple]].<ref name="fhvariants" /> The band also recounted that they avoided utilising instrumentation that they felt was typical for them during the recording process, with Mark stating "there's a lot of different kinds of songs on the album that I don't think we could've done before, but we can now".<ref name=":3">{{Cite web |last=Cohen |first=Ian |date=2025-01-30 |title=Black Country, New Road Head Into the Unknown |url=https://pitchfork.com/features/interview/black-country-new-road-album-forever-howlong/ |access-date=2025-02-07 |website=Pitchfork |language=en-US}}</ref>

==Other projects==
[[File:Jockstrap 10 23 23 Terminal West 2 (cropped).jpg|thumb|[[Jockstrap (band)|Jockstrap]] performing in 2023.]]
Beyond their own work, the band regularly performed live with fellow London experimental rock group [[Black Midi]], touring collectively as Black Midi, New Road. [[Geordie Greep]], the guitarist and vocalist of Black Midi, said the following: "If there's another band who are really good in the same city, who you also really get on with, there's no reason not to get together and do something proper. It's just making sure you don't do it for the sake of it and you have a solid reason for doing it".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://thequietus.com/articles/29818-black-midi-cavalcade-interview|title=The Road To The Best Ever: Black Midi Interviewed|date=7 April 2021|accessdate=11 April 2021|work=The Quietus|author=Daniel Dylan Wray}}</ref> In June 2021, the group also worked with London musician Ethan P. Flynn, releasing a live version of his song "Television Show".<ref>{{Cite web |last=DeVille |first=Chris |date=24 June 2021 |title=Ethan P. Flynn - "Television Show (Live)" (Feat. Black Country, New Road) |url=https://www.stereogum.com/2152187/ethan-p-flynn-television-show-live-feat-black-country-new-road/music/ |access-date=5 February 2022 |website=Stereogum |language=en}}</ref>

Violinist Georgia Ellery is one half of the experimental electronic duo [[Jockstrap (band)|Jockstrap]] with Taylor Skye, who have released two EPs and an album.<ref>{{cite web |last=Cliff |first=Aimee |date=2 April 2020 |title=Meet Jockstrap, an Uncanny Electro-Pop Duo From the Retro Future |url=https://pitchfork.com/features/rising/jockstrap-interview |accessdate=18 December 2020 |website=Pitchfork}}</ref> She also collaborated with [[Jamie xx]] on a [[BBC Radio 3]] session on 30 May 2020.<ref>{{cite web|date=30 May 2020|title=Listen to Jamie xx and Jockstrap's Georgia Ellery's new quarantine collaboration|url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/listen-to-jamie-xx-and-jockstraps-georgia-ellerys-new-quarantine-collaboration-2679008|website=nme.com|accessdate=18 December 2020}}</ref> She made her acting debut in 2019's ''[[Bait (2019 film)|Bait]]'',<ref name=":9">{{Cite news |last=Doran |first=John |date=2022-09-13 |title='He wore tartan pyjamas to the laundry!' Electro mashup masters Jockstrap come clean |url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2022/sep/13/tartan-pyjamas-laundry-mashup-masters-jockstrap |access-date=2025-06-06 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> which won a [[British Academy Film Awards|British Academy Film award]] for [[BAFTA Award for Outstanding Debut by a British Writer, Director or Producer|Outstanding Debut by a British Writer, Director or Producer]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ritman |first=Alex |date=6 January 2020 |title='Joker' Leads BAFTA 2020 Nominations |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/bafta-awards-2020-nominations-unveiled-1267114 |accessdate=7 January 2020 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]}}</ref> Ellery has performed and recorded as a member of conservatory folk trio Contours and Happy Beigel Klezmer Orkester, a six-piece Klezmer band formed during studies at [[Guildhall School of Music and Drama]].<ref name=":9" />

Bassist Tyler Hyde is the daughter of [[Underworld (band)|Underworld]] founder [[Karl Hyde]].<ref name="nmeinterview" /> She released her first music outside of Black Country, New Road under the alias Tyler Cryde, performing a duet with boyfriend Skydaddy on the single "Tear Gas".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kelly |first=Tyler Damara |date=10 January 2024 |title=Skydaddy and Tyler Cryde (Black Country, New Road) duet on new single, "Tear Gas" |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/news/skydaddy-and-tyler-cryde-black-country-new-road-duet-on-new-single-tear-gas |access-date=2025-06-07 |website=The Line of Best Fit |language=en-US}}</ref> Drummer Charlie Wayne was also a drummer of another British band, [[Ugly (UK band)|Ugly]].<ref>{{cite web |date=20 August 2019 |title=Charlie Wayne's band "Ugly" |url=https://www.gigwise.com/features/3355801/session-ugly-breeze-through-the-distillery-with-king-of-swings |accessdate=13 January 2021 |website=gigwise.com}}</ref> Saxophonist Lewis Evans has made a self-titled album under the alias Good with Parents and featured on the [[TRAAMS]] single "The Greyhound".<ref>{{cite web |date=11 August 2020 |title=TRAAMS return with their first new music in nearly five years. |url=https://www.readdork.com/news/traams-return-with-their-first-new-music-in-nearly-five-years-the-greyhound |accessdate=13 January 2021 |website=[[Dork (magazine)|Dork]]}}</ref> Evans also contributed saxophone to [[Squid (band)|Squid]]'s debut album ''[[Bright Green Field]]'', released on 7 May 2021.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jenner |first=Alice |date=30 April 2021 |title=Embodying our ever-changing world, Squid's Bright Green Field is a perfect blend of calm and chaos |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/reviews/albums/squid-bright-green-field-album-review |access-date=2025-06-07 |website=The Line of Best Fit |language=en-US}}</ref> Former vocalist Isaac Wood has occasionally released music under his solo moniker The Guest, including an acoustic version of "Track X" called "Track X (The Guest)", performed solo by Wood and predating the formation of Black Country, New Road.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kenneally |first=Cerys |date=18 May 2021 |title=Black Country, New Road release alternate version of "Track X" |url=https://www.thelineofbestfit.com/news/latest-news/black-country-new-road-release-alternate-version-of-track-x |access-date=2025-06-07 |website=The Line of Best Fit |language=en-US}}</ref>

stable.clean_text: {{Infobox musical artist | name = Black Country, New Road | image = Black_Country,_New_Road_at_O2_Shepherd's_Bush_Empire.png | caption = Black Country, New Road in 2023. From left to right: Kershaw, Ellery, Wayne, Mark, Hyde, Evans | image_size = | alt = The six members of the band standing in a line on-stage, smiling with their arms on each other's shoulders. | landscape = Yes | alias = | origin = Cambridge, England | genre = | works = Discography | years_active = 2018–present | label = | spinoff_of = | spinoffs = Black Midi, New Road | website = | current_members = * * Tyler Hyde * Lewis Evans * Georgia Ellery * May Kershaw * Charlie Wayne * Luke Mark | past_members = }} Black Country, New Road (commonly abbreviated to BC,NR or BCNR) are an English rock band formed in Cambridge in 2018. Originally founded by Tyler Hyde (vocals, bass), Lewis Evans (vocals, flute, saxophone), Georgia Ellery (violin, backing vocals), May Kershaw (vocals, keyboards), Charlie Wayne (drums, backing vocals) and Isaac Wood (guitar, lead vocals), they added their then-seventh member, guitarist Luke Mark, in 2019. The band gained initial attention through singles "Athens, France" and "Sunglasses" in 2019. Their debut album For the First Time, released in 2021, received widespread critical acclaim, was nominated for the Mercury Prize and reached No. 4 on the UK Albums Chart. Four days before the release of their 2022 second album Ants from Up There, Wood left the band, citing mental health struggles. The album received further critical acclaim and commercial success, debuting at No. 3 on the UK Albums Chart. Following the departure of their lead vocalist Isaac Wood, the band immediately began work on new material, with Hyde, Ellery and Kershaw sharing lead vocal duties. After touring throughout 2022, the band released the concert film Live at Bush Hall, in February 2023, featuring several new compositions and songs that had been written since Wood's departure. In 2025, the band announced their third studio album Forever Howlong, which was released in April 2025. In 2014, Tyler Hyde, Lewis Evans, Georgia Ellery, May Kershaw, Charlie Wayne, and Isaac Wood formed the East Cambridgeshire-based band Nervous Conditions, along with frontman Connor Browne and drummer Jonny Pyke. Following multiple allegations of sexual assault against Browne, Nervous Conditions split in early 2018. Browne issued a public apology, and the remaining members—unaware of the accusations at the time—soon formed a new project. the six-piece was named after the eponymous road in the West Midlands in England, which Wood suggested after using Wikipedia's "random article" button. They performed several live shows at the Brixton Windmill venue and gained a small level of notoriety within the South London music scene. According to drummer Charlie Wayne, the band first performed with Black Midi in October 2017, before Black Country, New Road had officially formed. Although the new band shared much of Nervous Conditions' personnel, their musical direction was distinct from previous efforts. In early 2019, Luke Mark joined Black Country, New Road as a second guitarist, expanding the band to a seven-piece lineup. and developed a strong cult following, Peter Helman of Stereogum noted the wide variety of genre influences on the track, describing it as a "wild ride", John Doran of The Quietus described the band, following the release of "Sunglasses", as "the best in the entire world". On October 2020, Black Country, New Road signed to Ninja Tune. Later that month, on the 28th, the band announced their debut album, For the First Time, which was released on 5 February 2021 through Ninja Tune, alongside the release of its lead single, "Science Fair". The album was produced by Andy Savours and recorded in March 2020 during the early stages of the first UK COVID-19 lockdown. Following the album's release, it was met with critical acclaim and charted at number four on the UK Albums Chart, and it became their first number-one release on the UK Independent Albums Chart. Their growing popularity led to bookings at major music festivals such as Glastonbury, Primavera Sound, and South by Southwest in 2020, although these appearances were cancelled due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Four of the album's songs were released as singles: "Chaos Space Marine", "Bread Song" on 2 November 2021, "Concorde" on 30 November 2021 and "Snow Globes" on 19 January 2022. On 3 November 2021, Black Country, New Road cancelled their upcoming European tour due to a band member's illness. The band released the Never Again EP on 3 December, a Rough Trade Records exclusive limited to 1,500 copies which features covers of songs by MGMT, ABBA and Adele. On 31 January 2022, lead vocalist and guitarist Isaac Wood announced his sudden departure from the band four days before the release of Ants From Up There via social media, citing struggles with his mental health. Black Country, New Road announced that the remaining six members had already begun to work on new music without Wood, as they were aware of Wood's planned exit long before it was announced. Out of respect for Wood, the band has chosen not to perform material from their first two albums live, but has expressed openness to his potential return in the future if he wishes to rejoin. The idea of other band members taking on vocal duties had already been discussed. According to Hyde, the change "wasn't totally new". Since then, vocal duties within the band were shared among Hyde, Evans, Ellery, and Kershaw. It also debuted at number three on the UK Albums Chart, the band's highest placement yet. A new tour, without Wood, commenced in May and ran through September. During this period, the band once again road-tested new material. thumb|Black Country, New Road performing at Glastonbury Festival in June 2023With Wood no longer a member, the band scheduled and announced a European tour in summer 2022, as well as an American tour supporting Black Midi. The band performed new, unreleased songs throughout their 2022 concerts, which Lewis Evans said was written specifically to give the band material to tour with and may not appear on the upcoming third album. On 14 November 2022, the band released a second EP of covers titled Never Again Pt. 2, to celebrate the band's placement on Rough Trade's album of the year list. The EP was limited to 1,500 copies and features covers of songs by Regina Spektor, Caroline Polachek, The Magnetic Fields, and Billie Eilish. On 14 February 2023, the band posted a video on social media containing a teaser for something "coming soon" for release on 20 February. It was then announced to be Live at Bush Hall, a recording of their performances at Bush Hall, London. The video was recorded over two days and was composed entirely of unreleased material that the band had debuted at concerts in 2022, and marks the band's first release of new music since Wood's departure. An album of the performance was released by the band on 24 March 2023. On 26 September 2023, it was announced that the band had signed a global publishing deal with Transgressive Publishing. Alongside this, it was revealed that the band were working on a follow-up release to Ants from Up There while touring. On 13 January 2025, the band released a 19-second teaser which included a new logo onto various social media platforms. On 22 January, the band announced that a new single titled "Besties" was set to be released on 30 January. The band later announced the release of their third album Forever Howlong, due to be released on 4 April 2025, accompanied by a North American and European tour. The second single from the album, titled "Happy Birthday", was released on 3 March, alongside an accompanying video directed by Lesley-Anne Rose. Their third single from the album, titled "For the Cold Country" was released on 26 March. Forever Howlong was released on multiple formats including on 2xLP, CD and cassette, with various collector's editions offering alternate artwork and tracklists. To promote the album, the band embarked on an international tour beginning on 7 April 2025 in Stockport, with performances scheduled through 31 October. Forever Howlong was released to critical acclaim. It debuted at number three on the UK Albums Chart and number one on the UK Independent Albums Chart. 22 in Australia, and 135 on the US Billboard 200, marking their first appearance on that chart. Black Country, New Road's musical style has been described as experimental rock, post-punk Their music is characterised by a fusion of diverse genres and compositional approaches. While rooted in the dissonant intensity and angular rhythms of post-punk, the band incorporates a wide array of influences that set them apart from more conventional rock acts. Their early sound was frequently compared to 1990s experimental rock groups such as Slint. It has also been described as "genre-scrambling" Black Country, New Road have also incorporated stylistic elements from klezmer, British folk, progressive rock, and minimal music. They are considered to be part of the emerging wave of British rock music known as the Windmill scene. The band's debut album For the First Time is rooted in experimental rock, post-punk, and post-rock. his lyrics were influenced by American singer-songwriter Father John Misty, with Wood declaring "he's not the best lyricist in the world, but what he is is entirely, entirely honest". The band often references other musicians in their songs, such as being associated to "the world's second-best Slint tribute act" in the song "Science Fair". "Track X" references their company with Black Midi. They mention Kanye West in their song "Sunglasses" with the line "leave Kanye out of this". "Athens, France" contains lyrics from Phoebe Bridgers' "Motion Sickness"; the original single also references Ariana Grande's "Thank U, Next". The band has also been noted for their unconventional merchandising choices and music videos incorporating surreal stock imagery. and indie rock. The band became "obsessed" with Canadian indie rock band Arcade Fire during the United Kingdom's COVID-19 lockdown; several critics have compared songs such as "Chaos Space Marine" and "The Place Where He Inserted the Blade" to Arcade Fire. Black Country, New Road also recalled listening to Kurt Vile and Frank Ocean for inspiration during the writing process. With their third album, following the departure of Wood, Forever Howlong has signaled a stylistic shift away from their first two albums, with the band going out of their way to avoid being "melodramatic" and aiming to be more "subtle" within their songwriting. thumb|[[Jockstrap (band)|Jockstrap performing in 2023.]] Beyond their own work, the band regularly performed live with fellow London experimental rock group Black Midi, touring collectively as Black Midi, New Road. Geordie Greep, the guitarist and vocalist of Black Midi, said the following: "If there's another band who are really good in the same city, who you also really get on with, there's no reason not to get together and do something proper. It's just making sure you don't do it for the sake of it and you have a solid reason for doing it". In June 2021, the group also worked with London musician Ethan P. Flynn, releasing a live version of his song "Television Show". Violinist Georgia Ellery is one half of the experimental electronic duo Jockstrap with Taylor Skye, who have released two EPs and an album. She also collaborated with Jamie xx on a BBC Radio 3 session on 30 May 2020. She made her acting debut in 2019's Bait, which won a British Academy Film award for Outstanding Debut by a British Writer, Director or Producer. Ellery has performed and recorded as a member of conservatory folk trio Contours and Happy Beigel Klezmer Orkester, a six-piece Klezmer band formed during studies at Guildhall School of Music and Drama. Drummer Charlie Wayne was also a drummer of another British band, Ugly. Saxophonist Lewis Evans has made a self-titled album under the alias Good with Parents and featured on the TRAAMS single "The Greyhound". Evans also contributed saxophone to Squid's debut album Bright Green Field, released on 7 May 2021. Former vocalist Isaac Wood has occasionally released music under his solo moniker The Guest, including an acoustic version of "Track X" called "Track X (The Guest)", performed solo by Wood and predating the formation of Black Country, New Road. Current members * Tyler Hyde – bass, backing vocals (2018–present), lead vocals, guitars (2022–present) * Lewis Evans – saxophone, flute (2018–present), backing and lead vocals (2022–present) * Georgia Ellery – violin, mandolin, guitars (2018–present), backing and lead vocals (2022–present) * May Kershaw – keyboards, piano, accordion, backing vocals (2018–present), lead vocals (2022–present) * Charlie Wayne – drums, percussion, banjo (2018–present), backing vocals (2022–present) * Luke Mark – guitars (2019–present), backing vocals (2022–present) Former members * Isaac Wood – lead vocals, guitars (2018–2022) * For the First Time (2021) * Ants from Up There (2022) * Forever Howlong (2025) {| class="wikitable sortable plainrowheaders" style="width: 80%;" |- ! scope="col" |Year ! scope="col" |Award ! scope="col" |Category ! scope="col" |Nominated work ! scope="col" |Result ! scope="col" class="unsortable" | |- | |Mercury Prize | Album of the Year | rowspan="2"|For the First Time | | style="text-align:center;"| |- | rowspan="2"| | Libera Awards | Best Breakthrough Release/Artist | | style="text-align:center;" | |- | AIM Independent Music Awards | Best Second Album | rowspan="2"| Ants from Up There | | style="text-align:center;" | |- | | Libera Awards | Creative Packaging | | align="center"| |- |} * * * * * Category:2018 establishments in England Category:English experimental rock groups Category:English post-rock groups Category:English post-punk music groups Category:Mixed-gender musical sextets Category:Musical groups from Cambridge Category:Musical groups established in 2018 Category:Ninja Tune artists

contested.title: Chinese people in Myanmar
contested.raw_text:
{{Short description|People of Han Chinese descent in Myanmar}} {{POV|date=September 2024}} {{EngvarB|date=October 2022}} {{Use dmy dates|date=October 2022}} {{Infobox ethnic group | group = Burmese Chinese | native_name = | image = Sino-Burmese merchant & wife.jpg | caption = A Sino-Burmese merchant and his wife in [[British Raj]] Rangoon in 1895 | population = 1,637,540 (2012){{cite web|url=https://www.cia.gov/the-world-factbook/countries/burma/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210109075741/https://www.cia.gov/the-world-factbook/countries/burma|url-status=dead|archive-date=9 January 2021|title=CIA – The World Factbook|publisher=Cia.gov|access-date=11 May 2012}}{{cite web|url=https://2009-2017.state.gov/r/pa/ei/bgn/35910.htm#people|title=Burma|publisher=State.gov|date=3 August 2011|access-date=11 May 2012}} | popplace = {{hlist|[[Yangon]]|[[Mandalay]]|[[Taunggyi]]|[[Kokang]]|[[Lashio]]|[[Myitkyina]]|[[Myeik, Myanmar|Myeik]]}} | rels = {{hlist|[[Theravada Buddhism]]|[[Mahayana Buddhism]]|[[Taoism]]|[[Confucianism]]|[[Christianity]]|[[Islam]]}} | related_groups = [[Kokang Chinese|Kokang]]{{•}} [[Panthay]]s
{{hlist|[[Overseas Chinese]]}} | langs = {{hlist|[[Burmese language|Burmese]]|[[Kunming dialect|Yunnanese Mandarin]]|[[Hokkien]]|[[Yue Chinese|Cantonese]]|[[Hakka language|Hakka]]|[[Standard Chinese]]}} }} {{Infobox Chinese | t = {{linktext|緬甸|華人}} | s = 缅甸华人 | p = Miǎndiàn huárén | y = Míhndihn wàyàn | poj = Biān-tián hôa-jîn | phfs = Mién-thien fà-ngìn | s2 = 缅甸华侨 | t2 = {{linktext|緬甸|華僑}} | p2 = Miǎndiàn huáqiáo | y2 = Míhndihn wàkìu | poj2 = Biān-tián hôa-kiâu | phfs2 = Mién-thien fà-kiaau | my = မြန်မာတရုတ်လူမျိုး }} ''' Burmese Chinese''', also '''Sino-Burmese''' or '''[[Tayoke]]''' ({{langx|my|[[wikt:တရုတ်|တရုတ်]]}}), are Burmese citizens of [[Han Chinese]] ethnicity. They are a group of [[overseas Chinese]] born or raised in [[Myanmar]] (Burma).{{cite book |last=Hooker |first=Michael Barry |title=Law and the Chinese in Southeast Asia |year=2002 |publisher=Institute of SoutheastAsian Studies |isbn=981-230-125-9 }} Burmese Chinese are a well established ethnic group and are well represented in all upper levels of Burmese society.{{Cite book|title=Soldiers and Diplomacy in Burma: Understanding the Foreign Relations of the Burmese Praetorian State |last1= Egreteau |first1=Renaud |last2=Jagan |first2=Larry |publisher=National University of Singapore Press |year=2013 |isbn=978-9971-69-673-3 |pages=271–273}} They play a leading role in Burma's business sector and dominate the Burmese economy.{{cite book |title=Asian Firms: History, Institutions and Management |last= Tipton |first=Frank B. |publisher=Edward Elgar Publishing |year=2008 |isbn=978-1-84720-514-8|page=277}} They also have a strong presence in Burma's political scene with several having been major political figures, including [[San Yu]], [[Khin Nyunt]], and [[Ne Win]].{{cite magazine |first=Htet |last=Win |url=http://www.irrawaddy.org/article.php?art_id=3799&page=1 |title=The Road To Riches?
