Sangay Choden (born 11 May 1963, fourth wife) {| class="wikitable" |- ! Name!! Birth!!colspan="2" |Marriage!! Issue |- |Prince DashoKhamsum Singye Wangchuck ([http://2008.bhutan-360.com/uploads/2008/10/corontion_prince_khamsum.jpg Photo] ) || || || || |- |Princess AshiEuphelma Choden Wangchuck ([http://i1235.photobucket.com/albums/ff431/PallasMoon/Bhutan/Newscom-sipaphotosthree329532.jpg Photo]) || || 29 October 2020 || Dasho Thinley Norbu || |} *11 November 1955 – 15 May 1972: His Royal Highness Dasho (Prince) Jigme Singye Wangchuck *15 May 1972 – 15 July 1972: His Royal Highness Trongsa Penlop Dasho Jigme Singye Wangchuck, The Crown Prince of Bhutan *15 July 1972 – 14 December 2006: His Majesty King Jigme Singye, The Fourth Druk Gyalpo, The King of Bhutan *14 December 2006 – present: His Majesty King Jigme Singye, The Fourth Druk Gyalpo, The King Father of Bhutan * : **70px Collar of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum (16 March 1987). * Nepal : **70px Member of the Order of Ojaswi Rajanya (5 October 1988). * : ** 70px Collar of the Order of Mubarak the Great (1990). * : ** 70px Order of Al Khalifa, 1st Class (1990). * : **70px Knight of the Royal Order of the Seraphim (1994). **Champions of the Earth 2005: **WWF J Paul Getty Award 2006: Bhutan King Jigme Singye Wangchuck has been conferred the 2006 J Paul Getty Conservation Leadership Award in recognition of "his leadership and deep concern for the environment." **The 2006 TIME 100 2006: His Majesty the Fourth Druk Gyalpo of Bhutan was also recognized by the 2006 TIME 100 for "setting a quietly revolutionary precedent." His legacy of Gross National Happiness, the transition from Absolute Monarchy to Democratic Constitutional Monarchy, and the Drafting of the Constitution sets a moral example of transforming the world. **Kyoto Hall Of Fame 2011: In a ceremony attended by around 600 people, including Their Imperial Highnesses Prince Akishino-miya and Princess Kiko-Sama and prominent citizens of Japan, Her Royal Highness Princess Ashi Kezang Choden Wangchuck received the Hall of Fame award on behalf of the Fourth Druk Gyalpo on February 13. His Majesty, Jigme Singye Wangchuck the fourth King of Bhutan was recognised for his outstanding contribution to the protection of the global environment. *Druk Gyalpo *Ngawang Namgyal *Penlop of Trongsa *Great Buddha Dordenma *House of Wangchuck *Gross National Happiness *Tashichho Dzong * * * * * *[https://web.archive.org/web/20080905202259/http://2008.bhutan-360.com/2007/11/21/the-royal-family-of-bhutan/ The Royal Family of Bhutan] (archived 5 September 2008) *[https://content.time.com/time/specials/packages/article/0,28804,1975813_1975846_1976550,00.html Time Magazine's 100 People Who Shape Our World 2006: King Jigme Singye Wangchuck] Category:Bhutanese monarchs Category:Bhutanese expatriates in India Category:Bhutanese expatriates in the United Kingdom Category:1955 births Category:Living people Category:Tibetan Buddhists from Bhutan Category:Monarchs who abdicated Category:Buddhist monarchs Category:Wangchuck dynasty Category:Anti-consumerists

The '''2016 Monaco GP2 Series round''' was a pair of motor races held on 27 and 28 May 2016 at the [[Circuit de Monaco]], [[Monte Carlo]], [[Monaco]] as part of the [[GP2 Series]]. It was the fourth round of the [[2016 GP2 Series]] and was run in support of the [[2016 Monaco Grand Prix]]. The first race, a 40-lap feature event, was won by [[Russian Time]] driver [[Artem Markelov]] after starting from 15th position. [[Norman Nato]] finished in second for [[Racing Engineering]] and [[MP Motorsport]]'s [[Oliver Rowland]] took third. [[Nobuharu Matsushita]] for [[ART Grand Prix]] won the following day's 30-lap sprint race from pole position with [[Carlin Motorsport|Carlin]]'s [[Marvin Kirchhöfer]] and Markelov's teammate [[Raffaele Marciello]] second and third.

[[Sergey Sirotkin (racing driver)|Sergey Sirotkin]] won the [[pole position]] for the feature race by posting the fastest lap in qualifying but a slow start off the [[Glossary of motorsport terms#G|grid]] allowed Nato into the lead. Nato pulled away from the rest of the field and led for the majority of the race until a [[Safety car|virtual safety car]] caught him out in the event's closing laps. Markelov was the main beneficiary of this and he made his mandatory [[pit stop]] to change tyres on the penultimate lap and held off Nato to take his maiden GP2 Series win. Matsushita started from pole position in the sprint race and repelled a challenge from the fast-starting Kirchhöfer to his right at the start. In a processional race, Matsushita opened out a 13.6 second lead over Kirchhöfer to win for the second time in GP2.

The results of the round meant Nato took the lead of the Drivers' Championship by one point over feature race winner Markelov. [[Alex Lynn]] maintained third place with 41 points with [[Pierre Gasly]] and Marciello fourth and fifth. In the Teams' Championship, Russian Time moved from fourth to first and Racing Engineering were eleven points behind but they retained second place. The leaders of the Teams' Championship enterin the event [[DAMS]] dropped to third while [[Prema Powerteam|Prema Racing]] fell to fourth with eight rounds left in the season.

==Background==

[[File:Circuito_de_Monte-Carlo_(3530969729).jpg|left|thumb|[[Circuit de Monaco]], where the race was held.]]

The 2016 Monaco GP2 Series round was the second of eleven scheduled events of the [[2016 GP2 Series]] season. It was held on 27 and 28 May 2016 at the [[Circuit de Monaco]] in [[Monaco]] and [[Card (sports)|supported]] the [[2016 Monaco Grand Prix]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://motorsports.nbcsports.com/tag/2016-gp2-calendar/|title=GP2, GP3 Schedules Confirmed for 2016, Featuring New Races|last=Smith|first=Luke|date=4 March 2016|publisher=[[NBC Sports]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160827170652/http://motorsports.nbcsports.com/tag/2016-gp2-calendar/|archive-date=27 August 2016|access-date=27 August 2016}}</ref> Tyre supplier [[Pirelli]] brought two types of tyre to the race: two dry compounds (super soft "options" and soft "primes").<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pirelli.com/tyre/ww/en/news/2016/05/24/soft-and-supersoft-p-zero-tyres-for-gp2-in-monaco/|title=Soft and supersoft P Zero tyres for GP2 in Monaco|date=24 May 2016|publisher=Pirelli|access-date=28 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082138/https://www.pirelli.com/tyre/ww/en/news/2016/05/24/soft-and-supersoft-p-zero-tyres-for-gp2-in-monaco/|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|df=dmy-all}}</ref> The [[drag reduction system]] (DRS) had one activation zone for the race: on the start/finish [[Straight (racing)|straight]] linking Anthony Noghes and Sainte Devote turns.<ref name="regulations">{{cite web|url=http://www.gp2series.com/Guide-to/The-regulations/|title=The Regulations|work=GP2 Series|access-date=27 August 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160407225502/http://www.gp2series.com/Guide-to/The-regulations/|archive-date=7 April 2016}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/2642188-monaco-grand-prix-2016-preview-start-time-tv-info-weather-schedule-odds|title=Monaco Grand Prix 2016 Preview: Start Time, TV Info, Weather, Schedule, Odds|last=Walthert|first=Matthew|date=25 May 2016|publisher=[[Bleacher Report]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160802211332/http://bleacherreport.com/articles/2642188-monaco-grand-prix-2016-preview-start-time-tv-info-weather-schedule-odds|archive-date=2 August 2016|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018|df=dmy-all}}</ref> There were 11 teams of 2 drivers each entered for the race for a total of 22 participants and each driver used the [[Dallara GP2/11]] vehicle.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Race result: GP2 Series 2016: Race 3 in Monte Carlo on 27 May 2016|url=https://www.speedsport-magazine.com/motorsport/formula-level2/gp2-series-formula-3000/2016/monte-carlo_race3_results.html|url-status=live|access-date=8 September 2021|publisher=Speedsport Magazine|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210908074332/https://www.speedsport-magazine.com/motorsport/formula-level2/gp2-series-formula-3000/2016/monte-carlo_race3_results.html |archive-date=8 September 2021 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Race result: GP2 Series 2016: Race 4 in Monte Carlo on 28 May 2016|url=https://www.speedsport-magazine.com/motorsport/formula-level2/gp2-series-formula-3000/2016/monte-carlo_race4_results.html|url-status=live|access-date=8 September 2021|publisher=Speedsport Magazine|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210908074330/https://www.speedsport-magazine.com/motorsport/formula-level2/gp2-series-formula-3000/2016/monte-carlo_race4_results.html |archive-date=8 September 2021 }}</ref>

Before the race, [[Prema Powerteam|Prema Racing]] driver [[Pierre Gasly]] led the Drivers' Championship with 33 points, seven ahead of [[Norman Nato]] of [[Racing Engineering]] in second, who in turn, was a further four points in front of third-placed [[Alex Lynn]] for [[Russian Time]]. [[DAMS]]' [[Nicholas Latifi]] and Russian Time racer [[Artem Markelov]] were both tied for fourth with 20 points each.<ref name="drivers">{{cite web|url=http://www.gp2series.com/Standings/Driver/?seasonid=173|title=Drivers Standings|work=GP2 Series|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170314142440/http://www.gp2series.com/Standings/Driver/?seasonid=173|archive-date=14 March 2017|url-status=dead|access-date=27 August 2016}}</ref> DAMS and Racing Engineering jointly led the Teams' Championship with 43 points each; Prema Racing stood in third position with 31 points and held a one-point gap over the fourth-placed Russian Time. [[Carlin Motorsport|Carlin]] were fifth with ten points.<ref name="teams">{{cite web|url=http://www.gp2series.com/Standings/Team/?seasonid=173|title=Teams' Standings|work=GP2 Series|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160828123043/http://www.gp2series.com/Standings/Team/?seasonid=173|archive-date=28 August 2016|url-status=dead|access-date=27 August 2016}}</ref>

==Practice and qualifying==

One 45-minute practice session was held on Thursday.<ref name="regulations" /> The start of the session was moved forward five minutes following repairs to a drain cover that was dislodged during the first Monaco Grand Prix practice session by [[Nico Rosberg]] and struck by [[Jenson Button]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=http://www.motorsport.com/gp2/news/monaco-gp2-sirotkin-tops-practice-as-ste-devote-claims-three-cars-740459/?s=1|title=Monaco GP2: Sirotkin tops practice as Ste Devote claims three cars|last=Khorounzhiy|first=Valentin|date=26 May 2016|website=[[motorsport.com]]|publisher=[[Motorsport Network]]|archive-url=https://archive.today/20180804082341/https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/monaco-gp2-sirotkin-tops-practice-as-ste-devote-claims-three-cars-740459/740459/?s=1%3Fs%3D1|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=28 January 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> [[Sergey Sirotkin (racing driver)|Sergey Sirotkin]] ([[ART Grand Prix]]) was fastest with a lap of 1 minute, 20.361 seconds, almost one-tenth of a second faster than Nato in second. Lynn, [[Arthur Pic]], [[Jordan King]] (Racing Engineering), [[Mitch Evans]] ([[Campos Racing]]), Gasly, [[Raffaele Marciello]] (Russian Time), [[Oliver Rowland]] ([[MP Motorsport]]) and Markelov occupied positions three to ten.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/124490|title=Monaco GP2: ART Grand Prix's Sirotkin fastest in practice|date=26 May 2016|website=[[Autosport]]|publisher=[[Haymarket Media Group|Haymarket Publications]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160602032445/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/124490|archive-date=2 June 2016|access-date=28 January 2017|url-status=live|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Gasly ran wide at Sainte Devote corner, colliding with an outside barrier, and removing his front-left wheel.<ref name=":0" /> The [[Safety car|virtual safety car]] (VSC) system was enabled to allow [[Motorsport marshal|marshals]] to extricate his car from the track.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.gp2series.com/News-Room/News/2016/May/Sirotkin-tops-free-practice-in-Monte-Carlo/|title=Sirtokin tops free practice in Monte Carlo|date=26 May 2016|website=GP2 Series|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160630013752/http://www.gp2series.com/News-Room/News/2016/May/Sirotkin-tops-free-practice-in-Monte-Carlo/|archive-date=30 June 2016|url-status=dead|access-date=28 January 2017|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Soon after, [[Nobuharu Matsushita]] (ART Grand Prix) locked his tyres on the run to the same corner and went straight into the wall. [[Daniël de Jong]] (MP Motorsport) [[Understeer and oversteer|oversteered]] into the Sainte Devote barrier and [[Sean Gelael]] (Campos Racing) ended the session early when he [[Stall (engine)|stalled]] his car and triggered a traffic holdup.<ref name=":0" />
<nowiki/>[[File:Sergey Sirotkin SMP Racing.jpg|thumb|upright|[[Sergey Sirotkin (racing driver)|Sergey Sirotkin]] ''(pictured in 2017)'' had the first GP2 Series [[pole position]] of his career.]]

Thursday's afternoon's qualifying session ran for 30 minutes. Qualifying was divided into two groups of 13 cars to avoid overcrowding. Odd numbered vehicles were drawn in Group A and the even numbered cars were put in Group B. The drivers' fastest lap times determined the starting order for the first race.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport-total.com/gp2/news/gp2-sergei-sirotkin-erobert-wichtige-monaco-pole-16052601|title=GP2: Sergei Sirotkin erobert wichtige Monaco-Pole|last=Dirnbeck|first=Gerald|date=26 May 2016|publisher=motorsport-total.com|language=de|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082403/https://www.motorsport-total.com/gp2/news/gp2-sergei-sirotkin-erobert-wichtige-monaco-pole-16052601|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> The pole position winner took four points for the Drivers' and Teams' Championships.<ref name="regulations" /> The second group was competitive because eight drivers temporarily claimed [[pole position]].<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|url=http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/124496|title=Monaco GP2: Sirotkin takes pole ahead of Nato in split session|date=26 May 2016|website=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket Publications|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160602032434/http://www.autosport.com/news/report.php/id/124496|archive-date=2 June 2016|access-date=28 January 2017|url-status=live|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Sirtokin clinched his maiden GP2 Series pole position with a time of 1 minute, 19.186 seconds,<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.crash.net/gp2/news/230757/1/sirotkin-eases-to-dominant-monaco-pole.html|title=GP2 Monte Carlo: Sirotkin eases to dominant Monaco pole|last=Barstow|first=Ollie|date=26 May 2016|website=crash.net|publisher=Crash Media Group|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170528174943/http://www.crash.net/gp2/news/230757/1/sirotkin-eases-to-dominant-monaco-pole.html|archive-date=28 May 2017|url-status=live|access-date=28 January 2017|df=dmy-all}}</ref> which was recorded in the session's closing period. He was joined on the [[Glossary of motorsport terms#G|grid's]] front row by Nato whose lap was seven-tenths of a second slower in the final seconds of Group A.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|url=http://www.motorsport.com/gp2/news/monaco-gp2-sirotkin-takes-pole-in-red-flagged-group-qualifying-740566/|title=Monaco GP2: Sirotkin takes pole in red-flagged group qualifying|last=Khorounzhiy|first=Valentin|date=26 May 2016|website=motorsport.com|publisher=Motorsport Network|access-date=28 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160529021426/http://www.motorsport.com/gp2/news/monaco-gp2-sirotkin-takes-pole-in-red-flagged-group-qualifying-740566/|archive-date=29 May 2016|url-status=live|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Nato's teammate King qualified in third, Evans fourth, and Rowland fifth. Lynn, [[Antonio Giovinazzi]] (Prema Racing), Marciello, Latifi and [[Luca Ghiotto]] ([[Trident Racing|Trident]]) rounded out the top ten qualifiers.<ref name=":2" />

Pic was the fastest driver not to qualify in the top ten; his fastest time was nearly 1.2 seconds off Sirotkin's pace. He was followed by Matsushita and [[Marvin Kirchhöfer]] (Carlin) and Gelael in 12th to 14th.<ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" /> 15th-place qualifier Markelov misjudged his approach to Sainte Devote corner, locked his car's tyres, went airborne after driving over the [[Curb|kerbing]] and ran straight into a barrier.<ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" /> The wall was separated into two halves, stopping the session for 20 minutes while it was repaired. Markelov was unhurt.<ref name=":3" /> [[Gustav Malja]] ([[Rapax Team|Rapax]]), [[Philo Paz Armand]] (Trident), de Jong and [[Jimmy Eriksson]] ([[Arden International]]) filled positions 16 to 19.<ref name=":2" /> Gasly had a car brake failure and entered the [[Pit stop|pit lane]] but missed the [[Truck scale|weighbridge]] when requested to enter the area. Gasly was required to start the feature event from the pit lane. [[Sergio Canamasas]] (Carlin) was judged to have made the same error and [[Nabil Jeffri]] (Arden International) was observed to block Markelov and both drivers were required to start from the pit lane. Jeffri could not take his penalty as he qualified 22nd. Since he could not take his three-place grid penalty, Jeffri began from the pit lane.<ref name=QualiPenalties>{{Cite web|url=http://www.gpupdate.net/en/gp2-news/339805/gasly-to-start-from-pits-after-penalty/|title=Gasly to start from pits after penalty|date=27 May 2016|website=GPUpdate|publisher=JHED Media BV|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170714173552/http://www.gpupdate.net/en/gp2-news/339805/gasly-to-start-from-pits-after-penalty/|archive-date=14 July 2017|url-status=dead|access-date=28 January 2017}}</ref>

;Notes
* {{Note|1|1}} — [[Pierre Gasly]] and [[Sergio Canamasas]] started from the [[Pit stop|pit lane]] having failed to stop at the [[scrutineer]]ing [[Truck scale|weighbridge]].<ref name=QualiPenalties/>
* {{Note|2|2}} — [[Nabil Jeffri]] received a three-place [[Glossary of motorsport terms#G|grid]] penalty after being found guilty of impeding [[Artem Markelov]] during Group B's qualifying session. However, since Jeffri could not take his penalty in full, he started from the pit lane.<ref name=QualiPenalties/>

==Races==
The first race was held over {{Convert|140|km|mi|abbr=on}} or 60 minutes (which ever came first) and the regulations required drivers to make one [[pit stop]]. The first ten finishers scored points, with two given to the [[fastest lap]] holder. The grid for the second race was determined by the finishing order of the first but with the first eight drivers in reverse order of where they finished. It was run for {{Convert|100|km|mi|abbr=on}} or 45 minutes (which ever came first) and, in contrast to the first race, drivers were not required to make pit stops. The top eight finishers earned points towards their respective championships.<ref name="regulations" />

===Feature race===
The race started in warm weather of {{Convert|22|C|F}} and a track temperature of {{Convert|34|C|F|abbr=on}} at 11:15 [[Central European Summer Time]] (CEST) ([[UTC+02:00|UTC+2]]) on 27 May.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|url=http://juniorteam.redbull.com/article/gp2-gasly-ready-early-monaco|title=GP2 – Gasly ready early for Monaco|date=25 May 2016|publisher=[[Red Bull Junior Team]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804083503/http://juniorteam.redbull.com/article/gp2-gasly-ready-early-monaco|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=4 August 2018}}</ref><ref name="RERace1">{{cite press release|title=Norman Nato finishes second for Racing Engineering today at Monaco|url=http://www.racing-engineering.com/GP2/news/324/324-e.html|publisher=Racing Engineering|date=27 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082138/http://www.racing-engineering.com/GP2/news/324/324-e.html|archive-date=4 August 2018|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Sirotkin made a slow start, allowing fellow front-row starter Nato to brake later than him and claim the lead into Sainte Devote corner.<ref name="RERace1" /><ref name="PSFeature1">{{cite web|last=David|first=Gruz|title=Markelov takes shock maiden win aided by late-race VSC|url=http://www.paddockscout.com/markelov-takes-shock-maiden-win-aided-by-late-race-vsc/34697|publisher=Paddock Scout|date=27 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160528155011/http://www.paddockscout.com/markelov-takes-shock-maiden-win-aided-by-late-race-vsc/34697|archive-date=28 May 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref> Sirtokin then repelled a challenge from King over second place. The field avoided incident on the first lap and the top three of Sirotkin, Nato and King pulled away from the fourth-placed Evans over the next few laps. On lap six, the first mandatory pit stops to change tyres were made by Giovinazzi, Ghiotto, Pic and Kirchhöfer in the lower end of the order. Meanwhile, Nato set consecutive [[fastest lap]]s to give himself a lead of 1.2 seconds over Sirotkin at the start of lap 10.<ref name="RERace1" /><ref name="MMagazinFeature">{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport-magazin.com/gp2/news-227888-monaco-markelov-fuchst-bei-gp2-crashfest-alle-aus/|title=Nato verliert Sieg in der Schlussphase – Monaco: Markelov fuchst bei GP2-Crashfest alle aus|last=Ebner|first=Tobias|date=27 May 2016|work=Motorsport Magazin|language=de|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082556/https://www.motorsport-magazin.com/gp2/news-227888-monaco-markelov-fuchst-bei-gp2-crashfest-alle-aus/|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> King was distanced by Nato and Sirotkin by lap 11 as Sirotkin made an error and cut the Novelle [[Chicane]], losing time.<ref name="MCOMFeatureRace">{{cite web|last=Khorounzhiy|first=Valentin|title=Monaco GP2: Markelov robs Nato of win in VSC farce|url=https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/monaco-gp2-markelov-robs-nato-of-win-in-vsc-farce-740834/740834/|work=motorsport.com|publisher=Motorsport Network|date=27 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082137/https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/monaco-gp2-markelov-robs-nato-of-win-in-vsc-farce-740834/740834/|archive-date=4 August 2018|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Rowland was challenging the slower Evans who straight-lined the Novelle Chicane at the exit of the tunnel to keep the position.<ref name="GP2FeatureReport" /> Evans was subsequently issued a five-second time penalty on the 20th lap after the stewards deemed him to have gained an illegal advantage.<ref name="MMagazinFeature" /><ref name="GP2FeatureReport">{{cite web|title=Artem Markelov soars to maiden win|url=http://www.gp2series.com:80/News-Room/News/2016/May/Artem-Markelov-soars-to-maiden-win/|work=GP2 Series|date=27 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160530183049/http://www.gp2series.com/News-Room/News/2016/May/Artem-Markelov-soars-to-maiden-win/|archive-date=30 May 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

[[File:Norman Nato 2012.JPG|left|thumb|upright|[[Norman Nato]] (''pictured in 2012'') came second after he lost the feature race win to [[Artem Markelov]] in the last ten laps of the event.]]

Meanwhile, another VSC was triggered on lap 18 when Gasly was trying to recover ground and mounted the back of Eriksson's car at La Racasse corner, which would ultimately result in Eriksson's retirement and debris was left on the track.<ref name="PSFeature1" /><ref name="CrashFeatureMarkelov">{{cite web|url=https://www.crash.net/gp2/race-report/230772/1/markelov-takes-contentious-monaco-win|title=GP2 Monte Carlo: Markelov takes contentious feature race win|last=Llewellyn|first=Craig|date=27 May 2016|work=crash.net|publisher=Crash Media Group|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082645/https://www.crash.net/gp2/race-report/230772/1/markelov-takes-contentious-monaco-win|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> Gasly was consequently forced to enter the pit lane to have his front wing replaced. The VSC was soon lifted after the debris was cleared from the circuit.<ref name="MMagazinFeature" /><ref name="MCOMFeatureRace" /> At the restart, Rowland quickly displaced Evans and charged after the leading trio of Nato, Sirotkin and King.<ref name="GP2FeatureReport" /> More drama came on lap 23 when Latifi was battling Markelov for seventh and got distracted. This caused Latifi to clip the barrier alongside the circuit in the tunnel and retired in the pit lane with a bent front-left [[Suspension (vehicle)|suspension]].<ref name="PSFeature1" /><ref name="MMagazinFeature" /><ref name="CrashFeatureMarkelov" /> Then, as Sirotkin was quickly catching Nato, his car got into an uncontrolled sideways manoeuvre after clipping the kerb too hard at the exit of La Piscine turn and hit the wall for the second successive feature race.<ref name="PSFeature1" /><ref name="MCOMFeatureRace" /><ref name="AutosportMarkelovWin">{{cite web|url=https://www.autosport.com/f2/news/124537/honda-matsushita-dominates-gp2-sprint-race|title=GP2 Monaco: Artem Markelov takes shock victory|date=27 May 2016|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket Publications|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082723/https://www.autosport.com/f2/news/124537/honda-matsushita-dominates-gp2-sprint-race|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> Another VSC was activated to allow for general cleanup and racing resumed on lap 24. On the next lap, a third VSC was required when Jeffri crashed at La Rascasse corner. The VSC was lifted on lap 26. Competitive action lasted for three laps until a fourth VSC came about after King struck the barrier; he drove to the pit lane to retire.<ref name="MMagazinFeature" />

Markelov moved into the lead when Lynn, Rowland and Evans pitted over laps 29 and 31 as Nato's pit crew were slow installing his front-right wheel. Markelow set faster lap times than anyone else despite being on heavily worn tyres.<ref name="GP2FeatureReport" /> The VSC was used twice more for separate incidents that Ghiotto caused on lap 36 as he left debris on the [[racing line]] and hit the inside barrier at the Loews [[Hairpin turn|hairpin]].<ref name="PSFeature1" /><ref name="MMagazinFeature" /><ref name="GPUpdateFeature">{{cite web|title=Markelov takes maiden win in GP2 thriller|url=http://www.gpupdate.net:80/en/gp2-news/339818/markelov-takes-maiden-win-in-gp2-thriller/|work=GPUpdate|publisher=JHED Media BV|date=27 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160531171621/http://www.gpupdate.net/en/gp2-news/339818/markelov-takes-maiden-win-in-gp2-thriller/|archive-date=31 May 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Markelov was the main beneficiary of this as he was 15 seconds ahead of Nato. He was quicker than Nato in the tunnel and Tabac turn while his rivals' pace was limited since they were slower in the tighter corners.<ref name="REPenaltyAutosport">{{cite web|url=https://www.autosport.com/f2/news/124576/stewards-had-to-explain-shock-markelov-win|title=Racing Engineering needed stewards to explain GP2 VSC confusion|date=30 May 2016|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket Publications|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082827/https://www.autosport.com/f2/news/124576/stewards-had-to-explain-shock-markelov-win|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref><ref name="MRTMCOM">{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/markelov-and-russian-time-certain-monaco-win-within-the-rules-740950/740950/|title=Markelov and Russian Time certain Monaco win within the rules|last=Khorounzhiy|first=Valentin|last2=Karpov|first2=Oleg|date=27 May 2016|work=motorsport.com|publisher=Motorsport Network|archive-url=https://archive.today/20180804082819/https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/markelov-and-russian-time-certain-monaco-win-within-the-rules-740950/740950/|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018|df=dmy-all}}</ref> After the restart, Markelov made his mandatory pit stop on lap 39 to switch onto the super soft tyres, emerging just ahead of Nato who could not overtake Markelov on his [[Glossary of motorsport terms#O|out-lap]].<ref name="PSFeature1" /><ref name="AutosportMarkelovWin" /> Because the five VSCs elongated the race, the number of laps was lowered to 40 from 42.<ref name="MTotalRace1">{{cite web|last=Dirnbeck|first=Gerald|title=Überraschungssieg von Markelow in Monte Carlo|url=https://www.motorsport-total.com/gp2/news/ueberraschungssieg-von-markelow-in-monte-carlo-16052701|publisher=motorsport-total.com|language=de|date=27 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> Markelov's fresher tyres allowed him to hold off Nato in the last two laps for his maiden GP2 Series victory by 1{{1/2}} seconds.<ref name="CrashFeatureMarkelov" /> Third was Rowland and the rest of the provisional order consisting of Lynn, Evans, Marciello, Giovinazzi, Kirchhöfer, Matsushita, de Jong, Pic, Canamasas, Gelael, Malja, Gasly and Armand.<ref name="PSFeature1" /> After the race, Giovinazzi and Malja received five-second time penalties for illegally cutting the Novelle Chicane and Sainte Devote corner, respectively, handing the sprint race pole position from Kirchhöfer to Matsushita.<ref name=FeatureRacePenalties>{{Cite web|url=https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/giovinazzi-penalty-hands-matsushita-pole-for-monaco-sprint-740975/740975/|title=Giovinazzi penalty hands Matsushita pole for Monaco sprint|last=Khorounzhiy|first=Valentin|date=27 May 2016|website=motorsport.com|publisher=Motorsport Network|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref>

===Sprint race===
[[File:Nobuharu Matsushita 2015.jpg|thumb|upright|[[Nobuharu Matsushita]] ''(pictured in 2015)'' won his second career victory in the GP2 Series by 13,6 seconds over [[Marvin Kirchhöfer]].]]

The second event began in a warm air temperature of {{Convert|21|C|F|abbr=on}} and a track temperature of {{Convert|35|C|F|abbr=on}} at 16:10 local time on 28 May.<ref name=":1" /><ref name="RER2">{{cite web|url=http://www.racing-engineering.com/GP2/news/325/325-e.html|title=Norman Nato leads the Driver’s Championship for Racing Engineering after today’s race at Monaco|date=28 May 2016|publisher=Racing Engineering|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082914/http://www.racing-engineering.com/GP2/news/325/325-e.html|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> Sirtokin's team changed the [[monocoque]] on his car after his crash in the feature race.<ref name="SirotkinMONColumn">{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/sergey-sirotkin-the-toughest-moment-of-my-life-742349/742349/|title=Sergey Sirotkin: The toughest moment of my life|last=Sirotkin|first=Sergey|author-link=Sergey Sirotkin (racing driver)|date=31 May 2016|work=motorsport.com|publisher=Motorsport Network|archive-url=https://archive.today/20180804082956/https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/sergey-sirotkin-the-toughest-moment-of-my-life-742349/742349/|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On the grid, Matsushita had a slow initial getaway and the second-placed Kirchhöfer was faster and drew alongside into Sainte Devote corner. However, Kirchhöfer did not move into the lead as Matsushita held him off by staying on the right and out-braking him.<ref name="SprintFSRace">{{cite web|last=Allen|first=Peter|title=Nobuharu Matsushita dominates GP2 Monaco sprint race|url=http://www.paddockscout.com/nobuharu-matsushita-dominates-gp2-monaco-sprint-race/34708|publisher=Paddock Scout|date=28 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160529110842/http://www.paddockscout.com/nobuharu-matsushita-dominates-gp2-monaco-sprint-race/34708|archive-date=29 May 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="AutosportMatshusitaSprint">{{cite web|url=https://www.autosport.com/f2/news/124537/honda-matsushita-dominates-gp2-sprint-race|title=Monaco GP2: Honda's Matsushita dominates sprint race|date=28 May 2016|work=Autosport|publisher=Haymarket Publications|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804082723/https://www.autosport.com/f2/news/124537/honda-matsushita-dominates-gp2-sprint-race|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> Behind the duo, Maricello went defensive against the fast-starting Evans and Nato went to the outside of Rowland to overtake him but he could not pass Lynn as the field were approaching Casino corner.<ref name="GP2SprintReport">{{cite web|title=Matsushita powers to victory in Monaco|url=http://www.gp2series.com:80/News-Room/News/2016/May/Matsushita-powers-to-victory-in-Monaco-/|work=GP2 Series|date=28 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160530185900/http://www.gp2series.com/News-Room/News/2016/May/Matsushita-powers-to-victory-in-Monaco-/|archive-date=30 May 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> From there on, the race settled into a rhythm as Matsushita opened his lead over Kirchhöfer to three seconds.<ref name="MCOMSprintEvent">{{cite web|url=https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/monaco-gp2-matsushita-claims-art-s-first-win-of-2016-741343/741343/|title=Monaco GP2: Matsushita claims ART's first win of 2016|last=Khorounzhiy|first=Valentin|date=28 May 2016|work=motorsport.com|publisher=Motorsport Network|archive-url=https://archive.today/20180804083115/https://www.motorsport.com/fia-f2/news/monaco-gp2-matsushita-claims-art-s-first-win-of-2016-741343/741343/|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018|df=dmy-all}}</ref> [[Racing flags#Yellow flag|Yellow flags]] were briefly waved by course officials on the fourth lap as King was about to pass Armand for 18th but he left the latter without enough space to get by and put him into a wall at the Loews hairpin. King incurred a five-second time penalty for causing the incident. On lap nine, further disruption was caused when Gelael slid wide at Sainte Devote and crashed into a barrier which activated the VSC.<ref name="RER2" /><ref name="SprintFSRace" /><ref name="MCOMSprintEvent" />

Sirotkin's poor season continued when the on-board [[fire extinguisher]] in his car was activated by debris from his tyre at the entry to Massenet turn on the 11th lap. Sirotkin's visibility was greatly hindered by foam on his visor and he stopped on the escape road at Casino Square corner and retired.<ref name="SirotkinMONColumn" /><ref name="AutosportMatshusitaSprint" /> At the front, Kirchhöfer took a second out of Matsushita's lead under the VSC only for the latter to respond by being six seconds by lap 15. It further grew as the race progressed and Kirchhöfer was left to holding off third-placed Maricello.<ref name="MCOMSprintEvent" /> Nato and Matsushita traded the fastest lap as the laps passed by with the accolade being claimed by Matsushita on lap 28 with a time of 1 minute and 21.554 seconds and earned him two championship points.<ref name="GP2SprintReport" /> In a processional race,<ref name="AutosportMatshusitaSprint" /> it was Matsushita's victory, achieving his second win in the GP2 Series after the [[2015 Hungaroring GP2 Series round|2015 Hungaroring feature race]],<ref name="MatshusitaSprintWin">{{cite web|url=https://www.crash.net/gp2/race-report/230827/1/honda-junior-matsushita-sprints-to-monaco-glory|title=GP2 Monaco: Honda junior Matsushita sprints to Monaco glory|last=Barstow|first=Ollie|date=28 May 2016|work=crash.net|publisher=Crash Media Group|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180804083138/https://www.crash.net/gp2/race-report/230827/1/honda-junior-matsushita-sprints-to-monaco-glory|archive-date=4 August 2018|url-status=live|access-date=3 August 2018}}</ref> and ensuring four different drivers had won all four of the season's races held at the time.<ref name="GPUpdateSprint">{{cite web|title=Matsushita cruises to victory in Monaco|url=http://www.gpupdate.net:80/en/gp2-news/339883/matsushita-cruises-to-victory-in-monaco/|work=GPUpdate|publisher=JHED Media BV|date=28 May 2018|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160531190214/http://www.gpupdate.net/en/gp2-news/339883/matsushita-cruises-to-victory-in-monaco/|archive-date=31 May 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Kirchhöfer finished 13.660 seconds later in second in Carlin's first podium since [[Felipe Nasr]] at the [[2014 Yas Marina GP2 Series round|2014 Abu Dhabi Sprint race]] and Maricello completed the podium in third.<ref name="MCOMSprintEvent" /> Off the podium, Evans took fourth and fifth-placed Alex Lynn led a pack of cars comprising Nato, Rowland and Markelov who were covered by 1.3 seconds in positions six through eight.<ref name="SprintFSRace" /> The final classified finishers were Pic, Canamasas, de Jong, Malja, Gasly, Ghiotto, Eriksson, King, Jeffri and Giovinazzi who stalled on the grid.<ref name="AutosportMatshusitaSprint" />

==Post-round==
The top three drivers in both races appeared on the podium to collect their trophies and spoke to the media in separate press conferences. Markelov was delighted to achieve his first win and stayed on the soft compound tyres because his team deemed their pace decent, "We really did a good job together. To finish P1 in Monaco and score my first victory in GP2, it’s amazing... I don’t have any other words. I’m really happy."<ref name="FeatureQuotes">{{cite web|title=Round 2 post Feature race quotes|url=http://www.gp2series.com:80/News-Room/News/2016/May/Round-2-post-Feature-race-quotes/|work=GP2 Series|date=27 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160530190044/http://www.gp2series.com/News-Room/News/2016/May/Round-2-post-Feature-race-quotes/|archive-date=30 May 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Nato, who came second, stated that he was displeased and angry over seeing Markelov take the lead as he did not feel the need to change tyres and was urged by his engineer to pressure him, "I tried to overtake him before Turn 5 and when I saw I couldn’t do it, I just focused on the end of the race. P2 is better than nothing. This race was for us today but it’s still good points for the team and the drivers’ standings."<ref name="FeatureQuotes" /> Rowland said he was pleased to finish third and complimented his team, "It was a pretty good day. It was just a bit strange when Artem came out first. I didn’t expect that!"<ref name="FeatureQuotes" />[[File:Oliver Rowland 2012.JPG|left|thumb|upright|[[Oliver Rowland]] ''(pictured in 2016)'' took the first podium of his GP2 Series career with a third-place finish in the feature race.]]

After the sprint race, Matsushita called his Monaco sprint race victory "even more special" than other circuits and said tyre degradation was low as he focused on the race, "I’m happy to come home with a trophy, a victory here and some good points for the team. It was my first win in Monaco so it feels special. I led the whole race, I felt good in the car so I’m just really happy."<ref name="SprintRace">{{cite web|title=Round 2 post sprint race quotes|url=http://www.gp2series.com:80/News-Room/News/2016/May/Round-2-post-sprint-race-quotes/|work=GP2 Series|date=28 May 2016|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160602063908/http://www.gp2series.com/News-Room/News/2016/May/Round-2-post-sprint-race-quotes/|archive-date=2 June 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Kirchhöfer spoke of his enjoyment racing at Monaco for the first time and had not anticipated achieving a podium result at the track, "I’m really happy with P2. I had a good gateway at the start but I couldn’t overtake Matsushita at the first corner. After that, I was thinking to bring the car home. But we are all happy, me and the team, with this result. There’s more to come!"<ref name="SprintRace" /> Third-placed Marciello called his race "boring" because he found it difficult to overtake Kirchhöfer and hoped for a safety car or an extended VSC. Nevertheless, he said he was still happy and wanted to continue the form into the [[2016 Baku GP2 Series round|Baku round]], "It’s still a good weekend with Artem’s P1 yesterday and my podium today but we need to improve during the qualifying. Our car is really good in the long runs and we have good pace. So our goal is to improve during the qualifying session."<ref name="SprintRace" />

The technical director of Racing Engineering Sébastien Viger explained his team visited the stewards for several hours after the feature race to understand how Nato lost the chance to win, "This showed how wrong things went for us with the multiple virtual safety cars. Basically, Markelov was very lucky as he took the VSC on slow zones [of the track] where he was not losing time and Norman and others took it on fast zones, which resulted in huge time losses."<ref name="REPenaltyAutosport" /> Despite the VSC allowing Markelov to increase his advantage, Svetlana Strelnikova, the team principal of Russian Time, said she felt they were within the regulations, "We led by 15 seconds [over Nato], but the question is 'where did the other 15 seconds come that we could stop and rejoin first?'. But there was VSC – six seconds to turn it off, six seconds to turn it on, and each driver reacts in a different way – they press the button at the moment that the engineer says on the radio and the car is running full speed again."<ref name="MRTMCOM" /> Valentin Khorounzhiy of [[motorsport.com]] wrote that the changes in gap were unlikely to account for Markelov's reaction time but for where on the track the VSC was activated as some drivers slowed to a different extent. He noted suggestions of protesting Markelov's victory would be unsuccessful.<ref name="MRTMCOM" />

Nato left Monaco as the leader of the Drivers' Championship with 49 points and Markelov's feature race victory moved him to second with just one point separating the two. Lynn's results allowed him to maintain third with 41 points while previous championship leader Gasly fell to fourth with 33 and Marciello was fifth with 28.<ref name="drivers" /> In the Teams' Championship, Russian Time moved from fourth to take the lead with 76 points and Racing Engineering retained second with 65 points. DAMS' results dropped them to third while Prema Racing maintained their hold on fourth. Carlin were still in fifth with eight rounds left in the season.<ref name="teams" />

stable.clean_text: The 2016 Monaco GP2 Series round was a pair of motor races held on 27 and 28 May 2016 at the Circuit de Monaco, Monte Carlo, Monaco as part of the GP2 Series. It was the fourth round of the 2016 GP2 Series and was run in support of the 2016 Monaco Grand Prix. The first race, a 40-lap feature event, was won by Russian Time driver Artem Markelov after starting from 15th position. Norman Nato finished in second for Racing Engineering and MP Motorsport's Oliver Rowland took third. Nobuharu Matsushita for ART Grand Prix won the following day's 30-lap sprint race from pole position with Carlin's Marvin Kirchhöfer and Markelov's teammate Raffaele Marciello second and third. Sergey Sirotkin won the pole position for the feature race by posting the fastest lap in qualifying but a slow start off the grid allowed Nato into the lead. Nato pulled away from the rest of the field and led for the majority of the race until a virtual safety car caught him out in the event's closing laps. Markelov was the main beneficiary of this and he made his mandatory pit stop to change tyres on the penultimate lap and held off Nato to take his maiden GP2 Series win. Matsushita started from pole position in the sprint race and repelled a challenge from the fast-starting Kirchhöfer to his right at the start. In a processional race, Matsushita opened out a 13.6 second lead over Kirchhöfer to win for the second time in GP2. The results of the round meant Nato took the lead of the Drivers' Championship by one point over feature race winner Markelov. Alex Lynn maintained third place with 41 points with Pierre Gasly and Marciello fourth and fifth. In the Teams' Championship, Russian Time moved from fourth to first and Racing Engineering were eleven points behind but they retained second place. The leaders of the Teams' Championship enterin the event DAMS dropped to third while Prema Racing fell to fourth with eight rounds left in the season. left|thumb|[[Circuit de Monaco, where the race was held.]] The 2016 Monaco GP2 Series round was the second of eleven scheduled events of the 2016 GP2 Series season. It was held on 27 and 28 May 2016 at the Circuit de Monaco in Monaco and supported the 2016 Monaco Grand Prix. Tyre supplier Pirelli brought two types of tyre to the race: two dry compounds (super soft "options" and soft "primes"). The drag reduction system (DRS) had one activation zone for the race: on the start/finish straight linking Anthony Noghes and Sainte Devote turns. There were 11 teams of 2 drivers each entered for the race for a total of 22 participants and each driver used the Dallara GP2/11 vehicle. Before the race, Prema Racing driver Pierre Gasly led the Drivers' Championship with 33 points, seven ahead of Norman Nato of Racing Engineering in second, who in turn, was a further four points in front of third-placed Alex Lynn for Russian Time. DAMS' Nicholas Latifi and Russian Time racer Artem Markelov were both tied for fourth with 20 points each. DAMS and Racing Engineering jointly led the Teams' Championship with 43 points each; Prema Racing stood in third position with 31 points and held a one-point gap over the fourth-placed Russian Time. Carlin were fifth with ten points. One 45-minute practice session was held on Thursday. Sergey Sirotkin (ART Grand Prix) was fastest with a lap of 1 minute, 20.361 seconds, almost one-tenth of a second faster than Nato in second. Lynn, Arthur Pic, Jordan King (Racing Engineering), Mitch Evans (Campos Racing), Gasly, Raffaele Marciello (Russian Time), Oliver Rowland (MP Motorsport) and Markelov occupied positions three to ten. Gasly ran wide at Sainte Devote corner, colliding with an outside barrier, and removing his front-left wheel. Soon after, Nobuharu Matsushita (ART Grand Prix) locked his tyres on the run to the same corner and went straight into the wall. Daniël de Jong (MP Motorsport) oversteered into the Sainte Devote barrier and Sean Gelael (Campos Racing) ended the session early when he stalled his car and triggered a traffic holdup. The pole position winner took four points for the Drivers' and Teams' Championships. Sirtokin clinched his maiden GP2 Series pole position with a time of 1 minute, 19.186 seconds, which was recorded in the session's closing period. He was joined on the grid's front row by Nato whose lap was seven-tenths of a second slower in the final seconds of Group A. Nato's teammate King qualified in third, Evans fourth, and Rowland fifth. Lynn, Antonio Giovinazzi (Prema Racing), Marciello, Latifi and Luca Ghiotto (Trident) rounded out the top ten qualifiers. ;Group A {| class="wikitable sortable" style="font-size:85%" |- |+Final Group A qualifying classification ! Sirtokin then repelled a challenge from King over second place. The field avoided incident on the first lap and the top three of Sirotkin, Nato and King pulled away from the fourth-placed Evans over the next few laps. On lap six, the first mandatory pit stops to change tyres were made by Giovinazzi, Ghiotto, Pic and Kirchhöfer in the lower end of the order. Meanwhile, Nato set consecutive fastest laps to give himself a lead of 1.2 seconds over Sirotkin at the start of lap 10. King was distanced by Nato and Sirotkin by lap 11 as Sirotkin made an error and cut the Novelle Chicane, losing time. Rowland was challenging the slower Evans who straight-lined the Novelle Chicane at the exit of the tunnel to keep the position. left|thumb|upright|[[Norman Nato (pictured in 2012) came second after he lost the feature race win to Artem Markelov in the last ten laps of the event.]] Meanwhile, another VSC was triggered on lap 18 when Gasly was trying to recover ground and mounted the back of Eriksson's car at La Racasse corner, which would ultimately result in Eriksson's retirement and debris was left on the track. Gasly was consequently forced to enter the pit lane to have his front wing replaced. The VSC was soon lifted after the debris was cleared from the circuit. Another VSC was activated to allow for general cleanup and racing resumed on lap 24. On the next lap, a third VSC was required when Jeffri crashed at La Rascasse corner. The VSC was lifted on lap 26. Competitive action lasted for three laps until a fourth VSC came about after King struck the barrier; he drove to the pit lane to retire. Markelov was the main beneficiary of this as he was 15 seconds ahead of Nato. He was quicker than Nato in the tunnel and Tabac turn while his rivals' pace was limited since they were slower in the tighter corners. After the restart, Markelov made his mandatory pit stop on lap 39 to switch onto the super soft tyres, emerging just ahead of Nato who could not overtake Markelov on his out-lap. Markelov's fresher tyres allowed him to hold off Nato in the last two laps for his maiden GP2 Series victory by 1 seconds. colspan="8" | Source: Sirtokin's team changed the monocoque on his car after his crash in the feature race. On the grid, Matsushita had a slow initial getaway and the second-placed Kirchhöfer was faster and drew alongside into Sainte Devote corner. However, Kirchhöfer did not move into the lead as Matsushita held him off by staying on the right and out-braking him. Behind the duo, Maricello went defensive against the fast-starting Evans and Nato went to the outside of Rowland to overtake him but he could not pass Lynn as the field were approaching Casino corner. From there on, the race settled into a rhythm as Matsushita opened his lead over Kirchhöfer to three seconds. Yellow flags were briefly waved by course officials on the fourth lap as King was about to pass Armand for 18th but he left the latter without enough space to get by and put him into a wall at the Loews hairpin. King incurred a five-second time penalty for causing the incident. On lap nine, further disruption was caused when Gelael slid wide at Sainte Devote and crashed into a barrier which activated the VSC. and ensuring four different drivers had won all four of the season's races held at the time. Kirchhöfer finished 13.660 seconds later in second in Carlin's first podium since Felipe Nasr at the 2014 Abu Dhabi Sprint race and Maricello completed the podium in third. Nato, who came second, stated that he was displeased and angry over seeing Markelov take the lead as he did not feel the need to change tyres and was urged by his engineer to pressure him, "I tried to overtake him before Turn 5 and when I saw I couldn’t do it, I just focused on the end of the race. P2 is better than nothing. This race was for us today but it’s still good points for the team and the drivers’ standings." Kirchhöfer spoke of his enjoyment racing at Monaco for the first time and had not anticipated achieving a podium result at the track, "I’m really happy with P2. I had a good gateway at the start but I couldn’t overtake Matsushita at the first corner. After that, I was thinking to bring the car home. But we are all happy, me and the team, with this result. There’s more to come!" Third-placed Marciello called his race "boring" because he found it difficult to overtake Kirchhöfer and hoped for a safety car or an extended VSC. Nevertheless, he said he was still happy and wanted to continue the form into the Baku round, "It’s still a good weekend with Artem’s P1 yesterday and my podium today but we need to improve during the qualifying. Our car is really good in the long runs and we have good pace. So our goal is to improve during the qualifying session." The technical director of Racing Engineering Sébastien Viger explained his team visited the stewards for several hours after the feature race to understand how Nato lost the chance to win, "This showed how wrong things went for us with the multiple virtual safety cars. Basically, Markelov was very lucky as he took the VSC on slow zones [of the track] where he was not losing time and Norman and others took it on fast zones, which resulted in huge time losses." Despite the VSC allowing Markelov to increase his advantage, Svetlana Strelnikova, the team principal of Russian Time, said she felt they were within the regulations, "We led by 15 seconds [over Nato], but the question is 'where did the other 15 seconds come that we could stop and rejoin first?'. But there was VSC – six seconds to turn it off, six seconds to turn it on, and each driver reacts in a different way – they press the button at the moment that the engineer says on the radio and the car is running full speed again." Valentin Khorounzhiy of motorsport.com wrote that the changes in gap were unlikely to account for Markelov's reaction time but for where on the track the VSC was activated as some drivers slowed to a different extent. He noted suggestions of protesting Markelov's victory would be unsuccessful. Nato left Monaco as the leader of the Drivers' Championship with 49 points and Markelov's feature race victory moved him to second with just one point separating the two. Lynn's results allowed him to maintain third with 41 points while previous championship leader Gasly fell to fourth with 33 and Marciello was fifth with 28. In the Teams' Championship, Russian Time moved from fourth to take the lead with 76 points and Racing Engineering retained second with 65 points. DAMS' results dropped them to third while Prema Racing maintained their hold on fourth. Carlin were still in fifth with eight rounds left in the season. ;Drivers' Championship standings {| class="wikitable" style="font-size: 85%;" |- ! scope="col" | +/– ! scope="col"| Pos ! scope="col"| Driver ! scope="col"| Points |- | 10px 1 | 1 | Norman Nato |align="right"| 49 |- | 10px 3 | 2 | Artem Markelov |align="right"| 48 |- | 10px | 3 | Alex Lynn |align="right"| 41 |- | 10px 3 | 4 | Pierre Gasly |align="right"| 33 |- | 10px 2 | 5 | Raffaele Marciello |align="right"| 28 |} ;Teams' Championship standings {|class="wikitable" style="font-size: 85%;" |- ! scope="col" | +/– ! scope="col"| Pos ! scope="col"| Driver ! scope="col"| Points |- | 10px 3 | 1 | Russian Time |align="right"| 76 |- | 10px | 2 | Racing Engineering |align="right"| 65 |- | 10px 2 | 3 | DAMS |align="right"| 61 |- | 10px 1 | 4 | Prema Racing |align="right"| 33 |- | 10px | 5 | Carlin |align="right"| 28 |} * Note: Only the top five positions are included for both sets of standings. * GP2 Monaco Monaco Category:Motorsport in Monaco
