"'''Upgrade U'''" is a song by American singer [[Beyoncé]] from her second studio album, ''[[B'Day]]'' (2006). It features additional vocals from rapper [[Jay-Z]]. It was composed by [[Swizz Beatz]], Cameron Wallace, Beyoncé, MK, [[Makeba Riddick|Makeba]], [[Sean Garrett]], [[Angela Beyincé]], [[Jay-Z]], Willie Clarke, [[Blowfly (musician)|Clarence Reid]], and Beyoncé's sister, [[Solange Knowles|Solange]]. [[Columbia Records]] released "Upgrade U" as a [[Promotional recording|promotional single]] on October 8, 2006, in the United States only. "Upgrade U" draws from the genres of [[hip hop music|hip hop]] and [[contemporary R&B|R&B]]. The concept of the song is that of a woman describing herself as the key to upgrading a man's lifestyle.

"Upgrade U" was generally well received by music critics, some praising Beyoncé's assertiveness while singing about her ability to improve her man's life (to one of extravagant luxury) with her presence. Many also praised the natural chemistry that Beyoncé and Jay-Z have in the song. After the release of ''B'Day'', "Upgrade U" started to gain popularity on R&B and hip hop radio stations in the United States. This prompted its pre-release debut on the US [[Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs]] chart and later on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] chart; after its promotional release, the song peaked at number 11 and at number 59 on the Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs and the Hot 100 charts respectively.

The music video for the song was directed by [[Melina Matsoukas]], and took one and a half days of shooting. Beyoncé did an impersonation of Jay-Z in the clip and is also seen amidst gold watches and jewelry, continuing the motif of luxury that is alluded to in the song. It finally emerged as the forty-fifth best R&B/Hip-Hop song of 2007 in the United States. Although Beyoncé did not perform "Upgrade U" in any televised appearances, the song was a part of her set list on [[The Beyoncé Experience]] tour (2007), and [[I Am... (Beyoncé tour)|I Am... World Tour]] (2009–10), as well as her and Jay Z's joint [[On the Run Tour (Beyoncé and Jay-Z)|On the Run]] (2014) and [[On the Run II Tour|On the Run II]] (2018) all-stadium tours.

==Background and release==
"Upgrade U" (or "Upgrade You") was written by Beyoncé Knowles, MK, Makeba, [[Sean Garrett]], [[Angela Beyincé]], [[Jay-Z]], Willie Clarke, [[Blowfly (musician)|Blowfly]] and [[Solange Knowles]]; with production from [[Swizz Beatz]], Cameron Wallace, and Beyoncé.<ref name="CDBooklet"/> It was recorded at [[Sony Music Studios]] in [[New York City]].<ref name="CDBooklet">{{cite AV media notes |title=B'Day deluxe edition |publisher=[[Sony Music Entertainment|Sony Music BMG Entertainment]] |year=2007}}</ref> [[T.I.]] was to be the original rap guest on the song; however, he was unable to record his verse for the song due to business reasons.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Reid |first=Shaheem |title=T.I was Beyonce's Original 'Upgrade'  |url=http://www.seefurtherthaniam.com/?p=356|publisher=See Further Than I Am |date=November 8, 2009 |access-date=January 8, 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091112081116/http://www.seefurtherthaniam.com/?p=356|archive-date=November 12, 2009}}</ref><ref name="uu">{{Cite news |last=Reid |first=Shaheem |title=Beyonce Album Preview: Harder Sound, Aggressive Songs Among B'Day's Gifts |url=http://www.mtv.com/news/articles/1539691/20060829/knowles_beyonce.jhtml |work=[[MTV News]] |date=August 30, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=December 23, 2007 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071223212109/http://www.mtv.com/news/articles/1539691/20060829/knowles_beyonce.jhtml |url-status=dead }}</ref> Ultimately, [[Jay-Z]] was selected as the featuring artist;<ref name="uu"/> "Upgrade U" becoming his second collaboration with Beyoncé on ''[[B'Day]]'' – the first being "[[Déjà Vu (Beyoncé song)|Déjà Vu]]" (2006). The song was released as a twelve-inch [[Promotional recording|promotional single]] on November 27, 2006, in the United States only.<ref name=Juno>[http://www.juno.co.uk/products/246850-01.htm " Beyoncé featuring Jay-Z – Upgrade U"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927212559/http://www.juno.co.uk/products/246850-01.htm |date=2011-09-27 }}. [[Juno Records]]. Retrieved January 8, 2011.</ref>

==Music structure, theme and lyrics==
"Upgrade U" (or "Upgrade You") is a [[hip hop music|hip hop]] song, with influences of [[pop music|pop]], [[soul music|soul]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=Beyonce – Biography |url=http://www.musicworldentertainment.com/beyonce.php |work=[[Mathew Knowles|Music World Entertainment]] |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071015051436/http://musicworldentertainment.com/beyonce.php |archive-date=October 15, 2007}}</ref> and [[contemporary R&B]].<ref name="slant"/> According to the sheet music published at ''Musicnotes.com'' by [[EMI|EMI Music Publishing]], the song is set in the [[Key (music)|key]] of [[D minor]], with a moderate [[Groove (music)|groove]] of 92 [[Tempo|beats per minute]] in [[Time signature|common time]].<ref name="MusicSheet">{{Cite book |title=Digital Sheet Music: Upgrade U  |work=Musicnotes |publisher=[[EMI|EMI Music Publishing]]}}</ref> Beyoncé's vocals range from the note of [[G (musical note)|G{{music|sharp}}<sub>3</sub>]] to [[E (musical note)|E<sub>5</sub>]].<ref name="MusicSheet"/> The song features a [[Synthesizer|synth]]-[[French horn|horn]] backdrop as well as a [[Bounce music|bounce]]-based [[Clapping|hand clap]] [[Beat (music)|beat]].<ref name="EW">{{Cite magazine |last=Rosen |first=Jody |title=Music Review: B'Day (2006)-Beyonce Knowles |url=http://ew.com/article/2007/12/21/10-best-songs-2007/ |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |date=September 1, 2007 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=December 28, 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141228194402/http://www.ew.com/ew/article/0,,20166984_2,00.html |url-status=live }}</ref> Like most of the tracks on the album, the beat is reinforced by the [[Roland TR-808]] [[drum]] machine,<ref name="Williams">{{Cite news |last=Williams |first=Chris |title=Beyonce's 'B'Day' Is Nothing to Celebrate |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/wp-dyn/content/article/2006/09/05/AR2006090501475.html |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=September 6, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=November 4, 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121104070148/http://www.washingtonpost.com/wp-dyn/content/article/2006/09/05/AR2006090501475.html |url-status=live }}</ref> while the song also features a heavy [[Bass (sound)|bass]].<ref name="wash"/> "Upgrade U" [[sampling (music)|samples]] [[Betty Wright]]'s 1968 song "[[Girls Can't Do What the Guys Do]]", composed by Willie Clarke and [[Blowfly (musician)|Clarence Reid]].<ref name="CDBooklet"/> Sal Cinquemani of ''[[Slant Magazine]]'' noted that similarities between "Upgrade U" and songs from the [[Destiny's Child]] era in the sense that it could make a "female self-empowerment" anthem.<ref name="slant">{{cite magazine |author=Sal Cinquemani |title=Beyoncé B'Day' |url=http://www.slantmagazine.com/music/review/beyonce-bday/939 |magazine=[[Slant Magazine]] |date=August 29, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=December 18, 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131218074406/http://www.slantmagazine.com/music/review/beyonce-bday/939 |url-status=live }}</ref> According to Mike Joseph of ''[[PopMatters]]'', "Upgrade U" contains "the natural chemistry" that Beyoncé and Jay-Z have in real life.<ref name="popp">{{Cite web |author=Mike Joseph |title=Beyoncé B'Day' |url=http://www.popmatters.com/pm/review/beyonce-b-day/ |work=[[PopMatters]] |date=September 11, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=November 7, 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121107015604/http://www.popmatters.com/pm/review/beyonce-b-day |url-status=live }}</ref> The concept of the song revolves around a woman offering luxuries to a man, to upgrade the lifestyle and the reputation of the latter;<ref name="Rodman">{{Cite web |last=Rodman |first=Sarah |title=Beyonce shows rage and range on new release |url=https://archive.boston.com/news/globe/living/articles/2006/09/04/beyonce_shows_rage_and_range_on_new_release/ |work=[[The Boston Globe]] |date=September 4, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=December 26, 2008 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081226102706/http://www.boston.com/news/globe/living/articles/2006/09/04/beyonce_shows_rage_and_range_on_new_release/ |url-status=live }}</ref> similar to the concept of "[[Suga Mama]]", another track from ''[[B'Day]]''.<ref name="nyt">{{Cite web |author=Jon Pareles |title=All That Success Is Hard on a Girl (or Sounds That Way) |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2006/09/04/arts/04choi.html?_r=2&oref=slogin&ref=music&pagewanted=all |work=[[The New York Times]] |date=September 4, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=January 16, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160116043657/http://www.nytimes.com/2006/09/04/arts/04choi.html?_r=2&oref=slogin&ref=music&pagewanted=all |url-status=live }}</ref> Similarly, Phil Harrison of ''[[Time Out (company)|Time Out]]'' commented that the song has "a strange business-like" quality, which seems to posit a love affair as a business arrangement.<ref name="hint"/>

{{Listen
 |filename     = Beyoncé UpgradeU.ogg
 |pos          = right
 |title        = "Upgrade U"
 |description  = A 28-second sample of "Upgrade U", featuring Beyoncé detailing how she will upgrade the lifestyle of her partner. Carrying a backdrop, consisting of [[synthesizer]]s and [[French horn|horn]]s, the song's [[Bounce music|bounce]]-based [[Clapping|hand clap]] [[Beat (music)|beat]] is amplified by the [[Roland TR-808]] [[drum]] machine over a heavy [[Bass (sound)|bass]].
}}
Throughout the song, both Jay-Z and Beyoncé [[name-dropping|name-check]] a list of goods from prestige brands, companies, and personalities: "[[Audemars Piguet]]", a Swiss watchmaker; "[[Jacob Arabo|Jacob the Jeweler]]", a celebrity jeweler; "[[Cartier SA|Cartier]]", a jeweler and watch manufacturer; "[[Hermès]]", a luxury goods company; "[[Lorraine Schwartz]]", a celebrity jeweler; "[[Ralph Lauren]] Purple Label", a higher-end division of the famous designer's clothing line; "Natura Bisse Diamond Cream", a diamond dust mixed with cream; "6 star pent suites" ([[Penthouse apartment|penthouse]]), of which only a handful exist in the world, such as at Crown Macau in China and Dreams Los Cabos near San Jose del Cabo in Mexico; "The [[Amalfi Coast]]", located in [[Southern Italy]]; "[[Fendi]]", a luxury goods company; and "The Bloomberg Luxury Accommodation Group", partly owned by the Calangian and [[Zobel de Ayala family|Zobel]] families and located around the world catering to [[Cinema of the United States|Hollywood]]-millionaire types.<ref name="rty">{{cite news|url=http://www.mtv.com/news/articles/1548533/20061219/jay_z.jhtml|title=Luxury Ties, Fancy Suites: A Guide To Beyonce And Jay-Z's 'Upgrade U'|last=Reid|first=Shaheem|author2=Cobbin-Wooten, Raslyn|date=December 20, 2006|work=[[MTV News]]|access-date=January 8, 2011|archive-date=August 14, 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100814114839/http://www.mtv.com/news/articles/1548533/20061219/jay_z.jhtml|url-status=dead}}</ref> According to Eb Haynes of [[AllHipHop]], Beyoncé displays "a lot of strength and confidence" when she mentions the assets she will gift her man to upgrade his lifestyle.<ref name="Haynes"/>

The song opens with a dialogue between Beyoncé and Jay-Z, with his [[Song structure (popular music)|verse rap]] sampling the lyrics, "How you gon' upgrade me? What's higher than number one? You know I used to beat that block. Now I be's the block."<ref name="wash"/><ref name="stylus">{{Cite web |author=Thomas Inskeep |title=Beyoncé B'Day' |url=http://www.stylusmagazine.com/reviews/beyonc/bday.htm |work=[[Stylus Magazine]] |date=September 14, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=June 4, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110604044908/http://stylusmagazine.com/reviews/beyonc/bday.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref> In the first verse, Beyoncé sings, "I hear you be the block but I'm the lights that keep the streets on".<ref name="wash">{{Cite news|access-date=January 21, 2011|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/wp-dyn/content/article/2007/05/18/AR2007051801968.html|title=Hip-Hop Hillary? She Could Use This 'Upgrade'|newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]|date=May 19, 2007|author=The Washington Post Staff Members|archive-date=November 11, 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121111201334/http://www.washingtonpost.com/wp-dyn/content/article/2007/05/18/AR2007051801968.html|url-status=live}}</ref> The [[Refrain|chorus]] samples the lyrics: "You need a real woman in your life, that's a good look [...] Take care of home and still fly, that's a good look [...] Let me upgrade you, flip a new page".<ref name="uu"/><ref name="Williams"/> In the second verse, according to Sarah Rodman of ''[[The Boston Globe]]'', Beyoncé "disturbingly conflates transforming her man into a leader" by [[Conflation|equating]] providing him with [[Veblen good]]s to "what [[Martin Luther King Jr.|Martin [Luther King Jr.]]] did for the people", and after declaring herself the equal of her man offers to "let [him] take the lead role".<ref name="Rodman"/> Describing the lyrics of the second verse as Beyoncé's "funnier and more idiosyncratic than ever", Tim Finney of ''[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]]'' commented that she boasts of the song's "extreme makeover hard-sell" as she probably knows that she is "the only R&B singer" who could deliver those lines "with a straight face."<ref name="po">{{cite web |url=http://pitchfork.com/reviews/albums/9378-bday/ |title=Review: ''B'Day'' |work=[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]] |publisher=Ryan Schreiber |author=Tim Finney |date=September 7, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=November 13, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151113152614/http://pitchfork.com/reviews/albums/9378-bday/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Before the song ends, Jay-Z performs another verse-rap<ref name="stylus"/> where he compares "the rock on his lady's finger to a tumor".<ref name="hint"/> In the third verse, Beyoncé declares to her love interest that his dynasty will ever remain incomplete without "a [[Tribal chief|chief]] like [her]".<ref name="wash"/> She then delivers the chorus again before switching to her fourth and last verse: "Hermes briefcase, Cartier top clips, silk-lined blazers" and the song ends.<ref name="Williams"/>

==Critical reception==
"Upgrade U" received positive reviews from music critics, including those who complimented the assertive way in which Beyoncé expresses her desire of upgrading her partner with valuable assets. Eb Haynes of [[AllHipHop]] called the track "the finest of the Bonnie and Clyde, Ride or Die series".<ref name="Haynes">{{cite web |last=Haynes |first=Eb |title=B'Day |url=http://allhiphop.com/stories/reviews/archive/2006/09/20/18134780.aspx |website=[[AllHipHop]] |date=September 20, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=December 9, 2008 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081209023209/http://allhiphop.com/stories/reviews/archive/2006/09/20/18134780.aspx |url-status=dead }}</ref> Sarah Rodman of ''[[The Boston Globe]]'' complimented the way that Beyoncé "declares herself an equal" and "disturbingly conflates transforming her man into a leader".<ref name="Rodman"/> Chris Williams of ''[[The Washington Post]]'' described the track a reflection of "buy-me-love sentiments".<ref name="Williams"/> Andy Kellman of [[AllMusic]] considered "Upgrade U" the "most potent track on the album", which he described as a "low-slung Cameron Wallace production where Beyoncé wears and buys the pants while making her proposition sound more like empowerment than emasculation".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kellman |first=Andy |title=B'Day |url={{AllMusic|class=album|id=r852067|pure_url=yes}} |work=[[AllMusic]] |publisher=[[Rovi Corporation]] |access-date=2008-02-10}}</ref> Tim Finney of [[Pitchfork (website)|''Pitchfork'']] called it a "stiffly blaring" track and praised its lyrics, describing them to paraphrase as eccentric in a pleasant manner.<ref name="po"/> Sal Cinquemani of [[Slant Magazine]] found "Upgrade U" could very well be a [[Destiny's Child]] track.<ref name="slant"/>

[[Bernard Zuel]] of ''[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]'' referred to it as a "mechanical" track.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Zuel |first=Bernard |title=B'Day |url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/cd-reviews/bday/2006/09/08/1157222307561.html |work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |date=September 8, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=October 25, 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121025161445/http://www.smh.com.au/news/cd-reviews/bday/2006/09/08/1157222307561.html |url-status=live }}</ref> Phil Harrison of ''[[Time Out (company)|Timeout]]'' commented that "['Upgrade U'] hints at ambivalence towards bling aspirations."<ref name="hint">{{Cite web |last=Harrison |first=Phil |title=Beyoncé: B'Day |url=http://www.timeout.com/london/music/review/audio/426/beyonce_b-day.html |work=[[Time Out (company)|Timeout]] |date=August 25, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080212004230/http://www.timeout.com/london/music/review/audio/426/beyonce_b-day.html |archive-date=February 12, 2008}}</ref> Mike Joseph of PopMatters wrote that the song survives not only due to Beyoncé's confident vocals, but also due to the chemistry that she and Jay-Z have both in the song and in real-life. He also added that Jay-Z's rhymes strongly suggest that "this rhyme animal is hungrier than ever".<ref name="popp"/> Carolyn Davis of ''[[Us Weekly|US Magazine]]'' described "Upgrade U" as a "catchy track" where Beyoncé and Jay-Z address rumors concerning "a possible engagement".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Davis |first=Carolyn |title=B-Day: Beyoncé's back and branching out with a more mature sound |url=http://www.usmagazine.com/node/560 |work=[[Us Weekly]] |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=November 13, 2006 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20061113000224/http://www.usmagazine.com/node/560 |url-status=live }}</ref>

Joy Rosen of ''[[Entertainment Weekly]]''{{'}}s listed "Upgrade U" at number five on his list of The 10 Best Songs of 2007, complimenting the collaboration of Beyoncé and Jay-Z in the song as "both halves of music's power couple pay tribute to the virtues of materialism."<ref name="EW"/> Shaheem Reid, Jayson Rodriguez and Rahman Dukes of MTV News placed the song at number 11 on his year-end list of 27 Essential R&B Songs of 2007.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mtv.com/news/articles/1576858/t-pain-beyonce-more-our-fave-rb-acts-07.jhtml|title=T-Pain, Beyonce, Justin Timberlake And More Are Our Favorite R&B Acts Of 2007|work=MTV News|first1=Shaheem|last1=Reid|first2=Jayson|last2=Rodriguez|first3=Rahman|last3=Dukes|date=December 20, 2007|access-date=May 30, 2013|archive-date=October 21, 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131021190748/http://www.mtv.com/news/articles/1576858/t-pain-beyonce-more-our-fave-rb-acts-07.jhtml|url-status=dead}}</ref> On their ranking of the best singles of aughts, ''[[Slant Magazine]]'' included the song at number 168.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.slantmagazine.com/house/article/rest-of-the-best-of-the-aughts-albums-singles-101-250|title=Beyonce: Rest of the Best of the Aughts|work=[[Slant Magazine]]|date=February 6, 2010|access-date=January 6, 2017|archive-date=June 2, 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160602004733/http://www.slantmagazine.com/house/article/rest-of-the-best-of-the-aughts-albums-singles-101-250|url-status=live}}</ref>"Upgrade U" was nominated in the category of Best Duet/Collaboration at the 2007 [[BET Awards]], but lost to "[[Runaway Love (Ludacris song)|Runaway Love]]" by [[Ludacris]] (featuring [[Mary J. Blige]]).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://randb.about.com/od/musicawardsshows/a/2007BETAwards.htm|title=2007 BET Awards Winners|author=Mark Edward Nero|work=[[About.com]]|date=June 27, 2007|access-date=January 8, 2011|archive-date=April 13, 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130413182550/http://randb.about.com/od/musicawardsshows/a/2007BETAwards.htm|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Chart performance==
After the release of ''[[B'Day]]'', and before "Upgrade U"'s official release as a single, the song gained popularity on R&B and hip hop radio stations in the United States.<ref>Caulfield, Keith. [http://www.billboard.com/articles/news/56340/ask-billboard "Ask Billboard – Replacing Irreplaceable"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140918042136/http://www.billboard.com/articles/news/56340/ask-billboard |date=2014-09-18 }}. ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]''. December 29, 2006. Retrieved January 8, 2011.</ref> Consequently, it entered the US [[Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs]] chart, and later the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] chart at number 92 in the issue dated November 18, 2006.<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://www.billboard.com/charts/2006-11-18/hot-100?order=gainer |title=Hot 100 – Week of November 18, 2006 |magazine=[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]] |date=November 18, 2006 |access-date=January 8, 2011}}</ref> After its release as a promotional single, it reached a peak at number fifty-nine on the Hot 100 chart and a peak at number 11 on the R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart.<ref name=Billboarddotcom>[{{BillboardURLbyName|artist=beyoncé|chart=Hot 100}} "Beyoncé – Artist Chart History – Singles"]. ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]''. Retrieved January 8, 2011.</ref> The song re-entered the "Hot 100" chart on four occasions: it first fell out of the chart on January 13, 2007; re-entered on January 20, 2007, at number 94 and charted for two weeks only;<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://www.billboard.com/charts/2007-01-20/hot-100?order=gainer |title=Hot 100 – Week of January 20, 2007 |magazine=[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]] |date=January 20, 2007 |access-date=January 8, 2011}}</ref> it re-entered the chart on February 10, 2007, and was given the title of 'Best Comeback', though it fell off the chart the following week;<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://www.billboard.com/charts/2007-02-10/hot-100?order=gainer |title=Hot 100 – Week of February 10, 2007 |magazine=[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]] |date=February 10, 2007 |access-date=January 8, 2011}}</ref> it re-entered seven weeks later on March 24, 2007, at number 98, and stayed there for two weeks;<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://www.billboard.com/charts/2007-03-24/hot-100?order=gainer |title=Hot 100 – Week of March 24, 2007 |magazine=[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]] |date=March 24, 2007 |access-date=January 8, 2011}}</ref> The song made its last comeback on the Hot 100 chart on April 14, 2007, at number eighty, once again gaining the title of the 'Best Comeback'. This time it remained in the chart for five weeks.<ref>{{cite magazine|url=http://www.billboard.com/charts/2007-04-14/hot-100?order=gainer |title=Hot 100 – April 14, 2007 |magazine=[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]] |date=April 14, 2007 |access-date=January 8, 2011}}</ref> Overall, it remained on the Hot 100 for eighteen non-consecutive weeks.<ref name=Billboarddotcom/> Although not officially released in the United Kingdom, "Upgrade U" charted for three weeks on the [[UK Singles Chart]] and peaked at number 176 on May 19, 2007.<ref name=zobbel>{{cite web|author=Zywietz, Tobias|url=http://zobbel.de/cluk/CLUK_K.HTM|title=Chart Log UK: Alex K – Kyuss|work=Zobbel|access-date=January 8, 2011|archive-date=March 12, 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120312080813/http://www.zobbel.de/cluk/CLUK_K.HTM|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Music video==
[[Image:Upgrade u video.jpg|thumb|right|Beyoncé doing her impersonation of Jay-Z. (''left'')]]
The music video for "Upgrade U" was shot during the two-week filming for the ''[[B'Day Anthology Video Album]]''. It was [[music video director|directed]] by [[Melina Matsoukas]] and Beyoncé herself. The video was shot between those of "Kitty Kat" and "[[Green Light (Beyoncé song)|Green Light]]", and took about one and half days of filming. [[Jay-Z]] performed his rap scenes first,  afterwards Beyoncé studied his scene, and then did her impersonation of him. Melina said: "If anybody knows Jay-Z, it would be Beyoncé".<ref name=behind>{{Cite web |author=Jennifer Vineyard |title=Beyonce: Behind The B'Day Videos |url=http://www.mtv.com/bands/b/beyonce/videos_07/news_feature_040207/index3.jhtml |work=[[MTV News]] |access-date=January 8, 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110624084117/http://www.mtv.com/bands/b/beyonce/videos_07/news_feature_040207/index3.jhtml|archive-date=June 24, 2011 }}</ref> Beyoncé commented on her impersonation of Jay-Z on [[MTV]], "I love what I did in ['Upgrade U'] because it's completely out of my character, or at least the character that people think I am. I was pretending to be Jay[-Z], and he was there, and I told him he had to leave, because I couldn't do it with him in the room — it was way too embarrassing. I think I did a pretty good job. I had the lip curl down!"<ref name=behind /> The video premiered on February 28, 2007, on BET's ''[[106 & Park]]'', with the video for Beyoncé's other single release, "[[Beautiful Liar]]" (2007), a duet with [[Shakira]], premiering the same day on ''[[Total Request Live]]'' (TRL).<ref>{{cite web |access-date=January 8, 2011 |url=http://english.turkcebilgi.com/Beyonce |title=Information about Beyonce |publisher=English TurkCebilgi |archive-date=July 17, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110717113124/http://english.turkcebilgi.com/Beyonce |url-status=live }}</ref>

In the beginning of the video, Beyoncé is seen mouthing the words of Jay-Z's lyrics, dressed in masculine [[hip hop fashion|hip-hop style clothing]]. She is later seen wearing vintage Cazal 907 sunglasses while singing in the backseat of a [[Rolls-Royce Silver Cloud#Silver Cloud III|Rolls-Royce Silver Cloud III]]. During the choruses she dances in a gold minidress, in front of a group of male back-up dancers. She continues the Jay-Z imitation through his rapped verse and, halfway through it, Jay-Z appears to finish the rap himself with Beyoncé dancing around him, wearing a classic white dress-shirt. Beyoncé is seen amidst gold watches and jewelry, continuing the motif of luxury that is alluded to in the song. The music video for "Upgrade U" peaked at number six on [[BET]]'s ''106 & Park'' while it peaked at number one in the United Kingdom on [[MTV Base (United Kingdom and Ireland)|MTV Base]]'s ''Chart Show'' on May 16, 2007.<ref>{{cite news|title=Beyonce Sets Billboard Chart Record as 'Beautiful Liar'|publisher=[[PR Newswire]]|date=March 29, 2007}}</ref> The music video has been used as a commercial to advertise high definition satellite television from [[DirecTV]];<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.avclub.com/have-you-no-shame-direct-tv-1798218229|title=Have You No Shame, Direct TV?|work=[[The A.V. Club]]|publisher=[[The Onion]]|date=November 2, 2009|author=Amelie Gillette|access-date=January 8, 2011|archive-date=October 14, 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111014231043/http://www.avclub.com/articles/have-you-no-shame-direct-tv%2C34875/|url-status=live}}</ref> though portions of the video were re-shot for her announcement.<ref>{{cite web|access-date=January 8, 2011|url=http://michigan.scout.com/a.z?s=162&p=2&c=705819|title=Cavalcade of Whimsy – The Coaching Situations|publisher=[[Scout.com]]|date=November 27, 2007|author=Pete Fiutak|archive-date=July 16, 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110716021533/http://michigan.scout.com/a.z?s=162&p=2&c=705819|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Live performances and covers==
[[File:Beyonce live 06.jpg|thumb|right|Beyoncé performing "Upgrade U" on [[The Beyoncé Experience]] with several male backup dancers.]]
Although Beyoncé did not perform the song in any televised appearances, it was a part of her set list on [[The Beyoncé Experience]] with Jay-Z in [[Los Angeles]] on September 2, 2007, and her worldwide [[I Am... (Beyoncé tour)|I Am... World Tour]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://perezhilton.com/tv/Beyonce_Diva_live_from_the_I_Am_World_Tour_DVD/?ptvid=08a3270cd5c8c&category_id=newest&category_start=0&category_end=4 |title=Beyoncé – Diva (live from the "I Am..." World Tour DVD) |publisher=Perez TV |date=November 15, 2010 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101116155809/http://perezhilton.com/tv/Beyonce_Diva_live_from_the_I_Am_World_Tour_DVD/?ptvid=08a3270cd5c8c&category_id=newest&category_start=0&category_end=4 |archive-date=November 16, 2010 |url-status=dead |df=mdy-all }}</ref> When Beyoncé performed the song in [[Sunrise, Florida]], on June 29, 2009, she was wearing a glittering gold leotard. As she sang, animated graphics of turntables, faders, and other nightclub equipment pulsed behind her, her dancers and the musicians.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.news-record.com/content/2009/06/28/article/beyonce_is_fierce_talent |title=Review: Beyonce is ... Fierce talent |author=Parke Puterbaugh |publisher=New Records |date=June 29, 2009 |access-date=January 10, 2011 |archive-date=March 8, 2012 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120308121341/http://www.news-record.com/content/2009/06/28/article/beyonce_is_fierce_talent |url-status=dead }}</ref> Beyoncé was accompanied by two drummers, two keyboardists, a percussionist, a horn section, three backup vocalists (collectively called Suga Mama), and guitarist Bibi McGill.<ref name="Self">{{cite web|access-date=January 10, 2011|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2009/06/23/arts/music/23beyonce.html?_r=1|title=Flash, Concepts and, Yes, Songs|author=Ben Ratliff|work=[[USA Today]]|date=June 22, 2009|archive-date=September 29, 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130929120917/http://www.nytimes.com/2009/06/23/arts/music/23beyonce.html?_r=1|url-status=live}}</ref> "Upgrade U" was included as the fourteenth and twenty-second tracks on her live albums ''[[The Beyoncé Experience Live]]'' (2007)<ref>{{cite web |url=https://music.apple.com/be/album/the-beyonce-experience-live/268305666 |title=The Beyoncé Experience (Live) [Audio Version] |work=Itunes Belgium |language=nl |publisher=[[Apple Inc.]] |date=November 19, 2007 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=November 11, 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121111011518/https://itunes.apple.com/be/album/the-beyonce-experience-live/id268305666 |url-status=live }}</ref> and ''[[I Am... (Beyoncé tour)|I Am... World Tour]]'' (2010), respectively.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://itunes.apple.com/be/preorder/i-am-world-tour/id399896699 |title=I Am...World Tour |work=Itunes Belgium |language=nl |publisher=[[Apple Inc.]] |date=November 26, 2010 |access-date=January 8, 2011 |archive-date=April 24, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150424172750/https://itunes.apple.com/be/preorder/i-am-world-tour/id399896699 |url-status=live }}</ref> "Upgrade U" was part of the set list of Beyoncé and Jay-Z's co-headlining [[On the Run Tour (Beyoncé and Jay-Z)|On the Run Tour]] (2014).<ref name="cos">{{cite magazine|url=https://consequence.net/2014/06/jay-z-and-beyonce-kick-off-on-the-run-tour-with-massive-42-song-setlist/|title=Jay Z and Beyoncé kick off On the Run Tour with massive 42-song setlist|magazine=[[Consequence of Sound]]|first=Alex|last=Young|date=June 26, 2014|access-date=June 26, 2014|archive-date=October 6, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211006192355/https://consequence.net/2014/06/jay-z-and-beyonce-kick-off-on-the-run-tour-with-massive-42-song-setlist/|url-status=live}}</ref>

[[2 Pistols]]' debut single "[[She Got It]]", which features [[T-Pain]] and the phrase "let me upgrade u", was included on his 2008 debut album, ''[[Death Before Dishonor (album)|Death Before Dishonor]]''. Additionally, [[Fabolous]]' "[[Make Me Better]]" featuring [[Ne-Yo]], released on his ''[[From Nothin' to Somethin']]'' album, uses the phrase "front page ya". Other songs that have made lyrical references to  "Upgrade U", include: [[Lil Mama|Lil' Mama]]'s "[[Lip Gloss (song)|Lip Gloss]]",<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://genius.com/Lil-mama-lip-gloss-lyrics|title=Lip Gloss|access-date=2022-03-05|archive-date=2022-03-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220305101704/https://genius.com/Lil-mama-lip-gloss-lyrics|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Kanye West]]'s first verse in the freestyle of [[Rich Boy]]'s "[[Throw Some D's]]",<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://genius.com/Kanye-west-throw-some-ds-lyrics|title=Throw Some D's|access-date=2022-03-05|archive-date=2022-03-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220305101710/https://genius.com/Kanye-west-throw-some-ds-lyrics|url-status=live}}</ref> and [[Bow Wow (rapper)|Bow Wow]]'s rap verse on the remix of [[Paula DeAnda]]'s "[[Easy (Paula DeAnda song)|Easy]]".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://genius.com/Paula-deanda-easy-single-version-lyrics|title=Easy (Single version)|access-date=2022-03-05|archive-date=2022-03-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220305101707/https://genius.com/Paula-deanda-easy-single-version-lyrics|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Lil Wayne]] performed a freestyle titled "Upgrade" over the beat of "Upgrade U" on his mixtape ''[[Da Drought 3]] ''that was later included on Nicki Minaj's mixtape "Playtime Is Over".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://genius.com/Lil-wayne-upgrade-lyrics|title=Upgrade|access-date=2022-03-05|archive-date=2022-03-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220305101706/https://genius.com/Lil-wayne-upgrade-lyrics|url-status=live}}</ref> Beyoncé's former [[Destiny's Child]] group member [[LeToya Luckett]] also referenced this song on a bonus track titled "Swagger" on her second album "[[Lady Love (LeToya album)|Lady Love]]", which features the lyric: 'like bee say, 'let me upgrade ya'.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://genius.com/Letoya-luckett-swagger-lyrics|title=Swagger|access-date=2022-03-05|archive-date=2022-03-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220305101704/https://genius.com/Letoya-luckett-swagger-lyrics|url-status=live}}</ref>

stable.clean_text: {{Redirect|Upgrade (song)|the Be More Chill song|Upgrade (Be More Chill song)"Upgrade" (Be More Chill song)}} {{Infobox song | name = Upgrade U | cover = Beyonce - Upgrade U (single).png | alt = | type = Promotional single | artist = Beyoncé featuring Jay-Z | album = B'Day | released = | recorded = 2006 | studio = Sony Music, New York City | genre = *Hip hop *R&B | length = 4:32 | label = Columbia | writer = * Beyoncé Knowles * Angela Beyincé * Solange Knowles * Makeba Riddick * Sean Garrett * Shawn Carter * Willie Clarke * Clarence Reid | producer = *Swizz Beatz *Cameron Wallace *Beyoncé *Sean Garrett | misc = {{External music video|type=song|}} }} "Upgrade U" is a song by American singer Beyoncé from her second studio album, B'Day (2006). It features additional vocals from rapper Jay-Z. It was composed by Swizz Beatz, Cameron Wallace, Beyoncé, MK, Makeba, Sean Garrett, Angela Beyincé, Jay-Z, Willie Clarke, Clarence Reid, and Beyoncé's sister, Solange. Columbia Records released "Upgrade U" as a promotional single on October 8, 2006, in the United States only. "Upgrade U" draws from the genres of hip hop and R&B. The concept of the song is that of a woman describing herself as the key to upgrading a man's lifestyle. "Upgrade U" was generally well received by music critics, some praising Beyoncé's assertiveness while singing about her ability to improve her man's life (to one of extravagant luxury) with her presence. Many also praised the natural chemistry that Beyoncé and Jay-Z have in the song. After the release of B'Day, "Upgrade U" started to gain popularity on R&B and hip hop radio stations in the United States. This prompted its pre-release debut on the US Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart and later on the US Billboard Hot 100 chart; after its promotional release, the song peaked at number 11 and at number 59 on the Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs and the Hot 100 charts respectively. The music video for the song was directed by Melina Matsoukas, and took one and a half days of shooting. Beyoncé did an impersonation of Jay-Z in the clip and is also seen amidst gold watches and jewelry, continuing the motif of luxury that is alluded to in the song. It finally emerged as the forty-fifth best R&B/Hip-Hop song of 2007 in the United States. Although Beyoncé did not perform "Upgrade U" in any televised appearances, the song was a part of her set list on The Beyoncé Experience tour (2007), and I Am... World Tour (2009–10), as well as her and Jay Z's joint On the Run (2014) and On the Run II (2018) all-stadium tours. "Upgrade U" (or "Upgrade You") was written by Beyoncé Knowles, MK, Makeba, Sean Garrett, Angela Beyincé, Jay-Z, Willie Clarke, Blowfly and Solange Knowles; with production from Swizz Beatz, Cameron Wallace, and Beyoncé. T.I. was to be the original rap guest on the song; however, he was unable to record his verse for the song due to business reasons. Ultimately, Jay-Z was selected as the featuring artist; "Upgrade U" (or "Upgrade You") is a hip hop song, with influences of pop, soul, and contemporary R&B. Beyoncé's vocals range from the note of G3 to E5. Like most of the tracks on the album, the beat is reinforced by the Roland TR-808 drum machine, while the song also features a heavy bass. According to Mike Joseph of PopMatters, "Upgrade U" contains "the natural chemistry" that Beyoncé and Jay-Z have in real life. The concept of the song revolves around a woman offering luxuries to a man, to upgrade the lifestyle and the reputation of the latter; similar to the concept of "Suga Mama", another track from B'Day. Similarly, Phil Harrison of Time Out commented that the song has "a strange business-like" quality, which seems to posit a love affair as a business arrangement. According to Eb Haynes of AllHipHop, Beyoncé displays "a lot of strength and confidence" when she mentions the assets she will gift her man to upgrade his lifestyle. In the first verse, Beyoncé sings, "I hear you be the block but I'm the lights that keep the streets on". The chorus samples the lyrics: "You need a real woman in your life, that's a good look [...] Take care of home and still fly, that's a good look [...] Let me upgrade you, flip a new page". Before the song ends, Jay-Z performs another verse-rap Sarah Rodman of The Boston Globe complimented the way that Beyoncé "declares herself an equal" and "disturbingly conflates transforming her man into a leader". Tim Finney of Pitchfork called it a "stiffly blaring" track and praised its lyrics, describing them to paraphrase as eccentric in a pleasant manner. Phil Harrison of Timeout commented that "['Upgrade U'] hints at ambivalence towards bling aspirations." Mike Joseph of PopMatters wrote that the song survives not only due to Beyoncé's confident vocals, but also due to the chemistry that she and Jay-Z have both in the song and in real-life. He also added that Jay-Z's rhymes strongly suggest that "this rhyme animal is hungrier than ever". Joy Rosen of Entertainment Weeklys listed "Upgrade U" at number five on his list of The 10 Best Songs of 2007, complimenting the collaboration of Beyoncé and Jay-Z in the song as "both halves of music's power couple pay tribute to the virtues of materialism." On their ranking of the best singles of aughts, Slant Magazine included the song at number 168."Upgrade U" was nominated in the category of Best Duet/Collaboration at the 2007 BET Awards, but lost to "Runaway Love" by Ludacris (featuring Mary J. Blige). After the release of B'Day, and before "Upgrade U"'s official release as a single, the song gained popularity on R&B and hip hop radio stations in the United States. Consequently, it entered the US Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart, and later the US Billboard Hot 100 chart at number 92 in the issue dated November 18, 2006. After its release as a promotional single, it reached a peak at number fifty-nine on the Hot 100 chart and a peak at number 11 on the R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart. The song re-entered the "Hot 100" chart on four occasions: it first fell out of the chart on January 13, 2007; re-entered on January 20, 2007, at number 94 and charted for two weeks only; it re-entered the chart on February 10, 2007, and was given the title of 'Best Comeback', though it fell off the chart the following week; it re-entered seven weeks later on March 24, 2007, at number 98, and stayed there for two weeks; The song made its last comeback on the Hot 100 chart on April 14, 2007, at number eighty, once again gaining the title of the 'Best Comeback'. This time it remained in the chart for five weeks. Overall, it remained on the Hot 100 for eighteen non-consecutive weeks. thumb|right|Beyoncé doing her impersonation of Jay-Z. (left) The music video for "Upgrade U" was shot during the two-week filming for the B'Day Anthology Video Album. It was directed by Melina Matsoukas and Beyoncé herself. The video was shot between those of "Kitty Kat" and "Green Light", and took about one and half days of filming. Jay-Z performed his rap scenes first, afterwards Beyoncé studied his scene, and then did her impersonation of him. Melina said: "If anybody knows Jay-Z, it would be Beyoncé". Beyoncé commented on her impersonation of Jay-Z on MTV, "I love what I did in ['Upgrade U'] because it's completely out of my character, or at least the character that people think I am. I was pretending to be Jay[-Z], and he was there, and I told him he had to leave, because I couldn't do it with him in the room — it was way too embarrassing. I think I did a pretty good job. I had the lip curl down!" In the beginning of the video, Beyoncé is seen mouthing the words of Jay-Z's lyrics, dressed in masculine hip-hop style clothing. She is later seen wearing vintage Cazal 907 sunglasses while singing in the backseat of a Rolls-Royce Silver Cloud III. During the choruses she dances in a gold minidress, in front of a group of male back-up dancers. She continues the Jay-Z imitation through his rapped verse and, halfway through it, Jay-Z appears to finish the rap himself with Beyoncé dancing around him, wearing a classic white dress-shirt. Beyoncé is seen amidst gold watches and jewelry, continuing the motif of luxury that is alluded to in the song. The music video for "Upgrade U" peaked at number six on BET's 106 & Park while it peaked at number one in the United Kingdom on MTV Base's Chart Show on May 16, 2007. The music video has been used as a commercial to advertise high definition satellite television from DirecTV; though portions of the video were re-shot for her announcement. thumb|right|Beyoncé performing "Upgrade U" on [[The Beyoncé Experience with several male backup dancers.]] Although Beyoncé did not perform the song in any televised appearances, it was a part of her set list on The Beyoncé Experience with Jay-Z in Los Angeles on September 2, 2007, and her worldwide I Am... World Tour. When Beyoncé performed the song in Sunrise, Florida, on June 29, 2009, she was wearing a glittering gold leotard. As she sang, animated graphics of turntables, faders, and other nightclub equipment pulsed behind her, her dancers and the musicians. Beyoncé was accompanied by two drummers, two keyboardists, a percussionist, a horn section, three backup vocalists (collectively called Suga Mama), and guitarist Bibi McGill. "Upgrade U" was included as the fourteenth and twenty-second tracks on her live albums The Beyoncé Experience Live (2007) and I Am... World Tour (2010), respectively. "Upgrade U" was part of the set list of Beyoncé and Jay-Z's co-headlining On the Run Tour (2014). 2 Pistols' debut single "She Got It", which features T-Pain and the phrase "let me upgrade u", was included on his 2008 debut album, Death Before Dishonor. Additionally, Fabolous' "Make Me Better" featuring Ne-Yo, released on his From Nothin' to Somethin album, uses the phrase "front page ya". Other songs that have made lyrical references to "Upgrade U", include: Lil' Mama's "Lip Gloss", Kanye West's first verse in the freestyle of Rich Boy's "Throw Some D's", and Bow Wow's rap verse on the remix of Paula DeAnda's "Easy". Lil Wayne performed a freestyle titled "Upgrade" over the beat of "Upgrade U" on his mixtape Da Drought 3 that was later included on Nicki Minaj's mixtape "Playtime Is Over". Beyoncé's former Destiny's Child group member LeToya Luckett also referenced this song on a bonus track titled "Swagger" on her second album "Lady Love", which features the lyric: 'like bee say, 'let me upgrade ya'. *12" single | style="text-align:center;"| 28 |- |- |} {|class="wikitable sortable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center;" |- !scope="col"| Chart (2007) !scope="col"| Position |- !scope="row"| Romania (Romanian Top 100) |68 |- !scope="row"| US Hot R&B/Hip Hop Songs (Billboard) | 45 |} * Category:2006 singles Category:Beyoncé songs Category:Jay-Z songs Category:Music videos directed by Melina Matsoukas Category:Song recordings produced by Swizz Beatz Category:Songs written by Sean Garrett Category:Songs written by Beyoncé Category:American hip-hop songs Category:Songs written by Solange Knowles Category:Songs written by Makeba Riddick Category:Song recordings produced by Beyoncé Category:2006 songs Category:Songs written by Blowfly (musician) Category:Songs written by Willie Clarke (songwriter) Category:American contemporary R&B songs

'''Rafsanjan''' ({{langx|fa|رفسنجان}} {{IPA|fa|ɹæfsænˈd͡ʒɒːn|}}){{efn|Also [[romanize]]d as '''Rafsanjān'''; also known as '''Bahrāmābād''' and '''Rafsinjān'''<ref>{{GEOnet3|-3080584}}</ref>}} is a city in the [[Central District (Rafsanjan County)|Central District]] of [[Rafsanjan County]], [[Kerman province|Kerman province, Iran]], serving as capital of both the county and the district.<ref name="Kerman Province Structure">{{cite report|title=Approval of the organization and chain of citizenship of the elements and units of the national divisions of Kerman province, centered in the city of Kerman|language=fa|website=lamtakam.com|via=Lam ta Kam|url=https://lamtakam.com/law/council_of_ministers/113033|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240130210435/https://lamtakam.com/law/council_of_ministers/113033|publisher=Ministry of the Interior, Defense Political Commission of the Government Council|last=Habibi|first=Hassan|archive-date=30 January 2024|date=c. 2024|orig-date=Approved 21 June 1369|id=Proposal 3233.1.5.53; Letter 907-93808; Notification 82812/T139K|access-date=30 January 2024}}</ref>

==Demographics==
===Population===
At the time of the 2006 National Census, the city's population was 136,388 in 33,489 households.<ref name="2006 Kerman Province">{{cite report|title=Census of the Islamic Republic of Iran, 1385 (2006): Kerman Province|language=fa|publisher=The Statistical Center of Iran|website=amar.org.ir|url=http://www.amar.org.ir/DesktopModules/FTPManager/upload/upload2360/newjkh/newjkh/08.xls|access-date=25 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110920093605/http://www.amar.org.ir/DesktopModules/FTPManager/upload/upload2360/newjkh/newjkh/08.xls|format=Excel|archive-date=20 September 2011}}</ref> The following census in 2011 counted 151,420 people in 39,281 households.<ref name="2011 Kerman Province">{{cite report|title=Census of the Islamic Republic of Iran, 1390 (2011): Kerman Province|language=fa|publisher=The Statistical Center of Iran|website=irandataportal.syr.edu|via=Iran Data Portal, Syracuse University|url=https://irandataportal.syr.edu/wp-content/uploads/Kerman.xls|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230329231518/https://irandataportal.syr.edu/wp-content/uploads/Kerman.xls|archive-date=29 March 2023|access-date=19 December 2022|format=Excel}}</ref> The 2016 census measured the population of the city as 161,909 people in 47,013 households.<ref name="2016 Kerman Province">{{cite report|title=Census of the Islamic Republic of Iran, 1395 (2016): Kerman Province|language=fa|publisher=The Statistical Center of Iran|website=amar.org.ir|url=https://www.amar.org.ir/Portals/0/census/1395/results/abadi/CN95_HouseholdPopulationVillage_08.xlsx|access-date=19 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020091047/https://www.amar.org.ir/Portals/0/census/1395/results/abadi/CN95_HouseholdPopulationVillage_08.xlsx|format=Excel|archive-date=20 October 2020}}</ref>

Rafsanjan is the biggest producer of [[pistachio]]s in the world. The [[Sarcheshmeh]] copper mines are among the largest in the world. In addition, the biggest and oldest mud-house in the world is in Rafsanjan. The city is also a major center of [[carpet]] production, although the rugs are sold as [[Kerman]]i rugs rather than Rafsanjani ones.

==Etymology== 
The origin of the name Rafsanjan is believed to have roots in [[Old Persian]]. According to one theory,{{which|date=December 2024}} the name is derived from the words "Rafsan" and "Kan," which in Old Persian mean "copper" and "mine," respectively. These two words gave rise to the original name of the city, "Rafsangan," which later transformed into its current [[Arabic]] version, "Rafsanjan."

Another theory suggests that the original name of the city was "Sanjan." Due to its location in the path of floods, the city suffered significant destruction and was renamed "Raft Sanjan" and then "Rafsanjan" among the people. It changed from “Raf” to “Raft”; adding a letter changes the meaning of the word in [[Persian language|Farsi]] to “sweeping”, signifying the damage of the grand rapids. According to a [[legend]], the city was renamed "Rafsanjan" after a massive [[Rapids|rapid]] occurred in the area, resulting in the change from "Sanjan" to "Rafsanjan."

==History==
{{Unreferenced section|date=January 2023}}
About the origin and creation of this city there are significant rhetorical stories. At the period of Qajar kingdom and under Naser-Aldin-Shah's reign, Rafsanjan was named az “Anas” and was part of Fars province; after a while it came under the control of Kerman’s government. In the history, Rafsanjan has been named as an important city due to being on the crossroad between Kerman and Yazd. At the end of Safavieh kingdom, Afghans attacked this city and caused disaster and catastrophe. The low rate of prosperity after mentioned attack was stretched until Qajar’s kingdom and the city was almost ruined until that time. In 1787, Agha Mohammad Khan Qajar attacked the  city and people decided to acquiesce in order to end the war, so he conquered the city without violence. In 1866, Ebrahim Khan Zahiradole (the
governor of Kerman) ordered to reshape and rebuild the city. Buildings were constructed and the situation started to improve. In 1913, Amir Mofkhem Bakhtiari ordered to build a strong wall around the city which part of ruins still remain today. This step caused importance and accredit to the city. For the first time in 1938, crossroads were built in the city, which are named as Emam Khomeyni, Enghelab, and Shohada these days.
Rafsanjan was also an important city due to high production of cotton earlier on time. Because of the high quality of this product, it was exported to India and Russia. In 1945, Rafsanjan was assigned as a 
township. Nowadays, Rafsanjan is considered as a of the most crowded cities of Kerman with major industries, oil depots, and agricultural mega-farms  that have an undeniable role in the economy of province and country. Such mineral and agricultural products are well-known not only in Iran, but all around the world.

==Geography==
Rafsanjan is located in south part of the [[Lut desert]], in north-west of [[Kerman province]]. The longitude of this city is 56 degrees east and the latitude is 30 degrees south. The Average distance between Kerman and this city is {{convert|110|km|mi|abbr=on}}. The city has an airport and railway (Tehran-Bandar Abbas route). Moreover, the altitude is {{convert|1460|m|ft|abbr=on}} and the land-measurement is approximately {{convert|10687|km|mi|abbr=on}}. North part of Rafsanjan has a common frontier with [[Bafgh]] and [[Zarand, Iran|Zarand]]; south part is neighboring with [[Bardsir]] and on west side with [[Anar, Iran|Anar]] and [[Shahre-Babak]]; finally east side of this city is neighboring with [[Kerman]] and Zarand. Rafsanjan has two seasonal rivers named as [[Shour]] and [[Giouderi]]. The mountains in the area are part of [[Zagros range]], and [[Sarcheshmeh]] and [[Davaran (mountain)|Davaran]] are the most famous. 

The underground lake [[Chah Darya]] is located in Rafsanjan.<ref>{{cite web |title=Where Legends Prevail and Rivers Meet {{!}} FinancialTribune |url=https://financialtribune.com/articles/travel/9435/where-legends-prevail-and-rivers-meet |website=financialtribune.com |access-date=23 May 2025 |language=en |date=20 January 2015}}</ref>

===Climate===
{{POV section|date=May 2023|talk=POV Climate}}
Rafsanjan has cold and chilly winters as well as very hot and dry summers. This city is located in the central part of Iran in the arid climate zone. Days are warm and nights are cold in general. The average amount of precipitation is {{convert|90|mm|in|abbr=on}} annually.

}}

=== Environment ===
Rafsanjan has been exposed to the polluting smoke of [[Sarcheshme]], [[Khatunabad]] and [[Shahrbabak]] copper smelters. On the other hand, up to 14 pistachio orchards are sprayed every year, as a result, dozens of tons of poison are released in the pistachio orchards of Rafsanjan. The level of arsenic contamination in Rafsanjan water is about ten times the permissible limit, and it is strongly recommended to use authorized water purification devices for drinking water, and to ensure the health of the water purification device, the water produced by the device should also be tested.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Mirzaei Aminiyan |first1=Milad |last2=Baalousha |first2=Mohammed |last3=Mousavi |first3=Rouhollah |last4=Mirzaei Aminiyan |first4=Farzad |last5=Hosseini |first5=Hamideh |last6=Heydariyan |first6=Amin |date=May 2018 |title=The ecological risk, source identification, and pollution assessment of heavy metals in road dust: a case study in Rafsanjan, SE Iran |journal=Environmental Science and Pollution Research International |volume=25 |issue=14 |pages=13382–13395 |doi=10.1007/s11356-017-8539-y |issn=1614-7499 |pmid=28255819|bibcode=2018ESPR...2513382M |s2cid=4036346 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Hakimi |first1=Hamid |last2=Ahmadi |first2=Jafar |last3=Vakilian |first3=Alireza |last4=Jamalizadeh |first4=Ahmad |last5=Kamyab |first5=Zahra |last6=Mehran |first6=Mahya |last7=Malekzadeh |first7=Reza |last8=Poustchi |first8=Hossein |last9=Eghtesad |first9=Sareh |last10=Sardari |first10=Farimah |last11=Soleimani |first11=Mohammadreza |last12=Khademalhosseini |first12=Morteza |last13=Abolghasemi |first13=Mohammadreza |last14=Mohammadi |first14=Movahedeh |last15=Sadeghi |first15=Tabandeh |date=2021-02-01 |title=The profile of Rafsanjan Cohort Study |url=https://doi.org/10.1007/s10654-020-00668-7 |journal=European Journal of Epidemiology |language=en |volume=36 |issue=2 |pages=243–252 |doi=10.1007/s10654-020-00668-7 |pmid=32725579 |s2cid=220809495 |issn=1573-7284|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

===Flora and fauna===
Wild plantations and trees include [[common fig]] and [[almond]]s. Wild animals which are living in mountainous areas are [[goat]]s, [[sheep|ewe]]s, [[gazelle]]s, [[wolf|wolves]], [[hyena]]s, [[African wildcat|wildcat]]s and some species of birds like [[rock dove|pigeon]]s, [[eagle]]s and [[partridge]]s.

==Pistachio industry==
Rafsanjan, a semiarid region in central Iran, is renowned for its high-quality pistachio production, generating nearly $1 billion annually.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web |last= |first= |title=Historic Data |url=https://iranpistachio.org/en/statistical-archive/historic-data |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Iran Pistachio Association {{!}} Official Site |date= 5 November 2018|language=en-gb}}</ref> The Iranian government has provided energy and water subsidies over the past several decades, attracting producers to the area, where more than 30,000 people are directly involved in the production by owning or managing pistachio orchards.<ref>{{Cite web |title=FAOSTAT |url=https://www.fao.org/faostat/en/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=www.fao.org}}</ref><ref name="auto"/>
However, economically viable pistachio production requires specific climatic conditions, such as long, hot summers and sufficient chill in winters, which occur in areas far from surface water resources like rivers or lakes, making the crop water-intensive.<ref>Benmoussa H, Luedeling E, Ghrab M, Ben Yahmed J, Ben MM. 2017. Performance of Pistachio ( L.) in warming Mediterranean Orchards. Environmental and Experimental Botany 140: 76–85.</ref><ref>Elloumi O, Ghrab M, Kessentini H, Ben MM. 2013. Chilling accumulation effects on performance of pistachio trees cv. Mateur in dry and warm area climate. Scientia Horticulturae 159: 80–87.</ref> As a result, pistachio producers in Rafsanjan have long relied on groundwater as the only source of water for irrigation.<ref>Mehryar S, Sliuzas R, Sharifi A, Van Maarseveen MFAM. 2016. The socio-ecological analytical framework of water scarcity in Rafsanjan township, Iran. International Journal of Safety and Security Engineering 6(4): 764–776.</ref><ref>Motagh M, Walter TR, Sharifi MA, Fielding E, Schenk A, Anderssohn J, Zschau J. 2008. Land subsidence in Iran caused by widespread water reservoir overexploitation. Geophysical Research Letters 35(16): L16403. https://doi.org/10.1029/2008GL033814</ref>

According to reports from the late 1980s, the Rafsanjani family is said to "control" Iran's multimillion-dollar pistachio market centered around the town of Rafsanjan.<ref>{{cite news| title=Wrong ways on a 'righteous path' |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] | date=20 February 1989 | url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/archive/politics/1989/02/20/wrong-ways-on-a-righteous-path/48401178-76ed-4bea-9708-2dab530e34a7/ | access-date=27 May 2023}}</ref> [[Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani|Ali Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani]], former Iranian president and one of the most powerful members of the regime in Iran, was the head of the parliamentary speaker and had close ties to the [[Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps|Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)]] construction firm, which built most of Iran's infrastructure, including dams.
<ref name="anarchy">{{Cite web |title="I am thirsty": statements from Soheil Arabi and the Federation of Anarchism Era on the uprising in Iran {{!}} libcom.org |url=https://libcom.org/article/i-am-thirsty-statements-soheil-arabi-and-federation-anarchism-era-uprising-iran |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=libcom.org |language=en}}</ref> These dams were used to redirect water to the agricultural fields of high-ranking members of the regime, including the Rafsanjani family, resulting in a water mafia-like scenario.<ref name="anarchy" />

=== Land rights ===
In the region, although communal “garden ownership” (baghcheh-dari) was prevalent among villagers, absentee landlords still held a virtual [[monopoly]] over the ownership of the basin's land and water resources.<ref name="womenjournal">{{cite book|title= Women's Studies at York Series|edition=First|publisher=Palgrave Macmillan|location=York|year=1993|page=117-118}}</ref> This is due to orchards being exempted from redistribution under the [[Iranian Land Reform|Iranian Land Reform Programme of 1962]], which sought to [[White Revolution|abolish the feudal system]] and redistribute arable land from large landowners to smaller agricultural workers. At the end of the 80s, a survey done in two Rafsanjan villages revealed that villagers owned only about 17% of the water and cultivated land, while the rest belonged to a small number of absentee landlords.<ref name="womenjournal" />
==== Gender disparities ====
During the same era, there was a shift in labor relations from the traditional [[sharecropping]] contracts, which were prevalent throughout most of the Persian plateau, to wage labor.<ref name="womenjournal" /> For pistachio cultivation, the new labor force was differentiated by skill and gender and was exclusively on a wage basis.<ref name="womenjournal" /> The laborers, called “ghararis”, who were by definition male and highly skilled in irrigation work, which was an exclusively male task, constituted the "labor aristocracy."<ref name="womenjournal" /> They were employed on a permanent basis with a monthly wage, a New Year bonus, and given a small plot of land free of charge with access to the landlord's irrigation water.<ref name="womenjournal" />

On the other hand, female laborers engaged in harvest and post-harvest operations occupied the lowest position in the labor hierarchy.<ref name="womenjournal" /> Their work was seasonal, and their labor remuneration was mostly based on a piece-rate basis, with payment made three to four months after finishing the work.<ref name="womenjournal" /> Additionally, the spinning and weaving of cotton textiles, which used to be a traditional income-earning activity for women, had at the time been virtually wiped out due to competition with the cheap [[Synthetic fiber|synthetic fibers]].<ref name="womenjournal" />

==Transport ==
Rafsanjan is located in the middle of Kerman-Yazd highway and also Chabahar transit pathway. The distances between Rafsanjan and the surrounding cities are:
{{convert|12|km|mi|abbr=on}} to Bardsir, {{convert|140|km|mi|abbr=on}} to Sirjan, {{convert|130|km|mi|abbr=on}} to Shahre-Babak, {{convert|90|km|mi|abbr=on}} to Anar, {{convert|75|km|mi|abbr=on}} to Zarand, {{convert|185|km|mi|abbr=on}} to Bafgh and {{convert|230|km|mi|abbr=on}} to Yazd.

Locally, people use both public and private transportation, however private vehicles are more popular among residents.
Rafsanjan also houses bus stations with destination to almost every major city around the country, cargo and freit terminal, domestic trains station in the middle of Tehran-Bandar Abbas railway, and also a commercial airport with flights to and from Tehran.

==Notable people==
*Former President of Iran [[Hashemi Rafsanjani]] was born in [[Nough, Iran|Nough]], near Rafsanjan
*Former Minister of Culture and Islamic Guidance, [[Mohammad Hosseini (politician)|Mohammad Hosseini]] was born in Koshkouyeh near Rafsanjan
*Seyyed [[Hossein Marashi]], former Iranian Vice-President of Cultural Heritage and Tourism, was born in Koshkouyeh near Rafsanjan. 
*[[Ali Samereh]], Iranian football player
*{{ill|Hussayn Jalali|fa|حسین جلالی}}, Shia Cleric
*[[Mark Amin]], vice president of [[Lions Gate Entertainment]] production company.
*{{ill|Mohammad Hashemian|fa|محمد هاشمیان}}, Shia Cleric
*[[Mehdi Tabatabaei]], Shia Cleric

==Colleges and universities==
*[https://www.iaurafsanjan.ac.ir/ Islamic Azad University Rafsanjan Branch]
*[https://www.aj.ac.ir/ Allameh Jafari University]
*[[Rafsanjan University of Medical Sciences]] [http://www.rums.ac.ir/] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210322044152/http://www.rums.ac.ir/ |date=22 March 2021 }}
*[https://www.vru.ac.ir/ Rafsanjan University of Vali Asr]
*[https://web.archive.org/web/20110208152406/http://rums.ac.ir/index.php?sid=78&slc_lang=en Rafsanjan School of Medicine]

==Sport==
Football club [[Mes Rafsanjan F.C.]] is based in the city.

==See also==

'''Samuel Cowan''' (10 May 1901 – 4 October 1964) was an English [[association football|football]] player and manager. A relative latecomer to the sport, Cowan did not play football until he was 17 and was 22 by the time he turned professional. He made his league debut for [[Doncaster Rovers F.C.|Doncaster Rovers]] in 1923, and signed for [[Football League First Division|First Division]] [[Manchester City F.C.|Manchester City]] the following season.

Cowan played [[defender (association football)|centre half]] for Manchester City for 11 seasons, captaining the team in the early to mid-1930s. Alongside [[David Silva]] and [[Vincent Kompany]], he is the only other player to have represented Manchester City in three [[FA Cup]] finals, as a runner-up in [[1926 FA Cup Final|1926]] and [[1933 FA Cup Final|1933]], and as a winner in [[1934 FA Cup Final|1934]]. Internationally, he gained three [[England national football team|England]] [[Cap (sport)|caps]] between 1926 and 1931. In total he played 407 times for Manchester City, putting him 12th in terms of all-time appearances. In 1935, he transferred to [[Bradford City A.F.C.|Bradford City]], and subsequently moved to [[Mossley A.F.C.|Mossley]] as [[player-manager]].

In 1938, Cowan joined [[Brighton & Hove Albion F.C.|Brighton & Hove Albion]] as a coach, and set up a [[Physical therapy|physiotherapy]] business. He returned to Manchester City as manager in 1946, winning the [[Football League Second Division|Second Division]] in his only season in charge. He continued to work in sports and physiotherapy until his death in 1964.

==Football career==
===Early career===
Cowan was born on 10 May 1901 in [[Chesterfield, Derbyshire|Chesterfield]], but moved further north in his early years and was raised in [[Adwick le Street]] near [[Doncaster]].<ref name="halloffame">James, ''The Official Manchester City Hall of Fame'', p. 152.</ref> He did not play football as a child, only gaining an interest in the sport when he took part in a game at a local park aged 17.<ref name="azmanc">{{cite book |last=Brandon |first=Derek <!--Invalid ISBN |isbn=0-86148-001-7 --> |ol=13768775M |title=A–Z of Manchester Football: 100 Years of Rivalry |publisher=Boondoggle |location=London |year=1978 |page=46}}</ref> He then started to play for local teams, including Adwick Juniors, [[Bullcroft Main Colliery F.C.|Bullcroft Main Colliery]] and [[Denaby United F.C.|Denaby United]] and had an unsuccessful trial at [[Huddersfield Town A.F.C.|Huddersfield Town]].<ref name="halloffame"/>

In 1923, Cowan gained his first professional contract following a successful trial at his local league club, [[Doncaster Rovers F.C.|Doncaster Rovers]] of the [[Football League Third Division North|Third Division (North)]].<ref name="halloffame"/> Despite playing as a defender, during his time at Doncaster he scored regularly, including a [[hat-trick]] of headed goals against [[Halifax Town A.F.C.|Halifax Town]] in March 1924.<ref name="Clayton">{{cite book |first=David |last=Clayton |title=Everything Under the Blue Moon |isbn=1-84018-687-9 |publisher=Mainstream Publishing |year=2002 |page=60}}</ref> His performances gained the attention of bigger clubs, and in December 1924 he joined [[Manchester City F.C.|Manchester City]], who sought a successor to the likes of [[Mick Hamill]] and [[Max Woosnam]].<ref name="mre">{{cite book |last=Penney |first=Ian |title=The Maine Road Encyclopedia |publisher=Mainstream Publishing |location=Edinburgh |year=1995 |isbn=1-85158-710-1 |pages=49–50}}</ref>

===Manchester City===
Cowan made his Manchester City debut in a 2–2 draw against [[Birmingham City F.C.|Birmingham City]] on 20 December 1924,<ref>{{cite book |last=James |first=Gary |title=Manchester City – The Complete Record |publisher=Breedon |location=Derby |year=2006 |isbn=1-85983-512-0 |page=169}}</ref> and scored his first goal for the club two weeks later, against [[Nottingham Forest F.C.|Nottingham Forest]]. He played in all but one of the remaining league matches that season, 21 in total.

Starting all but four of Manchester City's matches in the [[1925-26 in English football|1925–26 season]], Cowan was a key part of the team which reached the [[1926 FA Cup Final]], winning several ties by wide margins en route. However, in the final City were beaten 1–0 by [[Bolton Wanderers F.C.|Bolton Wanderers]]. Further disappointment followed in the [[English football league system|league]], when after a campaign characterised by erratic form, City were [[promotion and relegation|relegated]] on the final day of the season. By this time Cowan's reputation as a competent centre-back had grown, and he received an international call-up. He made his [[England national football team|England]] debut on 24 May 1926 playing an unfamiliar left-half role in a 5–3 win against [[Belgium national football team|Belgium]].<ref name="hof156"/> By 1931, he had earned three international [[cap (sport)|caps]], the others gained in matches against [[France national football team|France]] and [[Austria national football team|Austria]].<ref>{{Englandstats|ref=y|access-date=11 May 2008}}</ref> He also made appearances for the [[The Football League XI|Football League team]], including a trial match against England in 1931.

In the [[1926-27 in English football|1926–27 season]], Cowan made 27 appearances as his club sought an immediate return to the top division. The race for promotion went to the final match, with Manchester City and [[Portsmouth F.C.|Portsmouth]] both in contention for the second of the two promotion places. Cowan played in a resounding City win, an 8–0 victory against [[Bradford City A.F.C.|Bradford City]]. The watching crowd believed the result to be sufficient for promotion, but Portsmouth's match had been delayed by 15 minutes and was still in progress. A late Portsmouth goal meant the final scoreline in their match was a 5–1 win, enough to give Portsmouth second place on [[goal average]] by a margin of one two-hundredth of a goal.<ref>James, ''Manchester City – The Complete Record'', p. 116.</ref> The club won the [[Football League Second Division|Second Division]] championship the following season, gaining [[promotion and relegation|promotion]] to the top flight. The Second Division championship was Cowan's first honour in professional football.

In the early 1930s, Cowan became Manchester City captain, succeeding [[Jimmy McMullan]]. During his captaincy the club reached two further [[FA Cup Final]]s. The first of these was in [[1933 FA Cup Final|1933]], against [[Everton F.C.|Everton]]. During the match Cowan was up against Everton captain [[Dixie Dean]]. Both players were renowned for their heading ability. [[Matt Busby]] claimed that Cowan could "head a ball as far as most of us could kick it",<ref>James, ''Manchester City – The Complete Record'', p. 170.</ref> but Dean prevailed in the aerial battle, scoring Everton's second goal with a header. The ''[[Daily Mail]]'' observed that Dean's presence gave Cowan a dilemma: "He was torn between a determination not to leave Dean and a desire to help his forwards. He broke down between the two."<ref>{{cite book |first=Gary |last=James |title=Manchester: The Greatest City |publisher=Polar |location=Leicester |year=2002 |isbn=1-899538-22-4}} p136</ref> Everton were 3–0 victors, but when Cowan received his runner's up medal from the [[George VI of the United Kingdom|Duke of York]], he remarked that he would come back next year to win.<ref>Some sources mistakenly state that the remark was made to [[George V of the United Kingdom|King George V]], who did not attend the 1933 final.</ref> True to Cowan's word, City returned to [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley]] the following year. In the [[1934 FA Cup Final]], Cowan became the first and thus far only Manchester City player to represent the club in three FA Cup finals.<ref>James, ''The Official Manchester City Hall of Fame'', p. 153.</ref> He captained City to a 2–1 victory over [[Portsmouth F.C.|Portsmouth]]. As team captain Cowan held primary responsibility for motivating his fellow players and match tactics.<ref name="Kelly">{{cite book |last=Kelly |first=Graham |title=Terrace Heroes: The Life and Times of the 1930s Professional Footballer |publisher=Routledge |location=London |year=2004 |isbn=0-7146-5359-4 |page=94}}</ref> This was typical for a captain of his era, as [[Coach (sport)|managers]] of the period were chiefly administrators who had little input into coaching and tactics.<ref name="Kelly"/>

Cowan's final season at [[Maine Road]] was [[1934-35 in English football|1934–35]], in which he was ever-present. His final match for the club was a 5–0 defeat of [[Wolverhampton Wanderers F.C.|Wolverhampton Wanderers]] on 4 May 1935.<ref>James, ''Manchester City – The Complete Record'', p. 348.</ref> In total he made 407 appearances for Manchester City, scoring 24 goals. As of 2008 he ranks 12th all-time for Manchester City appearances. [[Bob Donnelly]] was signed to replace him in the 1935 close season, and [[Bradford City A.F.C.|Bradford City]] signed Cowan for a fee of [[pound sterling|£]]2,000 in October 1935. At the time the transfer fee was the second highest ever paid by the club.<ref name="hof156">James, ''The Official Manchester City Hall of Fame'', p. 156.</ref>

===Bradford City===
Bradford City were struggling in the [[Football League Division Two|Second Division]] at the time of Cowan's signing, having won just one of their first ten games of the [[1935-36 in English football|1935–36]] season. Cowan made his debut for them on 19 October 1935 in a 2–2 draw with [[Swindon Town F.C.|Swindon Town]], but it was not until Cowan's third game that Bradford won for the second time that season. Cowan missed just three of the club's remaining fixtures that season, playing 29 times in the league and three in the FA Cup as City finished 12th.<ref>{{cite book |last=Frost |first=Terry |title=Bradford City A Complete Record 1903–1988 |publisher=Breedon Books Sport |year=1988 |pages=226–227 |isbn=0-907969-38-0}}</ref> The following season, City again struggled. Cowan missed just one of the first 23 games, but after Christmas, he was restricted to just six; and without Cowan, Bradford finished 21st and were relegated to the Third Division (North).<ref>{{cite book |last=Frost |title=Bradford City A Complete Record 1903–1988 |pages=228–229}}</ref>

===Managerial career===
In 1937, Cowan was appointed player-manager at [[Mossley A.F.C.|Mossley]]. He led the club to the seventh place in the [[Cheshire County League|Cheshire League]] and to the [[Manchester Challenge Shield]] title. On the pitch, he played 39 matches and scored one goal.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.mossleyweb.com/Managers1.htm |title=The Mossley Managers |access-date=12 May 2008 |publisher=Mossley AFC |archive-date=30 May 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190530092253/http://www.mossleyweb.com/Managers1.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref> At the end of the season Cowan resigned to join [[Brighton & Hove Albion F.C.|Brighton & Hove Albion]] coaching staff. In October 1945, a shortage of players resulted in Cowan taking to the field in a wartime match against [[Bournemouth & Boscombe Athletic F.C.|Bournemouth & Boscombe Athletic]]. Aged 44 years and 156 days, he became the oldest ever Brighton player.<ref>{{cite news |title=Seagulls move for Beasant |url=http://www.theargus.co.uk/news/5117287.Seagulls_move_for_Beasant/ |publisher=The Argus |date=30 January 2003 |access-date = 13 December 2017}}</ref> While at Brighton, Cowan started a [[physiotherapy]] business based near the [[Goldstone Ground]].<ref name="Clayton"/>

In November 1946, Cowan became Manchester City manager, succeeding [[Wilf Wild]], who moved to a purely administrative role.<ref name="mre"/> Cowan had been known for his motivational skills as a player, and was part of a new generation of managers who took responsibility for tactics and team selection, of which Cowan's contemporary and former teammate [[Matt Busby]] became the most well-known. Cowan was given a salary of £2,000, and came to an agreement whereby he spent part of the week with the Manchester City team and part looking after his business interests in Brighton.<ref>James, ''Manchester City – The Complete Record'', p. 243.</ref> His first match as manager was a 3–0 win against [[Newport County F.C.|Newport County]], and the team then embarked upon a run of 19 matches without defeat. Despite achieving the Division Two title in his first season in charge, Cowan resigned in June 1947 as his commute from Brighton caused tension with club officials.<ref>James, ''Manchester City – The Complete Record'', p. 245.</ref>

==Post-football==
Cowan later worked as a [[physiotherapist]] for [[cricket]] club [[Sussex CCC]] and [[ice hockey]] team [[Brighton Tigers]]. As a trainer with the Tigers he was named in the British National League All Star team for three years running from 1956 to 1958.<ref>{{cite news |title=All Star Team Season 1955–1956 |url=http://www.ihjuk.co.uk/awards/allStar/55_56.html |publisher=Ice Hockey Journalists UK | access-date = 6 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=All Star Team Season 1956–1957 |url=http://www.ihjuk.co.uk/awards/allStar/56_57.html |publisher=Ice Hockey Journalists UK | access-date = 6 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=All Star Team Season 1957–1958 |url=http://www.ihjuk.co.uk/awards/allStar/57_58.html |publisher=Ice Hockey Journalists UK | access-date = 6 March 2011}}</ref> He died on 4 October 1964 aged 63, when he suffered a heart attack, while refereeing a football charity match in aid of Sussex [[wicket keeper]] [[Jim Parks junior|Jim Parks]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.youandyesterday.co.uk/articles/Cowan,_Samuel_-_Chesterfield-born_England_Football_International |title=Cowan, Samuel – Chesterfield-born England Football International |access-date=12 May 2008 |publisher=You and Yesterday |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080514181101/http://www.youandyesterday.co.uk/articles/Cowan,_Samuel_-_Chesterfield-born_England_Football_International <!--Added by H3llBot--> |archive-date=14 May 2008}}</ref> He left behind a widow and one son.<ref>{{cite news |url=http://archive.timesonline.co.uk/tol/viewArticle.arc?toDate=1964-10-20&fromDate=1964-10-01&currentPageNumber=1&resultsPerPage=10&sortBy=default&offset=0&viewName=&addFilters=&removeFilters=&addCat=&queryKeywords=Sam+Cowan&sectionId=1040&currPgSmartSet=1&pageId=ARCHIVE-The_Times-1964-10-05-06&articleId=ARCHIVE-The_Times-1964-10-05-06-006&xmlpath=&pubId=17&totalResults=2&addRefineFilters=&removeRefineFilters=&addRefineCat=&next_Page=false&prev_Page=false&date_dd_From=1&date_mm_From=10&date_yyyy_From=1964&date_dd_to_range=20&date_mm_to_range=10&date_yyyy_to_range=1964&date_dd_from_precise=1&date_mm_from_precise=10&date_yyyy_from_precise=1964&isDateSearch=false&dateSearchType=range&refineQuerykeywordText= |title=Ex-footballer dies while refereeing |date=5 October 1964 |access-date=24 June 2008 |page=6 |work=The Times}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> In 2004, he was elected to Manchester City's Hall of Fame.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.mcfc.co.uk/default.sps?pagegid={F4698C75-95FF-4A3E-936A-4F9BBBADDA74} |title=Hall of Fame |access-date=12 May 2008 |publisher=Manchester City FC |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20080416103310/http://www.mcfc.co.uk/default.sps?pagegid=%7bF4698C75-95FF-4A3E-936A-4F9BBBADDA74%7d |archive-date = 16 April 2008}}</ref> A street near Manchester City's former ground [[Maine Road]], ''Sam Cowan Close'', is named in his honour.<ref>James, ''The Official Manchester City Hall of Fame'', p. 158.</ref>

==Honours==

* [[FA Cup]] [[1934 FA Cup Final|1934]]; runner-up: [[1926 FA Cup Final|1926]], [[1933 FA Cup Final|1933]]
* [[Football League Division Two|Second Division]]: [[1927–28 Football League|1927–28]]

* [[Manchester Challenge Shield]]: 1937
* [[Football League Division Two|Second Division]]: [[The Football League 1946-47|1946–47]]
