stable.clean_text: {{Infobox football biography | name = Sam Cowan | image = SCowan1.JPG | caption = The King presents Sam Cowan with the FA Cup in 1934 | fullname = Samuel Cowan | birth_date = | birth_place = Chesterfield, England | death_date = | death_place = Haywards Heath, England | height = | position = Centre half | youthyears1 = | youthclubs1 = Adwick Juniors | youthyears2 = | youthclubs2 = Huddersfield Town | years1 = | clubs1 = Denaby United | caps1 = | goals1 = | years2 = | clubs2 = Bullcroft Main Colliery | caps2 = | goals2 = | years3 = 1923–1924 | clubs3 = Doncaster Rovers | caps3 = 48 | goals3 = 13 | years4 = 1924–1935 | clubs4 = Manchester City | caps4 = 369 | goals4 = 19 | years5 = 1935–1937 | clubs5 = Bradford City | caps5 = 57 | goals5 = 1 | years6 = 1937–1938 | clubs6 = Mossley | caps6 = 39 | goals6 = 1 | nationalteam1 = England | nationalyears1 = 1926–1931 | nationalcaps1 = 3 | nationalgoals1 = 0 | manageryears1 = 1937–1938 | managerclubs1 = Mossley (player-manager) | manageryears2 = 1946–1947 | managerclubs2 = Manchester City }} Samuel Cowan (10 May 1901 – 4 October 1964) was an English football player and manager. A relative latecomer to the sport, Cowan did not play football until he was 17 and was 22 by the time he turned professional. He made his league debut for Doncaster Rovers in 1923, and signed for First Division Manchester City the following season. Cowan played centre half for Manchester City for 11 seasons, captaining the team in the early to mid-1930s. Alongside David Silva and Vincent Kompany, he is the only other player to have represented Manchester City in three FA Cup finals, as a runner-up in 1926 and 1933, and as a winner in 1934. Internationally, he gained three England caps between 1926 and 1931. In total he played 407 times for Manchester City, putting him 12th in terms of all-time appearances. In 1935, he transferred to Bradford City, and subsequently moved to Mossley as player-manager. In 1938, Cowan joined Brighton & Hove Albion as a coach, and set up a physiotherapy business. He returned to Manchester City as manager in 1946, winning the Second Division in his only season in charge. He continued to work in sports and physiotherapy until his death in 1964. Cowan was born on 10 May 1901 in Chesterfield, but moved further north in his early years and was raised in Adwick le Street near Doncaster. He did not play football as a child, only gaining an interest in the sport when he took part in a game at a local park aged 17. He then started to play for local teams, including Adwick Juniors, Bullcroft Main Colliery and Denaby United and had an unsuccessful trial at Huddersfield Town. His performances gained the attention of bigger clubs, and in December 1924 he joined Manchester City, who sought a successor to the likes of Mick Hamill and Max Woosnam. Cowan made his Manchester City debut in a 2–2 draw against Birmingham City on 20 December 1924, and scored his first goal for the club two weeks later, against Nottingham Forest. He played in all but one of the remaining league matches that season, 21 in total. Starting all but four of Manchester City's matches in the 1925–26 season, Cowan was a key part of the team which reached the 1926 FA Cup Final, winning several ties by wide margins en route. However, in the final City were beaten 1–0 by Bolton Wanderers. Further disappointment followed in the league, when after a campaign characterised by erratic form, City were relegated on the final day of the season. By this time Cowan's reputation as a competent centre-back had grown, and he received an international call-up. He made his England debut on 24 May 1926 playing an unfamiliar left-half role in a 5–3 win against Belgium. He also made appearances for the Football League team, including a trial match against England in 1931. In the 1926–27 season, Cowan made 27 appearances as his club sought an immediate return to the top division. The race for promotion went to the final match, with Manchester City and Portsmouth both in contention for the second of the two promotion places. Cowan played in a resounding City win, an 8–0 victory against Bradford City. The watching crowd believed the result to be sufficient for promotion, but Portsmouth's match had been delayed by 15 minutes and was still in progress. A late Portsmouth goal meant the final scoreline in their match was a 5–1 win, enough to give Portsmouth second place on goal average by a margin of one two-hundredth of a goal. The club won the Second Division championship the following season, gaining promotion to the top flight. The Second Division championship was Cowan's first honour in professional football. In the early 1930s, Cowan became Manchester City captain, succeeding Jimmy McMullan. During his captaincy the club reached two further FA Cup Finals. The first of these was in 1933, against Everton. During the match Cowan was up against Everton captain Dixie Dean. Both players were renowned for their heading ability. Matt Busby claimed that Cowan could "head a ball as far as most of us could kick it", but Dean prevailed in the aerial battle, scoring Everton's second goal with a header. The Daily Mail observed that Dean's presence gave Cowan a dilemma: "He was torn between a determination not to leave Dean and a desire to help his forwards. He broke down between the two." Everton were 3–0 victors, but when Cowan received his runner's up medal from the Duke of York, he remarked that he would come back next year to win. True to Cowan's word, City returned to Wembley the following year. In the 1934 FA Cup Final, Cowan became the first and thus far only Manchester City player to represent the club in three FA Cup finals. He captained City to a 2–1 victory over Portsmouth. As team captain Cowan held primary responsibility for motivating his fellow players and match tactics. This was typical for a captain of his era, as managers of the period were chiefly administrators who had little input into coaching and tactics. In total he made 407 appearances for Manchester City, scoring 24 goals. As of 2008 he ranks 12th all-time for Manchester City appearances. Bob Donnelly was signed to replace him in the 1935 close season, and Bradford City signed Cowan for a fee of £2,000 in October 1935. At the time the transfer fee was the second highest ever paid by the club. Bradford City were struggling in the Second Division at the time of Cowan's signing, having won just one of their first ten games of the 1935–36 season. Cowan made his debut for them on 19 October 1935 in a 2–2 draw with Swindon Town, but it was not until Cowan's third game that Bradford won for the second time that season. Cowan missed just three of the club's remaining fixtures that season, playing 29 times in the league and three in the FA Cup as City finished 12th. The following season, City again struggled. Cowan missed just one of the first 23 games, but after Christmas, he was restricted to just six; and without Cowan, Bradford finished 21st and were relegated to the Third Division (North). In 1937, Cowan was appointed player-manager at Mossley. He led the club to the seventh place in the Cheshire League and to the Manchester Challenge Shield title. On the pitch, he played 39 matches and scored one goal. At the end of the season Cowan resigned to join Brighton & Hove Albion coaching staff. In October 1945, a shortage of players resulted in Cowan taking to the field in a wartime match against Bournemouth & Boscombe Athletic. Aged 44 years and 156 days, he became the oldest ever Brighton player. While at Brighton, Cowan started a physiotherapy business based near the Goldstone Ground. His first match as manager was a 3–0 win against Newport County, and the team then embarked upon a run of 19 matches without defeat. Despite achieving the Division Two title in his first season in charge, Cowan resigned in June 1947 as his commute from Brighton caused tension with club officials. Cowan later worked as a physiotherapist for cricket club Sussex CCC and ice hockey team Brighton Tigers. As a trainer with the Tigers he was named in the British National League All Star team for three years running from 1956 to 1958. He died on 4 October 1964 aged 63, when he suffered a heart attack, while refereeing a football charity match in aid of Sussex wicket keeper Jim Parks. He left behind a widow and one son. In 2004, he was elected to Manchester City's Hall of Fame. A street near Manchester City's former ground Maine Road, Sam Cowan Close, is named in his honour. * FA Cup 1934; runner-up: 1926, 1933 * Second Division: 1927–28 * Manchester Challenge Shield: 1937 * Second Division: 1946–47 {{Navboxes | title = Managerial positions | list1 = }} Category:1901 births Category:1964 deaths Category:Footballers from Chesterfield Category:English men's footballers Category:England men's international footballers Category:Men's association football defenders Category:Denaby United F.C. players Category:Bullcroft Main Colliery F.C. players Category:Doncaster Rovers F.C. players Category:Manchester City F.C. players Category:Bradford City A.F.C. players Category:Mossley A.F.C. players Category:English Football League players Category:English Football League representative players Category:English football managers Category:Mossley A.F.C. managers Category:Manchester City F.C. managers Category:English Football League managers Category:20th-century English sportsmen

contested.title: España 2000
contested.raw_text:
{{Multiple issues| {{refimprove|date=February 2023}} {{neutrality|date=February 2023}} {{Use dmy dates|date=March 2021}}}} {{Infobox political party | country = Spain | name = Spain 2000 | native_name = España 2000 | logo = [[File:LogoEspaña2000.svg|250px]] | colorcode = {{party color|Spain 2000}} | president = [[José Luis Roberto]] | leader1_title = Vice President | leader1_name = Antonio Martínez Cayuela | foundation = 2002 | predecessor = | ideology = [[Spanish nationalism]]
[[Right-wing populism]]
[[Euroscepticism]]
[[Social patriotism]]
[[Right-wing antiglobalism|Anti-globalism]]
[[Protectionism]]
[[Blaverism]]
[[Anti-LGBT sentiment|Anti-LGBTQ sentiment]]{{cite web | url=https://www.abc.es/espana/comunidad-valenciana/abci-fiscalia-investiga-delito-odio-unas-publicaciones-sobre-colectivo-lgtbi-y-viruela-mono-202206101215_noticia.html?ref=https%3A%2F%2Fen.wikipedia.org%2F | title=La Fiscalía investiga por delito de odio unas publicaciones sobre el colectivo LGTBI y la viruela del mono }} | position = [[Far-right politics|Far-right]] | european = | europarl = | national = RESPETO | international = | colours = Red and Yellow | seats1_title = [[List of municipalities of Spain|Mayors]][https://www.publico.es/politica/espana-2000-alcanza-alcaldia-municipio.html España 2000 alcanza la Alcaldía en un municipio madrileño con al apoyo del PP]. [[Público (Spain)|Público]], 16 June 2019. | seats1 = {{Composition bar|1|8131|hex={{party color|Spanish Alternative}}}} | seats2_title = [[List of municipalities of Spain|Local
Government]] | seats2 = {{Infobox political party/seats|6|67121|hex=red}} | headquarters = Dos Aguas, Valencia | website = [https://espana2000.es/ espana2000.es] }} '''Spain 2000''' ([[Spanish language|Spanish]]: ''España 2000''; '''E-2000''') is a [[Far-right politics|far-right]] [[political party in Spain]]. Founded in 2002, the party has been described as [[Neo-Nazism|neo-Nazi]]{{Cite journal |last=Olascoaga |first=Omar García |date=2017-10-20 |title=Los partidos neonazis en Europa: ¿un legado olvidado? |url=http://www.revistas.unam.mx/index.php/rri/article/view/62244 |journal=Revista de Relaciones Internacionales de la UNAM |language=es |issue=128 |issn=0185-0814}} and is currently led by [[José Luis Roberto]]. It has never obtained national or regional parliamentary representation. Since 2010, E-2000 has awaken interest among media. The party was mentioned by [[2011 Norway attacks|terrorist]] [[Anders Breivik]] in his pamphlet "2083. A declaration of European independence" back in 2011.{{Cite web |last=valencia |first=J. L. García LLAGües |date=2011-07-25 |title=El autor del doble atentado cita a España 2000 en el texto que inspiró la masacre |url=https://www.levante-emv.com/comunitat-valenciana/2011/07/25/autor-doble-atentado-cita-espana-13046899.html |access-date=2024-11-17 |website=Levante-EMV |language=es}} The last time the party ran in a [[General elections in Spain|general election]] was in [[2011 Spanish general election|2011]], where it obtained 9266 votes. Following the fourth [[Party Congress]], its president José Luis Roberto left the presidency and was replaced by [[Rafael Ripoll]].{{Cite web |date=2015-10-14 |title=España2000 eligió a Rafael Ripoll como nuevo presidente en su IV Congreso - Web oficial España 2000 |url=https://espana2000.es/2015/10/14/espana2000-eligio-a-rafael-ripoll-como-nuevo-presidente-en-su-iv-congreso/ |access-date=2024-11-15 |language=es}}{{Cite web |date=2015-10-14 |title=El alcalaíno Ripoll, elegido líder del 'Frente Nacional' español - Diario de Alcalá |url=http://diariodealcala.es/actualidad/politica/item/20254-el-alcala%C3%ADno-ripoll-elegido-l%C3%ADder-del-frente-nacional-espa%C3%B1ol |access-date=2024-11-15 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151014000525/http://diariodealcala.es/actualidad/politica/item/20254-el-alcala%C3%ADno-ripoll-elegido-l%C3%ADder-del-frente-nacional-espa%C3%B1ol |archive-date=14 October 2015 }} In October 2020, Ripoll resigned{{Cite web |date=2020-02-11 |title=Con alegría - Web oficial España 2000 |url=https://espana2000.es/2020/02/11/con-alegria/ |access-date=2024-11-15 |language=es}} and was succeeded by Roberto, who was chosen party leader once again.{{Cite web |date=2020-10-22 |title=José Luis Roberto vuelve a encabezar la lucha del "auténtico patriotismo español" - Web oficial España 2000 |url=https://espana2000.es/2020/10/22/jose-luis-roberto-vuelve-a-encabezar-la-lucha-del-autentico-patriotismo-espanol/ |access-date=2024-11-15 |language=es}} == History == === Origin === E-2000 has its origins in the Platform Spain 2000 ([[Spanish language|Spanish]]: ''Plataforma España 2000''), created for the [[2000 Spanish general election]] by the far-right political parties [[Democracia Nacional]] (DN), [[Movimiento Social Republicano]] (MSR), [[Partido Nacional de los Trabajadores]] (PNT) and [[Vértice Social Español]] (VSE). The project received the support of [[Jean-Marie Le Pen]], then president of the French [[National Rally|National Front]]. The platform was dissolved following the election due to infighting.{{Cite web |date=2007-10-22 |title=Enlaces E-2000 |url=http://esp2000.org/elecciones_gen_2000.htm |access-date=2024-11-14 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071022160654/http://esp2000.org/elecciones_gen_2000.htm |archive-date=22 October 2007 }} España 2000 was founded as a political party in July 2002 by a group of independents who had been former members of the previous platform. [[José Luis Roberto]] was chosen as the party leader. === Roberto's first leadership (2002–2015) === The party contested [[Elections in Spain|elections]] with minimal success. In the [[2008 Spanish general election|2008 general election]], for example, the party polled 7,543 votes, or 0.03% of the total.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120630044216/http://www.pre.gva.es/pls/argos_elec/DMEDB_elecPaises.informeElecDetallado?aVPaisId=E&aNNumElec=1&aVTipoElec=G&aVFechaElec=2008&aVLengua=v 2008 General Election result] However, in 2007 they gained their first elected representative when they won a council seat in [[Silla, Valencia|Silla]], a town south of Valencia city.[http://www1.pre.gva.es/pls/argos_elec/DMEDB_Elecmunicipios.informeElecDetallado?aNMuniId=46230&aNNumElec=1&aVTipoElec=L&aVFechaElec=2007&aVLengua=v Silla election results]{{dead link|date=December 2016 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} In the [[2011 Spanish municipal elections|2011 municipal elections]], España 2000 won five council seats.{{cite web|author=El Mundo|url=http://www.elmundo.es/elecciones/elecciones-2011/resultados/municipales/2011/12/28/p005.html|title=Resultados electorales en Alcalá de Henares|date=22 May 2011}} A councillor in [[Onda, Castellón|Onda]] with 649 votes (5.05%), one in [[Dos Aguas]] with 66 votes (12.43%), two in [[Silla, Valencia|Silla]] (Valencia) with 997 votes (9.56%) and one in [[Alcalá de Henares]] with 4,541 votes (5.18%). In 2011, the [[Terrorism|terrorist]] [[Anders Breivik]], author of the [[2011 Norway attacks|same year's Norway Attacks]], mentioned E-2000 in the page 1246 of his pamphlet "2083. A declaration of European independence". There, he called the Spanish party "a storm that has shaken [[Valencian Community|Valencia]] by surprise, winning councillors in three different municipalities". The party kept their single city councillor in Alcalá de Henares after the [[2015 Alcalá de Henares City Council election|2015 municipal election]], while making advances in other municipalities of the Henares industrial corridor,{{Cite journal|url=https://elpais.com/ccaa/2015/05/29/madrid/1432926872_165154.html|journal=[[El País]]|title=La ultraderecha aumenta en el Corredor del Henares|first=J. Javier|last=Barroso|date=29 May 2015}} namely three in [[Los Santos de la Humosa]], one in [[Velilla de San Antonio]] and another in [[San Fernando de Henares]]. E-2000 also obtained one councillor in Silla, which was one less than they had in 2011. Following the fourth party Congress the 10 October 2015, party leader José Luis Roberto resigned from the party presidency. === Ripoll's leadership (2015–2020) === [[Rafael Ripoll]], councillor of [[Alcalá de Henares]] from 2011 to 2019 for the party, was unanimously chosen the new party leader during the fourth party Congress on 10 October 2015. During his leadership, E-2000 developed good relationships with similar political parties in other European countries such as the [[Liberal Democratic Party of Russia]] led by [[Vladimir Zhirinovsky]], mainly due to his time serving as the manager of foreign relations of the political party Democracia Nacional.{{Cite web |title=MOVIMIENTO CONTRA LA INTOLERANCIA |url=http://www.movimientocontralaintolerancia.com/html/Admin/verNoticia.asp?cod=676&esBusq=True |access-date=2024-11-15 |website=www.movimientocontralaintolerancia.com}} E-2000 created the [[Plataforma Respeto|platform Respect]] ([[Spanish language|Spanish]]: ''Plataforma Respeto'') in 2016, together with the now defunct political parties [[Platform for Catalonia]] and [[Party for Freedom (Spain)|Party for Freedom]].{{Cite web |last=www.latribunadelpaisvasco.com |date=2016-04-25 |title=Nace la Plataforma Respeto, la versión española del Frente Nacional de Marine Le Pen |url=https://latribunadelpaisvasco.com/archive/4578/nace-la-plataforma-respeto-la-version-espanola-del-frente-nacional-de-marine-le-pen#ath |access-date=2024-11-17 |website=La Tribuna del País Vasco {{!}} El gran periódico digital del País Vasco |language=es}}{{Cite web |last=Altimira |first=María |date=2016-05-06 |title=Respeto, el nuevo frente ultraderechista español que probablemente no tenga papeleta el 26J |url=https://www.vice.com/es/article/respeto-nuevo-frente-ultraderechista-espanol-no-tenga-papeleta-26j/ |access-date=2024-11-17 |website=VICE |language=es}} The platform was led by Ripoll, inspired by [[Marine Le Pen]]'s National Front, and supported by [[Pegida]] president [[Tatjana Festerling]]. It called for "a Spanish future without corruption, with [[social justice]], stopping [[Islamisation]], with economic sovereignty and defending the family". It had no success. In 2019, E-2000 decided to not take part in the [[2019 Spanish general election|national]] and [[2019 Spanish regional elections|regional elections]], in order to avoid dividing the vote and to not damage the electoral expectations of [[Vox (political party)|Vox]].{{Cite web |date=2019-03-22 |title=El partido 'ultra' España 2000 renuncia a presentarse para "no dividir el voto" y apoyar a quien defiende "la unidad de España" |url=https://www.elmundo.es/comunidad-valenciana/2019/03/22/5c94b6c0fdddffc4418b45ce.html |access-date=2024-11-15 |website=ELMUNDO |language=es}}{{Cite web |last=S.L |first=EDICIONES PLAZA |title=España 2000 anuncia que no se presentará a las elecciones para no perjudicar a Vox |url=https://valenciaplaza.com/espana-2000-anuncia-que-no-se-presentara-a-las-elecciones-para-no-perjudicar-a-vox |access-date=2024-11-15 |website=Valencia Plaza |language=es}} Ripoll resigned as party leader on October 2020. === Roberto's second term (2020–present) === José Luis Roberto was chosen the party president once again during the fifth party Congress. == Ideology == [[File:Papeleta España 2000 congr 2011 VLC.jpeg|200px|thumb|left|Party list for the 2011 Spanish general election. ]] España 2000 defines itself as [[populism|populist]], [[Social patriotism|social]] and [[Democracy|democratic]].{{Cite web |title=ISSN 0210-587X (Print) {{!}} L'Espill {{!}} The ISSN Portal |url=https://portal.issn.org/resource/issn/0210-587X |access-date=2024-11-14 |website=portal.issn.org}} The party has been described as [[Far-right politics|far-right]]{{Cite web |title=ISSN 1989-872X (Online) {{!}} Revista mediterránea de comunicación {{!}} The ISSN Portal |url=https://portal.issn.org/resource/issn/1989-872X |access-date=2024-11-14 |website=portal.issn.org |language=en-gb}} and identified with [[neo-Nazism]]. Its discourse is characterized by a [[Spanish nationalism]] which is tied to [[Blaverism]] in the Valencian community. The party advocates a «National Republic» as opposed to both socialist republicanism and nationalist monarchists, criticising the appropriation of [[Republicanism in Spain|republicanism]] by the left-wing.{{Cite web |date=2013-09-17 |title=Una nueva República - Web oficial España 2000 |url=https://espana2000.es/2013/09/17/una-nueva-republica/ |access-date=2024-11-15 |language=es}} E-2000 advocates a mixture of [[social patriotism]] and [[social conservatism]], centering on the defense of the [[Traditional family|traditional]] [[family values]] set forth by the [[Traditionalist Catholicism|Traditionalist Catholic Church]]. The party opposes economic and real estate [[speculation]]. Key features of the party's [[Party platform|platform]] include the establishment of a more democratic voting system by abolishing the [[D'Hondt method]]; tighter control of Spanish borders to prevent [[illegal immigration]], and the immediate expulsion of illegal immigrants; giving [[Autochthonous people|native]] [[Spaniards]] priority access to jobs; more investment in the [[police]] and [[military]]; more [[social spending]], particularly in [[public education]] and protected housing; better salaries and labor conditions for the [[working class]]; and a [[public banking]] system. Two quotes by party leader José Luis Roberto,{{cite web |url=http://esp2000.org/debate_JL.htm |title=Debates |publisher=Esp2000.org |access-date=2011-09-16 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110815200247/http://esp2000.org/debate_JL.htm |archive-date=15 August 2011 }} indicative of his and the party's ideology, are the following: * ''¿Tu admitirías que tu mujer diese a luz si la hubiese violado un moro? Yo no. (...) Por eso España 2000 esta a favor de que se legisle el aborto en casos extraordinarios al igual que la Eutanasia.'' ("Would you consent to your wife having her baby had she been raped by a [[Moors|Moor]]? I wouldn't. (...) That's why ''España 2000'' is [[pro-choice]] under extraordinary circumstances, as it is in favour of [[euthanasia]]"). *''La religión del Islam es un cáncer para la sociedad europea.'' ("[[Islam]] is a [[cancer]] to European society"). == Organization == === Leadership === {|class="wikitable" |- ! colspan="2" |[[Party leader]]!! Time in office |- |1. |[[José Luis Roberto]]|| 2002–2015 |- |2. |[[Rafael Ripoll]]|| 2015–2020 |- |3. |[[José Luis Roberto]]|| 2020–present |- |} Its leader is [[José Luis Roberto]], [[lawyer]] and general secretary of the ''[[Asociación Nacional de Empresarios de Locales de Alterne]]''. Roberto, who is also a self-described "entrepreneur", owns the security firm ''Levantina de Seguridad'' (the "de facto" security syndicate in Valencia) and the law firm [[Roberto & Salazar]], having representation in [[Madrid]], [[Barcelona]], and [[Valencia, Spain|Valencia]] with at least 30 lawyers in office. He also owns several [[gyms]] in [[Valencia (province)|Valencia]], [[Andalusia]] and [[Catalonia]], among them Valencian ''Gym Levantina'', which was investigated for some time in relation to illegal [[vale tudo|valetudo]] "championships", and [[military surplus]] stores held responsible for furnishing a large part of the [[Madrid local police]] uniforms. One of Roberto's gyms, ''Chute Boxe'', held valetudo courses for the police, funded with public money and organized by the ''Sindicato Independiente de Policía''.[http://www.elplural.com/politica/detail.php?id=2182 Fascist group connected with the Valencian administration] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080627180554/http://www.elplural.com/politica/detail.php?id=2182 |date=27 June 2008 }} José Luis Roberto was arrested, although never formally indicted, during the [[Spanish Transition]], in connection to two terrorist actions against [[Catalan independentism|independentist]] meetings held in Valencia. He has been consistently accused, albeit without proofs, of founding and partially funding marginal far-right groups such as [[Acción Radical]] ''Frente Antisistema''. Permanently based in Valencia, he has published articles in local newspapers such as ''[[Las Provincias]]'', ''[[Diario de Valencia]]'' and ''[[Levante-EMV|Levante]]'', among them the infamous ''Yo también tengo libros nazis'' ("I also own [[Nazi]] books"). He has been constantly sued and criticized by anti-racist and anti-fascist organizations for engaging in [[hate speech]] and allowing open signs of bigotry from members and supporters in his party's meetings.{{cite web |url=http://www.levante-emv.com/secciones/noticia.jsp?pNumEjemplar=3375&pIdSeccion=19&pIdNoticia=256364&rand=1166244227709 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724162358/http://www.levante-emv.com/secciones/noticia.jsp?pNumEjemplar=3375&pIdSeccion=19&pIdNoticia=256364&rand=1166244227709 |url-status=dead |archive-date=2011-07-24 |title=Juzgan al líder de Espan´a 2000 por los incidentes y las consignas racistas durante una manifestación |publisher=levante-emv.com |access-date=2011-09-16 }}[http://www.abc.es/hemeroteca/historico-30-01-2007/abc/Valencia/el-juez-absuelve-al-lider-de-españa-2000-por-los-incidentes-en-una-protesta-contra-inmigrantes_1631190904295.html] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131116032713/http://www.abc.es/hemeroteca/historico-30-01-2007/abc/Valencia/el-juez-absuelve-al-lider-de-espa|date=16 November 2013}}{{cite web |url=http://esp2000.org/forum/forum_entry.php?id=117079&page=0&category=all&order=time |title=Biography: José Luis Roberto (according to España 2000) |publisher=Esp2000.org |access-date=2011-09-16 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110815200301/http://esp2000.org/forum/forum_entry.php?id=117079&page=0&category=all&order=time |archive-date=15 August 2011 }} === Prominent members === [[Ernesto Milá]] was recently appointed as the party's press secretary, and appeared in the election list for the [[2008 Spanish general election|2008 General Election]],{{cite web |url=http://www.elpais.com/especial/elecciones-generales/candidatos/Ernesto-Mila-Rodriguez/202/08 |title=Ernesto Mila Rodriguez en las Elecciones Generales 2008 en ELPAÍS.com |work=El País |access-date=2011-09-16 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121009004241/http://www.elpais.com/especial/elecciones-generales/candidatos/Ernesto-Mila-Rodriguez/202/08 |archive-date=9 October 2012 }}{{cite web|author=Publicado por Admin |url=https://e-2000benidorm.blogspot.com/2007/12/ernesto-mila-responsable-de-prensa-de.html |title=España 2000 Benidorm - Crónicas y Fotos: Ernesto mila responsable de prensa de españa 2000 |date=18 December 2007 |publisher=E-2000benidorm.blogspot.com |access-date=2011-09-16}}{{cite web|url=http://lafalange.mforos.com/163004/6137409-ernesto-mila-en-espana-2000/ |access-date=19 October 2008 }}{{dead link|date=June 2016|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}{{cite web|author=espanolesenaccion |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bJ-Dj58DgFE |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211222/bJ-Dj58DgFE |archive-date=2021-12-22 |url-status=live|title=Anti-immigration meeting in a multi-cultural district in Valencia |publisher=Youtube.com |date=2 March 2008 |access-date=2011-09-16}}{{cbignore}} ostensibly an attempt to gain preponderance over other neo-fascist groups in Spain by capitalizing on Milá's extensive history. Known for his past membership in the [[Partido Español Nacional Socialista]] (PENS), [[Fuerza Nueva]] and, later on, its splinter group, the ''Frente de Juventudes'', Milá was one of the members of the fringe right-wing extremist groups (usually named ''incontrolados'', "uncontrolled elements") who rallied against leftist or pro-democratic meetings during late [[Francoist Spain|Francoism]], usually assuming the role of unofficial mob breakers and violent counter-rioters. Although the PENS terrorist attacks on [[Catalan language|Catalan]] libraries (such as the ''Cinc d'Oros'', 1971) and libraries and public centers in [[València]] (1975), among others, were usually not prosecuted, a thwarted attempt on the [[Union of the Democratic Centre (Spain)|Union of the Democratic Centre]] headquarters, however, resulted in a formal indictment and forced Milá to flee for [[France]]. After a stay in [[Bolivia]], where he worked as an adviser for the short-lived [[Luis García Meza Tejada]] dictatorship along with infamous neo-fascists such as [[Stefano delle Chiaie]] and war criminal [[Klaus Barbie]],{{cite web|url=https://manuelcarballal.blogspot.com/2007/01/nazis-en-espaa-la-herencia-del-iii.html |title=Nazis in Spain |date=26 January 2007 |publisher=Manuelcarballal.blogspot.com |access-date=2011-09-16}}{{Cite book| last= Vázquez Montalbán| first=Manuel | authorlink=Manuel Vázquez Montalbán | title= Mis almuerzos con gente inquietante | year= 1984 | others= (see the whole chapter dedicated to Ernesto Milá) | publisher = Planeta | isbn= 978-84-9793-459-6 }} he returned to Spain.{{cite web |url=http://colombia.indymedia.org/news/2007/04/62502.php |title=Ernesto Milá y el CNI, desembarco en España 2000 : Indymedia Colombia |publisher=Colombia.indymedia.org |access-date=2011-09-16 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927163629/http://colombia.indymedia.org/news/2007/04/62502.php |archive-date=27 September 2011 }} Widely considered the most intellectually ambitious and well-connected of all Spanish neo-fascists who have escaped successful legal prosecution, Milá is nowadays devoted to "cultural" dissertations about diverse topics held dear by the far right in his [[blog]].{{cite web|url=http://infokrisis.blogia.com/ |title=INFOKRISIS, el blog de Ernest Milà |publisher=Infokrisis.blogia.com |date=1 July 2011 |access-date=2011-09-16}} == Public profile == The group organized demonstrations in districts of Valencia such as [[Ruzafa]] or ''Velluters'', places with a considerable immigrant population.[http://www.abc.es/hemeroteca/historico-05-03-2002/abc/Valencia/la-contramanifestacion-de-ruzafa-era-ilegal-segun-la-delegada-del-gobierno_82611.html La contra manifestación de Ruzafa era ilegal] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305015559/http://www.abc.es/hemeroteca/historico-05-03-2002/abc/Valencia/la-contramanifestacion-de-ruzafa-era-ilegal-segun-la-delegada-del-gobierno_82611.html |date=5 March 2016 }}, en el diario ''ABC''. As said above, José Luis Roberto was accused by ''[[SOS Racisme|SOS Racismo]]'' and others of racism and [[hate speech]], although the judge ruled out said accusations under the justification that the slurs and racist expressions uttered during the gatherings were "mere generic disqualifications".[http://www.abc.es/hemeroteca/historico-30-01-2007/abc/Valencia/el-juez-absuelve-al-lider-de-españa-2000-por-los-incidentes-en-una-protesta-contra-inmigrantes_1631190904295.html Judge rules out accusations over racist incidents] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131116032713/http://www.abc.es/hemeroteca/historico-30-01-2007/abc/Valencia/el-juez-absuelve-al-lider-de-espa |date=16 November 2013 }}, in the ''ABC'' newspaper. He has also organized [[soccer]] matches under the slogan ''Los españoles primero'' ("[[Spaniards]] first"), alleging that [[Latino (demonym)|Latino]] bands were "de facto" owners of the soccer courts and held a veto over the admission of other players: not only that was never confirmed but also it appears that the districts where said games were played have a reputation for being prone to rather low levels of conflict.[http://www.elplural.com/macrovida/detail.php?id=2812 Un partido fascista valenciano organiza un partido de fútbol xenófobo] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080627215627/http://www.elplural.com/macrovida/detail.php?id=2812 |date=27 June 2008 }}, en diario ''[[El Plural]]''. They also run a [[homeless shelter]] and a cafeteria.{{cite web|url=http://www.theworld.org/2012/11/radical-right-spain/ |title=Radical Right in Spain Still Mostly Underground |access-date=2013-06-06 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121203043711/http://www.theworld.org/2012/11/radical-right-spain/ |archive-date=3 December 2012 }} Every 12 October, the group's supporters gather to demonstrate in [[Valencia, Spain|Valencia]], starting in front of the statue of [[El Cid]] and finishing in front of the statue of [[James I the Conqueror]]. Slogans uttered by demonstrators at these meetings include "''Moros no, España no es un zoo''" (No to Moors, Spain is not a zoo) and "''España es una y no cincuenta y una''" (Spain is one, and not fifty one).[http://www.elpais.com/articulo/Comunidad/Valenciana/TSJ/confirma/prohibicion/manifestacion/Espana/2000/elpepuespval/20051001elpval_15/Tes El TSJ confirma la prohibición de una manifestación de España 2000] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110726034859/http://www.esp2000.org/php/prensa_ficha.php?ref=791 |date=26 July 2011 }} en ''[[Las Provincias]]''.[http://www.esp2000.org/php/prensa_ficha.php?ref=798 2011}}, en el periódico ''[[Levante EMV]]''.[http://www.20minutos.es/noticia/161805/0/Marcha/ultraderecha/Batasuna/ Marcha de ultraderecha] {{webarchive ''[[20minutos]]''[http://www.elpais.com/articulo/Comunidad/Valenciana/Cerca/700/ultras/manifiestan/inmigrantes/Valencia/elpepuespval/20061013elpval_2/Tes Cerca de 700 ultras se manifestaron contra la inmigración en Valencia] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111221035928/http://www.elpais.com/articulo/Comunidad/Valenciana/Cerca/700/ultras/manifiestan/inmigrantes/Valencia/elpepuespval/20061013elpval_2/Tes |date=21 Provincias''. During the 2007 march, attendance exceeded 1,000 people[http://www.cadenaser.com/espana/articulo/manifestaciones-malestar-social-cerca-millar/csrcsrpor/20071012csrcsrnac_7/Tes Cerca un millar manifestantes queman fotos de Ibarretxe, Carod-Rovira y ETA en Valencia] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111123154748/http://www.cadenaser.com/espana/articulo/manifestaciones-malestar-social-cerca-millar/csrcsrpor/20071012csrcsrnac_7/Tes |date=23 November 2011 }} en la [[Cadena Ser]][http://www.20minutos.es/noticia/289817/0/quema/carod/ibarretxe/ Cerca de mil personas queman en Valencia fotos de Ibarretxe, Carod-Rovira y ETA] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304062225/http://www.20minutos.es/noticia/289817/0/quema/carod/ibarretxe/ |date=4 March 2016 }} en [[20 Minutos]] (2,000 according to police sources).[http://www.panorama-actual.es/noticias/not238540.htm Miles de manifestantes de España 2000 queman fotos de Ibarretxe y Carod Rovira] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090608053203/http://www.panorama-actual.es/noticias/not238540.htm |date=8 June 2009 }} Panorama Actual.[http://www.elperiodico.com/default.asp?idpublicacio_PK=46&idioma=CAS&idnoticia_PK=449542&idseccio_PK=1008 Miles de Personas se manifiestan en Valencia Bajo el lema "Los españoles primero"] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071013051156/http://www.elperiodico.com/default.asp?idpublicacio_PK=46&idioma=CAS&idnoticia_PK=449542&idseccio_PK=1008 |date=13 October 2007 }} En el Periódico.[http://arquivo.pt/wayback/20090719094438/http://www.levante-emv.com/secciones/noticia.jsp?pRef=3685_19_355964__Comunitat-Valenciana-Manifestantes-convocados-2000-Valencia-queman-fotos-Ibarretxe-Carod-Rovira Manifestantes de extrema derecha queman fotos de Ibarretxe y Carod Rovira] Levante EMV. At the 2007 event, protestors burned pictures of the chairman of [[Esquerra Republicana de Catalunya]], [[Josep-Lluís Carod-Rovira]], and of the [[Basque Country (autonomous community)|Basque]] [[lehendakari]], [[Juan José Ibarretxe]], chanted ''En España los españoles primero: contra la inmigración ilegal y en defensa de nuestros derechos'' (In Spain the Spaniards come first: against illegal immigration and in defence of our rights), and displayed [[ETA (separatist group)|ETA]] emblems such as ''Menos pateras, que cierren las fronteras''.[http://www.elpais.com/articulo/espana/Cerca/millar/manifestantes/queman/fotos/Ibarretxe/Carod-Rovira/ETA/elpepuesp/20071012elpepunac_7/Tes Cerca un millar manifestantes queman fotos de Ibarretxe, Carod-Rovira y ETA] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111221035952/http://www.elpais.com/articulo/espana/Cerca/millar/manifestantes/queman/fotos/Ibarretxe/Carod-Rovira/ETA/elpepuesp/20071012elpepunac_7/Tes |date=21 December 2011 }} en el diario [[El País (España)|El País]] == Election results == === [[Cortes Generales]] === {|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:100%; line-height:16px;" |- ! rowspan="2" width="50px"| Election ! rowspan="2" | Leader ! colspan="5" | [[Congress of Deputies (Spain)|Congress of Deputies]] ! colspan="2"| [[Senate (Spain)|Senate]] ! rowspan="2" width="30px"| Rank ! rowspan="2" width="125px"| Government |- ! width="75px"| Votes ! width="45px"| % ! width="45px"| ±[[Percentage point|pp]] ! width="100px"| Seats ! width="40px"| +/− ! width="100px"| Seats ! width="40px"| +/− |- !
